=^075 Items ^*=

^077New Item: Blood Ruby^*
^947-^* Cost: 550 Gold
^947-^* Passively grants +150 Max Health.
   ^274*^* Found in the Relics shop.

^947+^* Blood Ruby acts as a buildup item for other items in the game that have Max Health bonuses.


^077New Item: Ether Jewel^*
^947-^* Cost: 450 Gold
^947-^* Passively grants +120 Max Mana.
   ^274*^* Found in the Relics shop.

^947+^* Ether Jewel acts as a buildup item for other items in the game that have Max Mana bonuses.


^077New Item: Nihil Crystal^*
^947-^* Cost: 200 Gold
^947-^* Passive effect: grants 0.5 Mana Regeneration per second.
   ^274*^* This effect does not stack with itself or Adeve's Cloak.
^947-^* While off cooldown: upon taking an instance of an attempted 60+ non-DoT Spell Damage, regenerates 40 Health and 20 Mana over 10 seconds and places the item on cooldown.
^947-^* Cooldown: 30 seconds
   ^274*^* Found in the Relics shop.

^947+^* Nihil Crystal is another option for Health and Mana sustain against Spell Damage while in lane. It will be a semi-reliable source of sustain for heroes in the suicide lane.



^077New Item: Adeve's Cloak^*
^947-^* Components: Blood Ruby (550 Gold) + Ether Jewel (450 Gold) + Nihil Crystal (200 Gold) + Recipe (1100 Gold) = 2300 Gold Total
   ^274*^* Can be upgraded 1 time, for a max item level of 2.
   ^274*^* Total cost is therefore 2300/3400 Gold.
^947-^* Passive stat bonuses: +180/270 Max Health, +140/180 Max Mana, +0.65/0.8 Mana Regeneration
   ^274*^* The passsive Mana Regeneration bonus does not stack with itself or Nihil Crystal.
^947-^* Passive effect: while off cooldown, taking damage from player-controlled enemy sources starts a 3 second timer on the item and starts accumulating damage. The timer is refreshed each time you take damage from player-controlled enemy sources.
^947-^* If the accumulated post-mitigation damage exceeds 20% of your Max Health or 300 (whichever is higher) while the timer is active, the item is placed on cooldown and Adeve's Protection is applied for 3 seconds.
^947-^* Adeve's Protection applies:
   ^274*^* 15/25 Health Regeneration per second
   ^274*^* 10/20 Mana Regeneration per second
   ^274*^* Increase your Debuff and Stun Reduction by 15/40%.
   ^274*^* Reduces incoming Damage by 15/40%.
   ^274*^* Reduces your Damage Output by 85/60%.
^947-^* Cooldown: 80 seconds
   ^274*^* Found under the Protective category in the shop.

^947+^* Burst damage has been an incredibly potent highlight of HoN's lifetime, to the point where not much can be done against it (i.e. the burst damage cannot be humanly reacted to).
^947+^* This item serves to mitigate the damage incurred from a surprise blink-initiation without needing to react to it so that your character has a more likely chance to respond if they are caught off guard.
^947+^* It is important to note that this item is not meant to be a hard counter against opponents with amazing initiation (it is still very possible the wielder can still die even with these effects) - it is just another tool for heroes who benefit greatly from using their spells in a teamfight to have a chance to use those spells instead of simply dying from burst damage.
^947+^* This item provides no offensive bonuses and costs a decent amount of Gold for its benefits. It is intended to be a niche item for certain heroes in specific game scenarios, and is generally disincentivized for certain core heroes and carry heroes.



^077New Item: Faux Bow^*
^947-^* Components: Alacrity Band (1200) + Soulscream Ring (440) + Fleetfeet (450) + Recipe (910) = 3000 Gold Total
^947-^* Grants: 30 Attack Speed, 8 Strength, 14 Agility, 8 Intelligence
   ^274*^* Found under the Combative category in the shop.

   ^274*^* Range: 700
   ^274*^* Cooldown: 20 seconds
^947-^* Active: Target an enemy hero within cast range to gain 70 bonus Attack Speed, infinite attack range and a 50% chance to gain True Strike against the target for up to 6 seconds or 4 attacks. Only grants infinite attack range on ranged heroes.
   ^274*^* Note that this behaviour will not allow your hero to attack another target that is currently outside of your regular attack range due to existing coding limitations. Your hero will not attempt to attack if you try to do this.

^947+^* Faux Bow allows heroes to have a method of finishing off fleeing targets that escape their Attack Range, while offering a mid-cost pickup option for ranged heroes that grants a decent amount of survivability.

_______________________________________________

Items with Buildup Changes

^077Arcane Bomb^*
^947-^* New component buildup: Blood Ruby (550) + Ether Jewel (450) + Recipe (900) = 1900 Gold Total
   ^274*^* Total cost was increased from 1650 Gold.
^947-^* No longer grants 4 Strength, 4 Agility and 14 Intelligence.
^947-^* Now passively grants 150 Max Health and 180 Mana.

^947+^* Arcane Bomb now gives about twice as much Max Health compared to before, while being slightly easier to build up and being able to take advantage of some changes earlier.
^947+^* The item is quite prevalent in the meta, so the total cost has been increased to account for all factors.
^947+^* These changes are both a buff and a nerf.

^077Brutalizer^*
^947-^* New component buildup: Slayer (2200) + Mighty Blade (1000) + Recipe (200) = 3400 Gold Total
   ^274*^* Total cost was reduced from 3450 Gold.
^947-^* Passive Strength bonus increased from 8 to 12.
^947-^* Physical Damage on proc increased from 35 to 70.
^947-^* Stun duration on proc increased from 1.4 seconds to 1.5 seconds.

^947+^* Brutalizer had little reason to be bought, so some proper adjustments were made to make it a more commonly viable option for heroes to use.

^077Codex^*
^947-^* New component buildup: Neophyte's Book (1000) + Major Totem (540) + Recipe (1000) = 2540 Gold Total
^947-^* No longer grants bonus Attack Damage.
^947-^* Now grants 6/7/8/9/10 Strength, 4/5/6/7/8 Agility and 16/18/20/22/24 Intelligence.
^947-^* Magic Damage dealt by the active part of the item changed from 400/500/600/700/800 to 350/450/600/750/900.
^947-^* Cast Range changed from 600/700/800/900/1000 to 600/675/750/825/900.
^947-^* Cooldown changed from 40/36/32/28/24 seconds to 30/28/26/24/22 seconds.
^947-^* Mana Cost changed from 180/160/140/120/100 to 120/135/150/165/180.
^947-^* Visual effects for the lightning now change with level!
   ^274*^* The colour order is white/green/blue/purple/red for levels 1/2/3/4/5 of the item.
   ^274*^* Credits to Whyzozerious for the visual effect improvements!

^947+^* Codex was always known as a non-viable item because of its lack of relevant bonuses while building it, as well as its high cost for what it grants.
^947+^* Making the item more accessible early on at a reduced price cost while increasing its rewards as you level it should make this a more viable item pickup in actual games.

^077Puzzlebox^*
^947-^* New component buildup: Blood Ruby (550) + Ether Jewel (450) + Ether Jewel (450) + Recipe (1050) = 2500 Gold Total
^947-^* Total cost per level of Puzzlebox changed from 2975/4000/5025 Gold to 2500/3550/4600 Gold.
^947-^* No longer grants 11/13/15 Strength and 18/22/26 Intelligence.
^947-^* Now grants 200/250/300 Max Health, 250/300/350 Max Mana, and 4/7/10 Attack Damage.

^947+^* In today's meta, Puzzlebox takes a little too long to build up. These buildup changes should buff the item so that it sees play more often.

   ^274*^* This reduces the overall Searing Damage by ~25%.

^077Frozen Light^*

^947-^* Max charges for Searing reduced from 3 to 2.
   ^274*^* This reduces the overall Searing Damage by ~25%.

^077Searing Light^*
^947-^* Searing Damage-over-Time (DoT) effect increased from 12 to 13 Magic Damage per second per charge.
^947-^* Max charges for Searing reduced from 3 to 2.
   ^274*^* This reduces the overall Searing Damage by ~25%.

^077Dawnbringer^*
^947-^* Searing Damage-over-Time (DoT) effect reduced from 18 to 15 Magic Damage per second per charge.
^947-^* Max charges for Searing reduced from 3 to 2.
   ^274*^* This reduces the overall Searing Damage by ~45%.
^947-^* Movement Speed Slow reduced from 40%/20% to 30%/15% if its wielder is Melee/Ranged, respectively.

^947+^* Dawnbringer was just a tad too effective despite its high cost. Its passive on-attack effects were toned down closer to the level of the Tier 2 brands.
^947+^* Lightbrand items in general were too efficient for what they are doing, so their Searing damage numbers were readjusted.

_______________________________________________

^077Alchemist's Bones^*
^947-^* Gold Bounty from killing a creep with its active effect increased from 165 Gold to 190 Gold.
^947-^* Experience Bounty multiplier from killing a creep with its active effect increased from 1.75x to 2x.

^077Beastheart^*
^947-^* Max Health bonus increased from 215 to 230.

^077Doom Bringer^*
^947-^* Base Attack Damage bonus increased from 250 to 300.
^947-^* Max charges reduced from 25 to 20.

^947+^* This makes the item more of a viable pickup for comebacks, if they are attempted in specific scenarios.

^077Dreamcatcher^*
^947-^* While not at max charges, passively gains 1 charge every 3 minutes.

^077Ghost Marchers^*
^947-^* Ranged heroes now only receive a 15% Movement Speed bonus from the active state (instead of 20%).

^077Golden Apple^*
^947-^* No longer passively grants Mana Regeneration.
^947-^* Instead, now passively grants +2 Attack Damage.

^077Jade Spire^*
^947-^* Bonus Cast Range reduced from 250 to 150.
^947-^* Recipe Cost reduced from 600 Gold to 300 Gold.
   ^274*^* Total Cost reduced from 2400 Gold to 2100 Gold.
^947-^* No longer has item drop or pickup restrictions (though its cast range bonuses still do not stack).
^947-^* No longer has a different version of the item in Mid Wars.

^947+^* Cast Ranges are integral to relative hero balance, and modifying this value too much makes some heroes more polarizing than others if they obtain this item.
^947+^* The Cast Range bonus is still large enough to make a difference, but it will be nowhere nearly as frustrating to deal with against certain heroes, while still providing a reasonably potent cast range increase to make a difference.

^077Kuldra's Sheepstick^*
^947-^* Cast Range reduced from 650 to 600.

^077Lunar Tear^*
^947-^* Passive Health Regeneration bonus increased from 0.4 to 0.5.
^947-^* Cost reduced from 80 Gold to 75 Gold.

^077Runed Cleaver^*
^947-^* Cleave Damage increased from 50% to 60%.

^077Sustainer^*
^947-^* Now passively grants 5 Attack Damage.

^077Toxin Claws^*
^947-^* No longer refunds any Gold when sold.

==^960 Bug Fixes & Optimizations ^*==



=^075 General ^*=

^947-^* Hero Mastery in the Learnatorium is now sorted properly.
^947-^* Mid Wars: fixed a cliffwalking spot near the Hellbourne teleporter cliff.
^947-^* Camera shake effects have been drastically reduced in magnitude.



=^075 Heroes ^*=

Certain abilities that are classified as "non-interrupting" (i.e. did not interrupt your current order) will now properly and intentionally interrupt the current caster's channeling state (if they are channeling).
   ^274*^* This generally does not change anything for most scenarios. However, there are some abilities that are clearly not meant to be used while channeling. Some notable examples include (but are not limited to):
   ^274*^* Artillery's Mortar Shot
   ^274*^* Cthulhuphant's Obliterate
   ^274*^* Prisoner's Prison Break
   ^274*^* Goldenveil's Dagger Dance
   ^274*^* Swiftblade's Counter Attack
   ^274*^* Lodestone's Shatterstorm

^059Chipper^*
^947-^* Rocket Barrage now has a touch effect type of Magic (i.e. will pass through units that have Magic Immunity).

^059Gemini^*
^947-^* Heropets (Fire, Ice and Light) are now properly deniable only if their Current Health percentage is less than 10% of their Max Health.
^947-^* Doom Bringer is now dropped properly when Gemini is in split form if damaged by player-controlled enemy units (i.e. this mechanic is propagated from Doom Bringer onto the split form units).

^059Lodestone^*
^947-^* Lodestone Plates now triggers properly under the intended conditions.

^059Nymphora^*
^947-^* Grace Nymphora's Teleport count (for teleporting ally heroes) is now correct.

^059Parallax^*
^947-^* Earthshatter (when boosted with Staff of the Master) now deals the correct amount of damage if Parallax's max mana is greater than the target's.

^059Qi^*
^947-^* Damage from Enlightenment now uses the correct damage multiplier when the damage source is from Ancestral Assault.

^059Tremble^*
^947-^* Terror Mound unit size has been increased slightly so it can be clicked more easily.
   ^274*^* This does not affect the size of the Terror Mound visual.



=^075 Items ^*=

^077Bound Eye^*
^947-^* Now properly drops on death in all circumstances.

^077Dreamcatcher and Soultrap^*
^947-^* Health Regeneration effect no longer gets dispelled by neutral creeps (and is now consistent with the behaviour of Health Potion, Mana Potion, etc.).

^059Qi^*

^256Enlightenment^*
^947-^* Damage-over-Time effects from Qi's abilities now apply the corrected amount of 0.75x the bonus damage per game frame (instead of 1x the bonus damage).
^947-^* "Extra" Attack Speed considered by the damage calculation is now capped at 250.
   ^274*^* These changes prevent Qi from doing excessive amounts of damage to heroes who get a large burst of Attack Speed in short durations (e.g. Silhouette, Scout).
   ^274*^* Note that Qi will still do a lot of damage to these heroes, but they will not be unreasonably large damage values.

=^075 Heroes ^*=

^059Cthulhuphant^*

^947-^* Trample now generally spawns less afterimage visuals of Cthulhuphant.
^947-^* All Cthulhuphant avatars now have visual effects for Maddening Revenge.
^947-^* Daemon Highlord Cthulhuphant now uses the proper voice lines for Dreams of Madness, Obliterate, and his taunt.
^947-^* Enemies now gain Sight of Cthulhuphant if they are forced to attack Cthulhuphant during Dreams of Madness.
   ^274*^* Credits to Gimj for submitting some of these changes!

^059Corrupted Disciple^*

^059Parasite^*

^059Warchief^*

=^075 Illusion Visuals ^*=

Illusions now have a new visual effect!
^947-^* Instead of simply applying a green texture over the original model, it now uses a light-blue translucent material over the model.
   ^274*^* This visual effect for illusions will only apply for the owner of the illusions and their allies.
   ^274*^* In other words, enemy illusions will not appear to have this visual effect on them.



=^075 Forests of Caldavar ^*=

^947-^* Ally player-controlled non-hero units near the Level 3 Towers will have unitwalking for the first 2 minutes.
^947-^* All NPC Neutral creeps now have Push Immunity for the first 2 minutes.

^947+^* These changes will address specific unintentional behaviours from being leveraged too early on in the laning phase of the game for 1 team to get a high reward without reasonable counterplay (or without an appropriate level of risk).
^947+^* These unintentional behaviours can still be used after the first 2 minutes. By that time, all players have more appropriate tools and levels available to them to have reasonable counterplay.
^947+^* These changes have no impact on regular gameplay.



=^075 Neutral Creeps ^*=

^059Catman Champion^*

^059Minotaur^*
^947-^* Attack Speed bonus to allies from the aura reduced from 20 to 15.

^059Vagabond Leader^*
^947-^* Bonus Health Regeneration per second granted to allies from the aura reduced from 5 to 4.
^947-^* Magic Damage dealt by Ground Pound reduced from 170 to 150.

^059Vulture Lord^*

=^075 Mid Wars ^*=

Banning Draft
^947-^* Ban phase time increased from 15 seconds to 30 seconds per round.

^947-^* Time period after all heroes have been picked (but before the game starts) increased from 20 seconds to 30 seconds.



=^075 Heroes ^*=



^059Adrenaline^*

^256Shard Blast^*
^947-^* Mana Cost changed from 55/70/85/100 to 50/70/90/110.
^947-^* Base Magic Damage reduced from 80/120/160/200 to 80/115/150/185.
^947-^* Cooldown reduced from 4 seconds to 3 seconds.
^947-^* Cooldown reduction for Rush decreased from 1.5 seconds to 1 second per enemy hero hit.
^947-^* When the Shard hits 2 or more enemy heroes, the mechanics have changed.

^947-^* Old mechanic:
   ^274*^* Cooldown is reduced to 0.5 seconds and 60% of the Mana Cost is refunded.
^947-^* New mechanic:
   ^274*^* Cooldown is now reduced by 0.75 seconds, and Mana Cost on the ability is reduced by 10/15/20/25.
   ^274*^* This effect stacks up to 2 times and lasts for 10 seconds.

^256Ember Shard^*
^947-^* Max pull distance reduced from 1000 units to 800 units.
^947-^* Regular distance at which the enemy unit stops from Adrenaline (when travelling to him) increased from 100 units to 150 units.

^256Death's Halo^*
^947-^* Bonus Strength per real hero caught reduced from 4/6/8 to 3/4/5.
   ^274*^* Still 2x the value for enemy heroes.
^947-^* No longer gains 2 bonus Attack Damage per real hero trapped.

^947+^* Adrenaline's Shard Blast was a little too explosive and lopsided when encountering multiple enemy heroes, and proved to have too much downtime in teamfights when not hitting 2+ enemy heroes.
^947+^* Shard Blast is still intended to perform extremely well when fighting multiple heroes, so its bonus effects will be more fair, sustained and consistent after these changes.

_______________________________________________

^059Artillery^*

^256LRM^*
^947-^* Percentage of Attack Damage bonus on each LRM rocket changed from 7/14/21/28% to 7/12/17/22%.
^947-^* Artillery is now Sighted for 1 second if LRM hits an enemy unit (instead of just player-controlled enemy units).
   ^274*^* If you hit a neutral creep, the neutral creep's team will have sight of Artillery (not the opposing Legion/Hellbourne team).
^947-^* Base Cast Range reduced from 1200 to 1100.
^947-^* Now uses the correct range increase value from Special Ammunition.

^256Mortar Shot^*
^947-^* Base Cast Range reduced from 1200 to 1100.
^947-^* Now uses the correct range increase value from Special Ammunition.

^256Homing Missile^*
^947-^* Base Cast Range reduced from 1200 to 1100.
^947-^* Now uses the correct range increase value from Special Ammunition.

^947+^* Prior to this patch, these 3 abilities were still using the values prior to 4.9.0, despite the fact that they were intended to be reduced.
^947+^* Despite this, Artillery's performance has been significantly higher than intended, so another small nerf was added on top of the intended cast range reduction. This should make his range less polarizing to deal with.

_______________________________________________

^059Blood Hunter^*

^256Hemorrhage^*
^947-^* Initial Superior Magic Damage increased from 200/300/400 to 250/375/500.
^947-^* Superior Magic Damage taken per unit travelled increased from 35/55/75% to 40/60/80%.
   ^274*^* Prior to this patch, the values in the tooltip were displayed as 30/45/60% - this change both corrects the tooltips and buffs the ability slightly.

_______________________________________________

^059Calamity^*

^256Funeral Pyre^*
^947-^* Agility gain state on self duration increased from 1.25x to 1.5x the original state duration.

_______________________________________________

^059Circe^*

^256Doppelganger^*
^947-^* Mana Cost changed from 100/85/70/55 to 60/70/80/90.

_______________________________________________

^059Cthulhuphant^*

^256Obliterate^*
^947-^* Using this ability will no longer interrupt your order queue.

^256Maddening Revenge^*
^947-^* Attackers now also have -30/40/50/60% Healing Received for 0.1 seconds upon attacking Cthulhuphant.
^947-^* Receiving damage from Cthulhuphant's front will grant Cthulhuphant 5/10/15/20 Health Regeneration per second for 2 seconds.
^947-^* The Health Regeneration effect and Return Damage now only have 0.33x effectiveness on Cthulhuphant's illusions.

^947+^* Although Cthulhuphant is in a generally good state, Maddening Revenge does not have really have much going for the ability at the moment.
^947+^* Granting Cthulhuphant slightly more durability and preventing regular lifesteal from being a hard counter to this ability will allow this ability to be more worth leveling during the game.

_______________________________________________

^059Deadlift^*

^256Onslaught^*
^947-^* Cast effect type changed from Magic to Physical.

_______________________________________________

^059Devourer^*
^947-^* Starting Armor increased from 1.16 to 2.

_______________________________________________

^059Draconis^*

^256Fiery Barrage^*
^947-^* Dragonflame now only spreads the flames once from Ablaze (instead of spreading it 4 times).
^947-^* Base Magic Damage per 4 seconds increased from 5/10/15/20 to 20.
^947-^* Damage rate increase per charge reduced from 100% to 70%.
^947-^* Max charges reduced from 8 to 5/6/7/8.
^947-^* Damage multiplier against heroes increased from 0.5/0.8/1.1/1.4x to 0.8/1/1.2/1.4x.

^947+^* Draconis has the ability to take camps using the unique mechanics of Fiery Barrage (where you damage different enemies to maximize damage by spreading the fire) without taking damage. However, it was too easy to quickly reach the max potential damage of Fiery Barrage against stacked neutral camps by using Dragonflame.

^947+^* These changes should slow down Draconis' damage rate against large stacks of neutral creeps with a lower level of Fiery Barrage for his level 1-4 jungling from autoattacks, while simultaneously ensuring that 1 Dragonflame cast does not nearly wipe out an entire camp. Overall, jungling should be better for the average player early on, while the damage output against stacks should be slower when Draconis is level 3-6.

_______________________________________________

^059Drunken Master^*

^256Stagger^*
^947-^* Using Lunge now resets the cooldown of Stagger (instead of reducing its cooldown to 0.8 seconds).

^256Drink^*
^947-^* Now instantly grants 3/6/9/12 Drink charges upon starting the channel.

^2563 Point Strike^*
^947-^* Strike point reset delay increased from 8 seconds to 12 seconds.

^947+^* Just a few more quality-of-life changes to Drunken Master to improve his combat flow in accordance with his Drink usage.

_______________________________________________

^059Empath^*

^256Essence Link^*
^947-^* Tether break range increased from 100 to 200.
^947-^* State on Empath (or the target ally hero, if used while As One is active) is no longer considered a buff (and thus can no longer be dispelled).
   ^274*^* This ensures that Essence Link does not get dispelled if As One is used after Essence Link is cast.
   ^274*^* Note that the debuff on the enemy can still be dispelled, which will cancel Essence Link on Empath (or her host from As One).

_______________________________________________

^059Engineer^*

^256The Keg^*
^947-^* Magic Damage increased from 90/150/210/270 to 90/160/230/300.
^947-^* Stun duration increased from 1.6/1.8/2/2.2 seconds to 1.6/1.9/2.2/2.5 seconds.

_______________________________________________

^059Fayde^*

^256Shadow Walk^*
^947-^* Now properly acts as a disjoint upon activation.

_______________________________________________

^059Flint Beastwood^*

^256Hollowpoint Shells^*
^947-^* Bonus Physical Damage reduced from 20/40/60/80 to 16/34/52/70.
^947-^* Stun duration reduced from 0.05/0.1/0.15/0.2 seconds to 0/0.05/0.1/0.15 seconds.
^947-^* Now only procs against enemy units (i.e. no longer procs against wards or ally units).

^256Money Shot^*
^947-^* Cooldown changed from 20/15/10 seconds to 20 seconds.

^059Goldenveil^*

^256Dagger Dance^*
^947-^* Now deals 3.5/5/6.5/8 Magic Damage every 0.25 seconds to targets other than the main target within the area of effect.

^256Perch & Plunge^*
^947-^* While Perching on a tree, cooldown is set to 6 seconds if the Perched tree is destroyed.

_______________________________________________

^059Ichor^*

^256Life Leech^*
^947-^* Life Leech's state application now occurs before the Magic Damage takes place.
   ^274*^* This allows the Magic Damage dealt by this ability to trigger from the Life Leech state, allowing Ichor to gain a small amount of healing when Life Leech's projectile hits an enemy.

_______________________________________________

^059Keeper of the Forest^*

^256Nature's Guidance^*
^947-^* Stealth state on Keeper of the Forest will not be dispelled if Nature's Guidance is cast while near trees.

_______________________________________________

^059Klanx^*

^256B.A.N.G.^*
^947-^* State duration for enemies facing the flash increased from 1.5 seconds to 2 seconds.

^256Lackey^*
^947-^* Shots now deal 0.33x damage in a 300 radius to enemies around (but excluding) the main target.

_______________________________________________

^059Kraken^*

^256Release the Kraken!^*
^947-^* Now constantly applies Restrain to enemies within radius.

_______________________________________________

^059Ophelia^*

^256Nature's Wrath^*
^947-^* Cast Range increased from 600 to 700.

^256Command^*
^947-^* Movement Speed bonus for creeps reduced from 10/20/30/40 to 15/20/25/30.
^947-^* Max Health bonus reduced from 40/120/160/200 to 0/60/90/120.
^947-^* Creeps controlled with Command now have a minimum Base Health of 600/700/800/900.

^947+^* Combined with the neutral creep changes in this patch, these changes should slightly reduce Ophelia's performance later in the game without destroying the hero's performance and unique tempo control during the early game.
^947+^* These changes also allow Ophelia to have some other targets for Command as the game progresses without sacrificing much durability on them.

_______________________________________________

^059Pandamonium^*

^256Flurry^*
^947-^* Now destroys trees within its affected area.

^256Flick^*
^947-^* Now deals 20 Physical Damage to the target upon landing near them.
^947-^* Delay for flicking (after landing near the target) reduced from 0.4 seconds to 0.35 seconds.
^947-^* Pandamonium is no longer stunned for a full 0.5 seconds before actually flicking the target backwards.
   ^274*^* Instead, the stun duration on Pandamonium for his Flick animation now matches the delay time for flicking (0.35 seconds).

^256Face Smash^*
^947-^* Pandamonium now faces the target after every attack.
   ^274*^* This ensures that Pandamonium faces the target consistently, if for some reason the target or Pandamonium are displaced without breaking the channel.

^947+^* Some simple quality-of-life changes were made to Pandamonium, making him feel more responsive for the player playing the hero.

^947+^* Pandamonium's Flurry now has more consistent behaviour while near a tree line, and he can act a little sooner out of the Flick animation.

^947+^* The small damage addition to Flick makes it a more reliable tool to put an enemy's Portal Key on cooldown and dispel an enemy's Health Potion, etc.

_______________________________________________

^059Parasite^*

^256Infest^*
^947-^* Experience and Gold granted to Parasite when the Infested Creep dies is now a static 125 Experience and 75 Gold (rather than the values based off of the Infested Creep).
   ^274*^* This will always happen regardless of how the Infested Creep dies.

   ^274*^* These values are the highest values among the strongest units in the Hard neutral camps.
^947-^* If an enemy player has damaged the creep within the last 10 seconds, the enemy team also will receive the static amount of Experience and Gold split evenly among each enemy player.

^947+^* These changes ensure that the behaviour of the death of the Infested Creep is consistent for both Parasite and his opponents, while also making a failed gank much less punishing for Parasite.
^947+^* The changes prevent Parasite from completely denying his opponents of Experience and Gold, while also preventing his opponents from denying Parasite Experience and Gold if they kill the creep.

_______________________________________________

^059Plague Rider^*
^947-^* Base Strength increased from 18 to 19.
^947-^* Base Intelligence increased from 18 to 20.

^256Extinguish^*
^947-^* Cooldown reduced from 60/50/40/30 seconds to 45/40/35/30 seconds.

^256Plague Carrier^*
^947-^* Cooldown reduced from 140/100/60 seconds to 100/80/60 seconds.

_______________________________________________

^059Puppet Master^*

^256Voodoo Puppet^*
^947-^* Cooldown reduced from 130/120/110 seconds to 120/110/100 seconds.

_______________________________________________

^059Qi^*

^256Crippling Puncture^*
^947-^* Movement Speed tapering midpoint shifted from 15% to 50% of the total slow duration.
   ^274*^* This is a small buff, as the tapering mechanic starts later in the slow duration.

^256Ancestral Assault^*
^947-^* Initial delay reduced from 0.6 seconds to 0.4 seconds.
^947-^* Number of instances changed from 1/2/3 to 3.
^947-^* SuperiorMagic Damage reduced from 125 to 50/90/130.
^947-^* Triggered damage from Enlightenment caused by Ancestral Assault deals 0.4/0.7/1x Damage.
^947-^* Disarm and Perplex duration changed from 1 second to 0.5/0.75/1 seconds.
^947-^* Now also applies a 20% Movement Speed Slow.

^947+^* These changes should smooth out Ancestral Assault and make its performance more appropriate at each phase of the game, without making it feel bad in the early phases of the game.

_______________________________________________

^059Rampage^*

^256Stampede^*
^947-^* Movement Speed bonus now lingers for 1 second after hitting the target.

^256Horned Strike^*
^947-^* While moving, cooldown is reduced at a rate of (Movement Speed)/1000 seconds.

^256The Chains That Bind^*
^947-^* Physical Damage dealt increased from 25/35/45% to 35/45/55% of the distance travelled.

^947+^* Rampage's kit now plays more towards having higher Movement Speed and encourages Rampage to keep moving!

_______________________________________________

^059Riptide^*

^256Perfect Storm^*
^947-^* Cooldown reduced from 160/120/80 seconds to 130/105/80 seconds.

_______________________________________________

^059Shadowblade^*

^947-^* Base Strength, Agility and Intelligence reduced from 25 to 22.
^947-^* Strength, Agility and Intelligence growth per level increased from 1.8 to 2.
^947-^* Starting Attack Damage reduced from 55-58 to 47-50.

^256Soul's Sight^*
^947-^* Mana Cost increased from 45/50/55/60 to 50/60/70/80.
^947-^* While Soul's Sight is active, Mana Cost is increased to 70/100/130/160.
   ^274*^* This is done to compensate for the extra mana you get from Intelligence form.
^947-^* Stun duration reduced from 0.9/1/1.1/1.2 seconds to 0.5/0.6/0.7/0.8 seconds.
^947-^* Magic Damage dealt on initial proc reduced from 20/30/40/50 to 16/24/32/40.
^947-^* Now procs its one-time bonus on-hit effects when attacking an enemy unit (as opposed to just enemy heroes).
^947-^* One-time bonus on-hit effect now expires after 5 seconds if it is not used.

^256Essence Shift^*
^947-^* When Strength is your main attribute: max Health lost per point of bonus Strength granted by this ability reduced from 3 to 2.5/2/1.5.

^947+^* Shadowblade has been liked mostly as a carry that can choose and change their main attribute/build throughout the game, rather than a jack-of-all-trades hero. These changes will change his stats to be more suitable for the carry role.
^947+^* These changes will also reduce the extremes of his current frustrations (Soul's Sight first-hit effect from the previous patch).
^947+^* The changes to Essence Shift should make Shadowblade's Strength build more viable in the mid and late game without contributing to the frustration of him being unkillable in the early game.
^947+^* Soul's Sight will still have its uses, as it was the most largely neglected form.
^947+^* In the end, all 3 forms should be viable depending on the game & route you want to go with as Shadowblade.

_______________________________________________

^059Shellshock^*

^256Rolling Thunder^*
^947-^* Removed 0.3 second delay from initial activation.
^947-^* Delay after actually launching (second activation) increased from 0.1 seconds to 0.2 seconds.

_______________________________________________

^059Soulstealer^*

^256Dread^*
^947-^* Armor penalty for enemies increased from 2/3/4/5 to 3/4/5/6.
^947-^* Now also passively grants Soulstealer 0.5/1/1.5/2 Magic Armor Penetration.
^947-^* Can now toggle the aura on and off with the "Extra Ability 1" hotkey (default hotkey "D").
   ^274*^* This hotkey change does override the short range Demon Hand cast hotkey - however, most players use the "Primary Ability 1" hotkey (default hotkey "Q") to cast the short range Demon Hand.

_______________________________________________

^059Swiftblade^*

^256Swift Slashes^*
^947-^* Now Sights and Reveals the main target for 0.2 seconds when this ability is fully cast.

_______________________________________________

^059War Beast^*

^256Metamorphosis^*
^947-^* While active, max Movement Speed for your own units reduced from 550/600/650 to 550/575/600.

_______________________________________________





=^075 Items ^*=

Changes to Armor Items:

^077Ring of the Teacher^*
^947-^* Armor bonus for self increased from 1 to 2.
^947-^* Armor bonus to allies from the aura reduced from 2 to 1.
^947-^* Mana Regeneration bonus from the aura increased from 0.65 to 0.8.

^077Abyssal Skull^*
^947-^* Now passively grants 2 Armor to self.
^947-^* Armor bonus to allies from the aura reduced from 5 to 3.5.
^947-^* Mana Regeneration bonus from the aura increased from 0.8 to 1.0.

^077Nome's Wisdom^*
^947-^* Now passively grants 2 Armor to self.
^947-^* Armor bonus to allies from the aura reduced from 2 to 1.
^947-^* Mana Regeneration bonus from the aura increased from 1 to 1.2.

^077Plated Greaves^*
^947-^* Armor granted to allies from the active state reduced from 1.5 to 1.
^947-^* Movement Speed bonus reduced from 65 to 55.

^077Sol's Bulwark^*
^947-^* Armor bonus to allies from the ally aura reduced from 3 to 2.

^947+^* Armor items have had far too much benefit for allies at such a low cost.
^947+^* The aura effects of these items were generally made weaker so that Physical Damage is not countered so easily and quickly for an entire team in the early phases of the game.

_______________________________________________

Changes to the Mana Battery line of items:

^077Mana Battery^*
^947-^* Added back into the game (exact same item as Power Supply from Patch 4.9.1, just with a different name, icon and visual effects).
   ^274*^* Cost: 200 Gold
   ^274*^* Available to be purchased in the Relics shop and Outpost.

^077Power Supply^*
^947-^* Now upgrades from Mana Battery.
^947-^* Components: Mana Battery (200 Gold) + Minor Totem (50 Gold) + Minor Totem (50 Gold) + Recipe (240 Gold) = 540 Gold Total
^947-^* Passively grants 3 Strength, Agility and Intelligence.
^947-^* Maximum of 15 charges.
^947-^* No longer purchasable in the Outpost.
^947-^* The other effects are the same as Mana Battery.

^077Sacrificial Stone^*
^947-^* Recipe cost increased from 110 Gold to 420 Gold.
   ^274*^* Total cost increased from 850 Gold to 1500 Gold.
^947-^* Base bonus Strength, Agility and Intelligence increased from 5 to 8.
^947-^* Increased Healing Done adjusted from +15% to +20%.

^947+^* Mana Battery and Power Supply both fulfilled unique benefits and had solid reasons to be picked up early in the game at their specific price points, so they were both largely reverted back to their legacy states.
^947+^* Sacrificial Stone would be too stat-efficient if its recipe cost was not adjusted, though we still want the item to have a solid reason to be picked up. Therefore, it is now one of the most stat-efficient items to pick up and upgrades from a fantastic item at a moderate (but relatively affordable) cost. It also buffs healing output even more as its particular niche effect.

_______________________________________________

^077Frostwolf's Skull^*
^947-^* Movement Speed Slow (when used by a melee hero) increased from 35% to 40%.
^947-^* Scouting activation: radius increased from 5000 units to 7000 units.
^947-^* Scouting activation: cooldown reduced from 30 seconds to 24 seconds.
^947-^* Dire Frost Wolf activation: cooldown reduced from 50 seconds to 40 seconds.
^947-^* Dire Frost Wolf activation: in addition to its current effects, also applies a 1 second Root on impact.

^077Ghost Marchers^*
^947-^* Passive Movement Speed bonus reduced from 70 to 60.
^947-^* Bonus Attack Damage reduced from 24 to 22.

^077Grimoire of Power^*
^947-^* Recipe cost reduced from 1100 Gold to 900 Gold.
   ^274*^* Total cost reduced from 5400 Gold to 5200 Gold.
^947-^* Active effect has been reworked:
   ^274*^* Activate to grant+30% Spell Damage to self for 15 seconds.

^947+^* The defensive effect on Grimoire of Power was too strong for the game. It is likely to make a return with more appropriate negative side effects on another item in the future.
^947+^* In the meantime, Grimoire of Power's effect is reminiscent of its previous passive effect.

_______________________________________________

^077Orb of Zamos & Grave Locket^*
^947-^* Bonus gold multiplier increased from 0.25x to 0.75x when a non-player controlled Neutral Creep is killed by the corresponding Synchronized hero.

^077Ultor's Heavy Helm^*
^947-^* Component change: instead of Ringmail, now requires a Shield of the Five.
^947-^* Recipe cost reduced from 1025 Gold to 675 Gold.
   ^274*^* Total cost remains the same (3650 Gold).
^947-^* Now passively grants 2 bonus Strength, Agility and Intelligence.
^947-^* Active effect now also grants 1 Armor to affected allies.
   ^274*^* Bonus Armor effect does not stack with Plated Greaves or Shield of the Five.
^947-^* Damage Block value for self increased from 70 to 80 (35 to 40 if you are a ranged hero that has Ultor's Heavy Helm).

^947+^* Shield of the Five now builds into an item other than Plated Greaves, making Ultor's Heavy Helm more viable (especially with the nerfs to the other team Armor items).
^947+^* Credits to datfizh for the general suggestions for Ultor's Heavy Helm!

^077Void Talisman^*
^947-^* Recipe cost reduced from 1160 Gold to 1060 Gold.
   ^274*^* Total cost reduced from 1700 Gold to 1600 Gold.

_______________________________________________

User Interface
^947-^* New UI: no longer warns that you have no region selected when attempting to start a Public Game or Bot Match game.
^947-^* Lobby music now plays properly (there was something wrong with the encoding of the sound file).
^947-^* When a hero denies themselves, the game properly displays a message using gender-neutral terminology ("X has killed themselves").

Healing is now sourced from the appropriate entity for the following sources:
^947-^* Accursed's Flame Consumption
^947-^* Blood Hunter's Feast
^947-^* Deadlift's Soul Syphon
^947-^* Defiler's Unholy Expulsion

^947-^* Nymphora's Volatile Pod

=^075 Heroes ^*=

^059Adrenaline^*
^947-^* Shard Blast's sound effects are now quieter.
^947-^* Shard Blast will no longer play a sound for each time it hits an opponent (and instead will only play once per cast instance).
^947-^* Ember Shard state on enemies now expire when Adrenaline is dead.
   ^274*^* However, if they are already getting pulled when Adrenaline actually dies, the pull will still continue as intended.
^947-^* If the leash range is broken by the target, Ember Shard no longer pulls enemies if they are invulnerable or have Physical Immunity.
^947-^* Fixed Death's Halo detection radius so that units from the outside do not accidentally get sucked into the center if they direct path towards the inside (from the outside) and then attempt to walk away again.

^059Amun-Ra^*
^947-^* Ignite now display the appropriate Magic Damage value in the popup.

^059Cthulhuphant^*
^947-^* Obliterate's visual model is now correct for the default Cthulhuphant avatar and King Cthulhuphant avatar.
^947-^* Sound effects for Dreams of Madness and Obliterate now plays correctly for the Woolly Cthulhuphant, Royal Cthulhuphant, Caller Cthulhuphant, Daemon Highlord, and HoNTour Cthulu avatars.
   ^274*^* Credits to Gimj for submitting some of these changes!
^947-^* Trample no longer displaces enemies with Push Immunity.

^059Deadlift^*
^947-^* Onslaught's leash range is now properly adjusted if Onslaught's cast range is increased (e.g. by Jade Spire).
^947-^* All Undead creeps spawned from Onslaught now get the proper Movement Speed state.
^947-^* Undead creeps spawned from Onslaught will now have the correct behaviour after being bound to a projectile.
^947-^* Soul Syphon now heals for the correct amount when the minions spawned from Onslaught expire.
^947-^* Soul Syphon now heals Deadlift properly if a unit with a timed life expires naturally.

^059Empath^*
^947-^* In-game icon now displays the default avatar icon (rather than the HD avatar icon).

^059Engineer^*
^947-^* Ol' Briarpatch Engineer's Energy Field gadget model is now properly displayed on Low Model graphic settings.

^059Gemini^*
^947-^* Light Wolf can now purchase items from an item shop.
   ^274*^* Items will be transferred to Gemini's inventory if there is enough space. Otherwise, it will go to the Stash as per regular behaviour.
   ^274*^* In other words, purchase mechanics are the same as if Fire was purchasing items from an item shop.
   ^274*^* Note that Light does not actually have its own item inventory.

^059Goldenveil^*
^947-^* Perch & Plunge's cooldown is now set to the correct value when the Perched tree is destroyed.

^059Ichor^*
^947-^* The healing from Life Leech is now properly sourced from Ichor (not the target).
   ^274*^* Affects interactions from healing increase items like Sacrificial Stone.
^947-^* Life Leech's projectile no longer bypasses Magic Immunity if the target gains Magic Immunity after Life Leech has been cast, but before Life Leech's projectile hits them.
^947-^* Transfusion's initial projectile now properly deals Magic Damage to enemies in the impact area even if it has been disjointed.

^059Kane^*
^947-^* Face Off now properly cancels Homecoming Stone if the target is Disarmed.

^059Legionnaire^*
^947-^* Decapitate no longer attempts to deal Magic Damage to an enemy unit that he was already designated to kill if they meet Decapitate's kill conditions.

^059Midas^*
^947-^* Midas now travels with the projectile of Elemental Warp.
   ^274*^* This has no gameplay impact in most situations and makes the behaviour consistent with other projectile-based travel behaviour from other abilities.

^059Nomad^*
^947-^* True Strike now applies a lingering sighted/revealed state on the target for 1 second or until True Strike's attack occurs (whichever occurs first), after its trigger conditions are met (i.e. when entering into melee range of the target).
^947-^* True Strike's trigger range reduced from 200 to 175.
   ^274*^* All of these small adjustments to True Strike/Mirage Strike mitigate the instances where the skill is being abnormally cancelled in certain conditions (e.g. near trees when the opponent is attempting to fog you).
   ^274*^* In the majority of circumstances, this will not affect gameplay and only serve as quality-of-life changes.

^059Pebbles^*
^947-^* Chuck now properly deals damage to the thrown unit when the thrown unit is unbound from the projectile.

^059Pestilence^*
^947-^* Fixed an exploit where Swarm (when boosted by Staff of the Master) can globally affect an enemy unit.

^059Prisoner^*
^947-^* The ball and chain from Shackled will now properly follow a unit if that unit becomes order disjointed (e.g. with Fayde's Shadow Walk ability).
^947-^* Prison Break's effects (when boosted by Staff of the Master) now properly affects Physically Immune units if targeting self.
^947-^* Prison Break's effects (when boosted by Staff of the Master) no longer become dispelled if an affected target gains invulnerability at any point before the state naturally expires.

^059Rampage^*
^947-^* Might of the Herd no longer interrupts your order queue when activated.
^947-^* Tooltip for Horned Strike no longer mistakenly says that it has Bash properties.

^059Rhapsody^*
^947-^* In-game icon now displays the default avatar icon (rather than the HD avatar icon).

^059Riptide^*
^947-^* Undertow's tooltip now properly states it deals 30/45/60/75 Magic Damage.
   ^274*^* No gameplay change has been made, as Undertow still deals this damage even prior to this patch.

^059Shadowblade^*
^947-^* Soul's Sight's effects that apply on the first hit will now properly check for misses (e.g. against Void Talisman or Evasion sources) and immunities (e.g. Shrunken Head) when the attack projectile impacts, rather than predetermining the effect when the attack projectile is launched.
^947-^* Fixed some incorrect model positions for Shadowblade's avatars in the HoN store.

^059Solstice^*
^947-^* Illuminate no longer interrupts your order queue when activated.

^059Tempest^*
^947-^* Elementals do not inherit their forced-attack order after splitting if they were affected by Deadlift's Onslaught state.

^059Vindicator^*
^947-^* Space Wizard Merlin's Glyph of Silence now properly plays its catchy space jam soundtrack while it is active!

^059Wildsoul^*
^947-^* Throwback Wildsoul now shows the correct model in the HoN store and avatar selection screen.

^059Witch Slayer^*
^947-^* Attack projectiles for Pimp Slayer and Witch Hunter now have the correct models.



=^075 Items ^*=

^077Bottle, Health Potion, Mana Potion, Rejuvenation Potion, Refreshment Rune effects^*
^947-^* Damage from sources that are non-boss, non-player controlled (NPC) neutral creeps (when not owned by a player) will no longer accidentally disable the active states of these items if the source deals damage to the hero while the source is dead (e.g. a ranged projectile that hits a hero after the source dies).
^947-^* Refreshment Rune's state is now consistent with the behaviour of the related state effects from Bottle, Health Potion, Mana Potion and Rejuvenation Potion.

^077Genjuro^*
^947-^* Each player can only purchase a maximum of 1 Genjuro per game.
^947-^* Genjuro can no longer be dropped.

^077Homecoming Stone^*
^947-^* Disarm state on towers is no longer considered a Debuff.
   ^274*^* This should prevent assist attribution bugs if a tower kills a hero while someone is teleporting to it.

^077Veiled Rot^*
^947-^* Effect now also applies in a 600 radius around the user for the user's owned units when the fade state expires naturally.
   ^274*^* This does not change much in most situations, but it specifically lets Parasite properly take advantage of Veiled Rot with his Infested creep (i.e. use Veiled Rot, then Infest a creep).

^077Whispering Helm^*
^947-^* Can no longer be activated on a Mimic (e.g. Moira's Ephemeral Forge, Circe's Twisted Visage).

=^075 Cosmetic Updates ^*=

^947-^* HUD has been reverted to its state prior to Patch 4.9.0.
^947-^* Music tracks have been reverted to their state prior to Patch 4.9.0.
^947-^* Christmas is over, thus the Forests of Caldavar ("New") and Mid Wars maps have reverted to its state prior to Patch 4.9.0.
   ^274*^* After some reconsideration, keeping the UI in from February to April is not suitable.

^947-^* The priority level of name colours (in which they display in a chat channel) have been readjusted to be more suitable in terms of their overall value.
   ^274*^* The most notable change is the Gold Shield color - players who use this name colour appear above all other non-volunteer, non-staff name colours (for their help to get HoN started)!



=^075 User Interface ^*=

^947-^* Region selection checkboxes are now visible in the "Play Now" screen (for the "new UI"), without having to press the "Regions" button.
   ^274*^* This change should improve visibility on what regions a user is queuing for before actually queuing up for a game.

^947-^* Region selection for all players will be reset when the patch occurs.
   ^274*^* By default,  no regions will be selected (as opposed to having all of them selected).

^947+^* This change will force players to select the regions they prefer, and should drastically alleviate issues with players playing on servers where they have high ping.



=^075 Forests of Caldavar ("New") ^*=

^539Kongor Buff Reward^*

Neutral Camps

^059Werebeast Enchanter^*
^947-^* Far Sight's Clearvision radius reduced from 1800 to 1200.



=^075 Ranked Pick ^*=

^947-^* Banning Phase duration increased from 20 seconds to 30 seconds.
^947-^* Wait time between end of banning phase and start of picking phase reduced from 5 seconds to 3 seconds.


=^075 Matchmaking ^*=

^947-^* Player colour order is now fully randomized in Matchmaking and Champions of Newerth games.
   ^274*^* In other words, the player with the highest MMR in Matchmaking and Champions of Newerth games will no longer always be in the Blue or Pink player slot.



=^075 General ^*=

^947-^* Ranged Creep aggro range reduced from 800 to 750.



=^075 Heroes ^*=

^059Adrenaline^*
^947-^* Primary attribute changed from Agility to Strength.
^947-^* Base Armor increased from 2.36 to 3.
^947-^* Base Strength increased from 20 to 24.
^947-^* Strength growth per level increased from 2 to 2.5.
^947-^* Base Agility reduced from 24 to 18.
^947-^* Agility growth per level reduced from 2.4 to 1.6.
^947-^* Base Intelligence increased from 18 to 20.
^947-^* Intelligence growth per level increased from 1.5 to 1.8.

^256Shard Blast^*
^947-^* Bonus damage is now based on Strength (instead of Agility).
^947-^* Mana Cost reduced from 80/90/100/110 to 55/70/85/100.
^947-^* Base Magic Damage increased from 80/115/150/185 to 80/120/160/200.
^947-^* Bonus Magic Damage based on your primary attribute increased from 20/30/40/50% to 30/40/50/60%.
^947-^* Touch radius increased from 90 to 100 (120 on tooltip for cast indicator accuracy).
^947-^* Cooldown is now reduced to 0.5 seconds (from 0.6 seconds) when hitting 2 or more enemy heroes with this ability.

^256Rush^*
^947-^* Now behaves like a regular dash/charge with pathing (i.e. does not force the destination to be at the very end, like Dark Lady's Charging Strikes).
   ^274*^* No longer cuts trees along its path.
^947-^* Cooldown reduced from 14/13/12/11 seconds to 13/12/11/10 seconds.
^947-^* Range rescaled from 700 to 575/650/725/800.
^947-^* No longer has a Dread Stack mechanic.
^947-^* Magic Damage changed from 20 + (10 Magic Damage per Dread Stack consumed) to 80/120/160/200 + 30/40/50/60% of your Strength.

^256Ember Shard^*
^947-^* Tether duration changed from 1.8/2.2/2.6/3 seconds to 2/2.5/3/3.5 seconds.
^947-^* Self state duration increased from 4 seconds to 8 seconds.
^947-^* Now refreshes attack cooldown on cast.
^947-^* Enemies now also receive a 25% Movement Speed Slow (in addition to being Restrained) after being pulled.
^947-^* Restrain/Movement Speed Slow state duration changed from 1 second to 0.8/1.2/1.6/2 seconds.

^256Death's Halo^*
^947-^* Duration is reduced by 1 second when Adrenaline receives cumulative damage equal to 25% of his Max Health (from 20%).
^947-^* No longer grants an Agility bonus per real hero trapped.
^947-^* Now grants 4/6/8 bonus Strength, 2 bonus Attack Damage, and 1 bonus Armor per real hero trapped (x2 for enemy heroes).
^947-^* Magic Armor bonus per real hero caught increased from 1 to 1.5.

^947+^* Adrenaline was dependent on too many attributes, and many options could have been taken to address this. Changing from an Agility hero to a Strength hero was the option that was chosen. With the proper readjustments, he now only requires damage and mana sustain to be effective.

^947+^* Adrenaline was also still not surviving nearly as much as expected against multiple opponents during Death's Halo, so he received significant durability buffs while it is active.

_______________________________________________

^059Armadon^*

^256Armordillo^*
^947-^* Spine Burst damage threshold when taking damage to the rear reduced from 150 Damage to 130 Damage.

_______________________________________________

^059Artillery^*

^256LRM^*
^947-^* Now deals 0.8x Damage to non-hero units.

^947+^* Artillery's weakness of farming was alleviated a little too well. A slight reduction to damage dealt against non-hero units should balance his performance out once again.

_______________________________________________

^059Calamity^*

^256Dragon's Path^*
^947-^* Magic Damage per dragon reduced from 25/50/70/95 to 20/45/65/90.

^256Funeral Pyre^*
^947-^* Agility gain state on self duration reduced from 1.75x to 1.25x the original state duration.
^947-^* Damage dealt by the extra attack instance now deals 50/60/70/80% of Calamity's Attack Damage (instead of 100%).

^947+^* Calamity has seen a recent increase in play rate with great success, particularly in tournament play.
^947+^* Most of her damage is coming from Funeral Pyre and Dragon's Path, so small nerfs to these abilities should make it more bearable to deal with her in the laning phase and during mid-game.

_______________________________________________

^059Defiler^*

^256Unholy Expulsion^*
^947-^* Spirit Movement Speed and Max Movement Speed increased from 400/450/500 to 650.

_______________________________________________

^059Draconis^*
^947-^* Base Movement Speed reduced from 300 to 290.

^256Draconic Defense^*
^947-^* Cooldown increased from 60/55/50/45 seconds to 75/70/65/60 seconds.

^256Fiery Barrage^*
^947-^* Base Physical Damage per second changed from 6/12/18/24 to 5/10/15/20.

^947+^* These changes should mitigate Draconis' farming speed in the jungle early on and give him a little bit more of a weakness overall.

_______________________________________________

^059Drunken Master^*
^947-^* Base Agility increased from 12 to 14.

^256Drink^*
^947-^* Max Charges increased from 16/24/32/40 to 18/27/36/45.
   ^274*^* Total max Evasion and Stun/Debuff Duration Reduction increased from 16/24/32/40% to 18/27/36/45%.
^947-^* In addition to existing bonuses, each Drink charge now also grants a 1.5% Chance to Critically Strike for 1.5x Attack Damage.
^947-^* Healing from the natural expiration of a Drunk Charge increased from 6 Health to 7 Health.

^2563 Point Strike^*
^947-^* Physical Damage reduced from 300/475/600 to 250/375/500.

^947+^* Drunken Master has historically been too focused on being bursty and being built as a glass cannon.
^947+^* With the iterations of Drunken Master in 2018-2020, the major complaint was that 3 Point Strike required little to no skill to deal an immaculate amount of burst damage to his targets during the early to mid game.
^947+^* Recent changes have addressed this by making it take a bit more skill to achieve said burst and bring back the healthy mechanics of the original Drink ability, though the hero has been underperforming.

^947+^* To address all of these concerns and address the hero's performance, a decent amount of damage has been shifted from his ultimate and placed into his Drink mechanic.
^947+^* Maintaining a high number of Drink charges allow him to function as a traditional carry/semi-carry, granting him a multitude of defensive and offensive buffs, while still allowing him a choice to Drink for offense/defense and using his skills for mobility/damage while reducing the number of Drink Charges on himself.

_______________________________________________

^059Fayde^*

^256Reflection^*
^947-^* Now reduces the target's Healing Received and Health Regeneration by 20/40/60% for 4 seconds.

^947+^* This small change brings back some legacy effects onto Reflection.

_______________________________________________

^059Goldenveil^*

^256Perch & Plunge^*
^947-^* While perched on a tree, Perch no longer counts as an ability cast for certain sources (e.g. Power Supply).

^947+^* This change is more of a bugfix, but it has more implications on Goldenveil's performance in lane.

_______________________________________________

^059Ichor^*

^256Transfusion^*
^947-^* Cast effect type changed from Magic to SuperiorMagic.
   ^274*^* This allows Ichor to properly latch onto allies and enemies that have Magic Immunity.

_______________________________________________

^059Keeper of the Forest^*

^256Nature's Guidance^*
^947-^* Camouflage will now only apply its root state on enemies when attacking out of stealth if the attacker is a non-illusion Keeper of the Forest hero (i.e. not his minions or other ally heroes).
^947-^* Root duration reduced from 1.2/1.6/2/2.4 seconds to 1.2/1.4/1.6/1.8 seconds.

_______________________________________________

^059Kraken^*
^947-^* Base Intelligence increased from 16 to 18.
^947-^* Turn rate increased from 360 to 700 degrees per second.

^256Tsunami Charge^*
^947-^* Travel speed increased from 850 to 1000 units per second.

^947+^* A few small number tweaks for Kraken to keep up with the meta.

_______________________________________________

^059Monkey King^*
^947-^* Strength growth per level increased from 1.7 to 2.
^947-^* Base Armor increased from 2.8 to 3.2.

^947+^* A small survivability buff to Monkey King to keep up with the meta.

_______________________________________________

^059Moon Queen^*

^256Multi-Strike^*
^947-^* Bounce radius increased from 450 to 500.

^256Lunar Glow^*
^947-^* Moon Queen receives 1.5x the damage bonus from this ability.

^947+^* Moon Queen currently does not have a good enough reason to be picked over Forsaken Archer at the moment. These changes should help her deal more overall damage than Forsaken Archer if she is able to attack her foes.

_______________________________________________

^059Moraxus^*

^256More Axes^*
^947-^* Cooldown increased from 1.25 seconds to 1.5 seconds.

^947+^* The increased number of axes was a sufficient buff to Moraxus on its own. As a result, the cooldown of this ability is being reverted to its original values (prior to Patch 4.9.0).

_______________________________________________

^059Nitro^*

^256Ballistic^*
^947-^* Mana Cost reduced from 15 to 12.

^256Blast Off^*
^947-^* Mana Cost reduced from 100/110/120/130 to 95/105/115/125.

^947+^* A few minor buffs to Nitro were given as she is slightly underperforming. She should be fine where she is after these changes.

_______________________________________________

^059Nomad^*
^947-^* Strength growth per level increased from 1.8 to 2.
^947-^* Agility growth per level increased from 2.8 to 3.
^947-^* Intelligence growth per level increased from 2 to 2.2.

^256Edge Counter^*
^947-^* On expiration, now grants 20/25/30% Damage Reduction for the next 5 seconds.
   ^274*^* This state propagates to illusions he spawns while active.

^947+^* These changes should give Nomad just the right amount of durability he needs without making him too frustrating to deal with.

_______________________________________________

^059Ophelia^*

^256Nature's Wrath^*
^947-^* Cast Range reduced from 800 to 600.
^947-^* Duration reduced from 6 seconds to 5 seconds.

^256Command^*
^947-^* Cast Range reduced from 900 to 600.
^947-^* Cooldown increased from 8 seconds to 15 seconds.

^947+^* Ophelia has been picked and banned more frequently in competitive matches, so she is receiving a few nerfs. She will still be able to dominate the early game, but just does it a little less effectively now.

_______________________________________________

^059Puppet Master^*
^947-^* Base Movement Speed reduced from 300 to 295.

^256Whiplash^*
^947-^* No longer passively grants Attack Speed.

^256Voodoo Puppet^*
^947-^* Voodoo Puppet's Max Health increased from 500/700/900 to 500/750/1000.
^947-^* Damage multiplier reduced from 130/155/180% to 110/120/130%. 
^947-^* Cooldown changed from 150/135/120 seconds to 130/120/110 seconds.
^947-^* Leash Range increased from 1000 to 1500.
^947-^* Now passively grants Puppet Master 15/30/45 Attack Speed.

^947+^* Puppet Master's burst damage as a carry with ganking tools has been too high throughout the history of HoN.
^947+^* With the latest free passive bonus Attack Speed on Whiplash and Elder Parasite being a new meta item pickup for Puppet Master, his burst damage potential has been dampened in favour of having a bit more sustained damage over time compared to before.
^947+^* Voodoo Puppet is gradually becoming more of what it was originally designed for: a damage proxy for when the target goes out of range, and a proxy to apply Crowd Control to the target without being near the target. It can still be used to burst a target down at close range, but it is just less effective at doing so.
^947+^* Elder Parasite will also be receiving a minor nerf in conjunction with this change, as the item in general has been proven to be a little too powerful after its recent changes. These combined changes should put Puppet Master in a better state than before.

_______________________________________________

^059Rhapsody^*

^256Disco Inferno^*
^947-^* Magic Damage dealt to enemies reduced from 20/30/40/50 to 18/27/36/45.
^947-^* Healing per second applied to allies reduced from 13/22/31/40 to 10/18/26/34.

^947+^* Rhapsody has been performing well since her changes in the previous patch. Her numbers on Disco Inferno were proven to be a tad high, so they are being reduced slightly to compensate.

_______________________________________________

^059Shadowblade^*

^947-^* Strength, Agility and Intelligence growth per level increased from 1.5 to 1.8.

^256Gargantuan's Blast^*
^947-^* Gargantuan Form no longer changes Base Attack Time.

^256Feint's Siphon^*
^947-^* Attack Damage drain now applies against enemy non-player-controlled units with 0.5x the original values.

^256Soul's Sight^*
^947-^* The first attack against an enemy hero in this form deals a bonus 20/30/40/50 Magic Damage and applies a 0.9/1/1.1/1.2 second Stun.

^947+^* After looking at Shadowblade's performance, part of his kit has been reverted more closely to its legacy form, while still allowing Shadowblade to leverage factors (such as the Attack Damage steal from Ancients and Kongor) without them being too good in those situations.

^947+^* Soul's Sight now also provides more support-oriented bonuses to allow Shadowblade to have some capabilities as a support hero when underfarmed or when underperforming. Soul's Sight also gained a bit more laning utility to help Shadowblade perform better in this phase.

_______________________________________________

^256Rolling Thunder^*

_______________________________________________

^256Silver Bullet^*

^947+^* Witch Slayer is still not great in a competitive environment - these changes aim to keep him more relevant in the meta, while reducing the polarizing performance of Silver Bullet slightly (while maintaining it as a net buff prior to patch 4.9.0).

^059Zephyr^*

^256Wind Shield^*

   ^274*^* This prevents melee units from missing if they attack at the edge of Zephyr's hurtbox and Zephyr is currently moving away from an enemy melee unit.

=^075 Items ^*=



^077Barbed Armor ^*
^947-^* Duration reduced from 5 seconds to 4 seconds.
^947-^* Returned Damage type is now the same as the received damage type.
^947-^* Returned Damage percentage increased from 65% to 80%.
^947-^* Cooldown increased from 30 seconds to 40 seconds.

^947+^* The Returned Damage type change makes it so that the wielder of Barbed Armor will not reduce the amount of Damage Returned to their attacker based on the wielder's Armor/Magic Armor value (this was counter-synergy and was why Barbed Armor was considered a "bad item" in the past).

^947+^* Instead, the damage will now be returned of the appropriate type, so that the Returned Damage will be mitigated by the appropriate Armor type of the attacker (and not the wielder of Barbed Armor).

^947+^* Overall, these changes allow appropriate counterplay from the attacker's perspective without the effect of Barbed Armor being too strong, while still enabling Barbed Armor to do its job and scale appropriately as the game goes on.

_______________________________________________

^077Elder Parasite^*
^947-^* Passive Attack Damage bonus reduced from 10 to 5.
^947-^* Damage Amplification for damage received while the state is active increased from 12% to 15%.
^947-^* Passive bonus Attack Speed reduced from 20 to 15.
^947-^* Recipe Cost increased from 600 Gold to 800 Gold.
   ^274*^* Total Cost increased from 2000 Gold to 2200 Gold.

^947+^* Elder Parasite is supposed to be a mid-game high-risk, high-reward item for autoattack carries, and has been readjusted accordingly to fill this role. It was just a tad too good in the previous patch.

_______________________________________________

^077Lightbrand, Frozen Light, Searing Light, Dawnbringer, Grimoire of Power^*
^947-^* The Searing state is no longer applied in a 200 radius.

^077Grimoire of Power^*
^947-^* Debuff Reduction and Stun Reduction state duration reduced from 1 seconds to 0.6 seconds.
^947-^* Recipe Cost increased from 700 Gold to 1100 Gold.
   ^274*^* Total Cost increased from 5000 Gold to 5400 Gold.
^947-^* Can no longer be activated while Perplexed or under a Forced Order effect (e.g. Legionnaire's Taunt).
^947-^* Cooldown increased from 70 seconds to 90 seconds (becomes 72 seconds after its own cooldown reduction mechanic).
^947-^* Now applies an Activation Modifier state for 2 seconds, which prevents other Activation Modifiers from being applied while active.
^947-^* Now uses an Activation Modifier (i.e. this cannot be active at the same time as Shrunken Head or Void Talisman).

^947+^* Grimoire of Power has proven to be an incredibly viable item pickup option in the game compared to Frostwolf's Skull and Geometer's Bane (finally). In fact, it is likely a little too good right now, as this item also allows you to farm better compared to its counterparts. Because of this, a cost increase is perfectly justified.
^947+^* The active effect has been nerfed a little bit to make sure that it still does what it should do (allow the user to cast at least one final ability after breaking CC) without that duration being too long, as well as preventing items like Shrunken Head from being used to guarantee an escape.

_______________________________________________

^077Ioyn Stone^*
^947-^* Can now be purchased at the Outpost.

_______________________________________________

^077Iron Shield^*
^947-^* Deflection chance against enemy heroes reduced from 100% to 70%.

^947+^* This change allows the item to still be a strong pickup on melee Agility heroes in the laning phase, but does not enable their trades to be too effective.

_______________________________________________

^077Orb of Zamos & Grave Locket^*
^947-^* Now only generate 0.25x bonus Gold when a non-player controlled Neutral Creep is killed by the corresponding Synchronized hero.

^947+^* This item was simply not intended to synergize well with heroes that clear Neutral Creeps throughout the game.

_______________________________________________

^077Pegasus Boots^*
^947-^* Now prioritizes teleporting to ally heroes before prioritizing other targets.

_______________________________________________

^077Power Supply^*
^947-^* No longer has a recipe (i.e. it is a standalone item now).
^947-^* Now costs 200 Gold.
^947-^* No longer passively grants bonus Strength, Agility or Intelligence.
^947-^* Max charges reduced from 15 to 10.
^947-^* Can now be purchased at the Outpost.

^077Sacrificial Stone^*
^947-^* New component buildup: Power Supply (200) + Major Totem (540) + Recipe (110) = 850 Gold Total
^947-^* Increased Healing Done adjusted from +10% to +15%.
^947-^* Health restored per charge increased from 10 to 12.

^947+^* Power Supply now functions like the old Mana Battery - it was too cost-efficient for the stats it provided at the 300 Gold price point, and there was a need for the item to be at a low cost for its active functionality.

^947+^*To prevent changing the item hotkey order once again, Power Supply and Sacrificial Stone were properly adjusted (rather than reintroducing Mana Battery).

_______________________________________________

^077Veiled Rot^*
^947-^* Now applies to the user even if they are invulnerable (addresses a corner case when respawning at fountain right away).

_______________________________________________

^077Void Talisman^*
^947-^* Recipe Cost increased from 960 Gold to 1160 Gold.
   ^274*^* Total Cost increased from 1500 Gold to 1700 Gold.
^947-^* Passive Strength, Agility and Intelligence bonus reduced from 6 to 5.
^947-^* While Void Talisman is active, your hero deals 25% less Damage.

^947+^* Void Talisman now carries a little bit more of a penalty when used, as well as it being less efficient of an item when picked up.
^947+^* It should still be accessible enough to support heroes, while reducing the damage output of certain heroes while active.

==^960 Design ^*==



=^075 Hero Rework - Adrenaline ^*=

^947+^* Adrenaline has been reworked to have a completely new niche, specializing in catching heroes with high mobility.

^947+^* Death's Halo is the crux of the rework, essentially being changed to act as an "Arena" skill. This skill forces all units to essentially be confined within a space for a duration, essentially locking down a teamfight to take place in an area.

^947+^* As a result, Adrenaline also acts as a true counter to heroes with escape abilities (or the recipient of mobility sources like Rally's Compel or Tablet of Command) by forcing them to stay within Death's Halo.

^947+^* Adrenaline also has a new playstyle that favours engaging multiple heroes at once, while aiming to catch and trap as many heroes as possible to prevent their escape & give his team an advantage.

^947+^* In the process, Adrenaline lost his key frustration factors when his opponents were playing against him. These frustration factors include (but are not limited to): True Damage % based on Max Health with no actual counterplay, having too much uptime on his lane harass, and having way too many dashes available at once, gated only by Mana Cost rather than Mana Cost and cooldown.

^947+^* Because of all these changes, Adrenaline's "energy" mechanic has been removed from the game. It allowed Adrenaline to have infinite harass in the laning phase & his skills had to be much weaker to compensate for that, and was overall too difficult to properly balance for the scope of the entire game. The "energy" mechanic just does not have a place in HoN where Health Sustain sources are costly to obtain in the laning phase.

 



^059Adrenaline (Reworked)^*

^947-^* Attack Range changed from 450 to 150.
   ^274*^* Now considered a Melee hero.
^947-^* Movement Speed increased from 290 to 310.
^947-^* Base Strength increased from 19 to 20.
^947-^* Strength growth per level increased from 1.9 to 2.
^947-^* Agility growth per level reduced from 2.5 to 2.4.
^947-^* Base Intelligence increased from 10 to 18.
^947-^* Intelligence growth per level increased from 1 to 1.5.
^947-^* Model size increased by approximately 30%.


^075Ability 1: Shard Blast (Reworked)^*
   ^274*^* Range: 800
   ^274*^* Mana Cost: 80/90/100/110
   ^274*^* Touch Radius: 90 (110 on tooltip for cast range indicator accuracy)
   ^274*^* Cooldown: 4 seconds

^947-^* Target a direction to throw a fast piercing Shard, dealing Magic Damage equal to 80/115/150/185+ 20/30/40/50% of your Agility to enemy units hit (0.75x damage to non-hero units).
^947-^* The Shard applies a base 15% Movement Speed Slow that lasts 2 seconds. Each Shard increases the slow by 5%, stacking up to 1/2/3/4 times.
^947-^* If the Shard hits 2 or more enemy heroes, the cooldown is reduced to 0.6 seconds and 60% of the Mana Cost is refunded.
^947-^* Reduces the cooldown of Rush by 1.5 seconds for each enemy hero hit.



^075Ability 2: Rush (Reworked)^*
   ^274*^* Range: 700
   ^274*^* Mana Cost: 70
   ^274*^* Cooldown: 14/13/12/11 seconds

Passive:
^947-^* Applies 4 Dread Stacks with autoattacks and 5/6/7/8 Dread Stacks from Shard Blast when hitting an enemy unit. Dread Stacks last 6 seconds and refresh on hit.
^947-^* Max of 12/16/20/24 Dread Stacks applied on an enemy unit.

Active:
^947-^* Target a location to charge towards it, destroying trees and consuming all Dread Stacks from enemies you pass through and dealing Magic Damage equal to (20 + 10 Magic Damage per Dread Stack consumed).
^947-^* Adrenaline gains 16/24/32/40% Movement Speed for 3 seconds upon dashing through an enemy hero.



^075Ability 3: Ember Shard (Reworked)^*
   ^274*^* Mana Cost:  70/80/90/100
   ^274*^* Cooldown: 15 seconds

^947-^* Activate to empower your next attack within 4 seconds with your Ember Shard, cleaving for 100% of your Attack Damage in a 250/300/350/400 radius, and Restrains + tethers affected enemy heroes for the next 1.8/2.2/2.6/3 seconds.
^947-^* If the distance between a tethered enemy hero and Adrenaline exceeds 750 units, the hero is pulled to Adrenaline. The enemy hero is Stunned & Restrained while being pulled towards Adrenaline, then Restrained for 1 second afterwards.
   ^274*^* Max pull distance of 1000 units.



^075Ability 4: Death's Halo (Reworked)^*

   ^274*^* Mana Cost: 100/150/200
   ^274*^* Cooldown: 100/90/80 seconds
   ^274*^* Radius: 750
^947-^* Activate to spawn Death's Halo around Adrenaline for 5/6/7 seconds, surrounding the area with shard walls.
^947-^* All units caught within Death's Halo cannot escape its area (including teleportation or "blink" skills of any kind).
   ^274*^* Units who do end up outside of Death's Halo will be forcefully teleported to a point back inside Death's Halo within half of this ability's radius.
^947-^* While active, Adrenaline gains 100% Slow Resistance, 100% Movement Speed, and 6/8/10 Agility and 1 Magic Armor per real hero caught (2x for enemy heroes).
^947-^* Adrenaline can cancel Death's Halo prematurely 2 seconds after it is cast.
^947-^* Death's Halo is destroyed if Adrenaline dies.
^947-^* Duration of Death's Halo is reduced by 1 second every time Adrenaline receives cumulative damage equal to 20% of his Max Health.

^947+^* Blacksmith is receiving numerous buffs (mentioned later in the patch notes), so the boosted Chaotic Flames ability (Risky Cast) had to be more limited in its usage, as it was used as a "free" second proc of Fireball most of the time with generally unlimited availability.
