=^075 Captain's Pick ^*=

^947-^* The lobby host can now manually designate a team to get the First Ban before the game starts.
^947-^* Number of Rounds in the first Banning Phase increased from 2 to 3.
   ^274*^* Total number of Hero Bans in the first Banning Phase increased from 4 to 6.
^947-^* Hero Pick alternation method changed.
   ^274*^* Current method: 1-1-1-1-1-1 (total of 6 picks, 3 per team) for the first Hero Pick Phase, then 1-1-1-1 (total of 4 picks, 2 per team) for the second Hero Pick Phase.
   ^274*^* New method: 1-2-2 (total of 5 hero picks, 3 for the team that has First Ban and 2 for the other team) for the first Hero Pick Phase, then 2-2-1 (total of 5 picks, 3 for the team did not have First Ban and 2 for the other team) for the second Hero Pick Phase.
^947-^* Base time per Hero Pick Phase increased from 30 seconds to 35 seconds.
^947-^* Second Banning Phase now begins with the team that did not have First Ban at the start of the game.

^947+^* These changes should reduce the advantages of the team that has the first hero pick and gives the team that goes second some opportunity to counter pick in the drafting phase of the game.
^947+^* Overall, these changes allow for increased hero pick diversity in competitive tournaments!



=^075 General ^*=

^947-^* Towers: Bonus Attack Damage dealt to heroes changed from 20 to 20 + (1 per game minute elapsed).
^947-^* Heal Output increase mechanic now functions properly with Lifesteal.
   ^274*^* Examples of Heal Output increase mechanics: Sacrificial Stone, Rhapsody's new Arcane Hymn effects.



=^075 Kongor ^*=

^947-^* Now has a base 30% Slow Resistance.
^947-^* Enrage state: now also grants Kongor +30% Slow Resistance.
   ^274*^* These changes only apply for the Legion & Hellbourne Kongors on the Forests of Caldavar ("New") map.
^947-^* Bonus Siege Damage from the 2x Kongor Kill state now scales with time:
   ^274*^* Grows linearly from 20 minutes to 45 minutes.
   ^274*^* Minimum of 20% bonus Siege Damage, maximum of 60% bonus Siege Damage.


=^075 Mid Wars ^*=

^947-^* Hellbourne ramp that is closest to the Merrick's Rune spawn point has been adjusted to more closely resemble the angle that its Legion-side analog faces Merrick's Rune.
^947-^* Merrick's Rune can no longer be attacked in Mid Wars.
^947-^* Main Base Structure's Health Regeneration per second increased from 20 to 30 (when not damaged by an enemy within the last 10 seconds).




=^075 Heroes ^*=

^059Aluna^*

^256Emerald Lightning^*
^947-^* Attack Speed steal reduced from 50 to 20/30/40/50.
   ^274*^* The Attack Speed steal with Emerald Red's effect remains unchanged.

_______________________________________________

^059Arachna^*

^256Webbed Shot^*
^947-^* Magic Damage per second increased from 8/16/24/32 to 10/20/30/40.

^256Precision^*
^947-^* No longer grants True Strike to allies.

^947+^* This change should help Arachna be a slightly better lane bully, without bringing back all of the frustration factors associated with the hero.
^947+^* Precision's aura effect also overloaded the ability with utility. This effect is now transferred to Flint Beastwood's Dead Eye instead.

_______________________________________________

^059Artillery^*

^256LRM^*
^947-^* No longer deals reduced damage to non-hero units.
^947-^* Artillery is now Sighted for 1 second if LRM hits an enemy player-controlled unit.

^256Special Ammunition^*
^947-^* Bonus Cast Range on abilities reduced from 200/400/600/800 to 150/300/450/600.
^947-^* Movement Speed Slow applied by abilities changed from 15% to 13/16/19/22%.

^947+^* Artillery's premise for being a long range hero has been one of the main causes of the frustration in playing against the hero.
^947+^* Artillery was buffed in certain areas, while simultaneously reducing his max spell Cast Range values slightly until there is more time to properly address his hero design more thoroughly.

_______________________________________________

^059Blacksmith^*

^256Flaming Hammer^*
^947-^* Duration changed from 7.5 seconds to 5/6/7/8 seconds.
^947-^* Magic Damage per second rescaled from 10/20/30/40 to 20/25/30/35.
   ^274*^* Overall Magic Damage dealt (before factoring in the negative Magic Armor from this ability) changed from 75/150/225/300 to 100/150/210/280.

^256Frenzy^*
^947-^* Now grants an additional 10% Movement Speed for self.
^947-^* Now grants an additional 10/15/20/25 Attack Speed for self.

^256Chaotic Flames^*
^947-^* Chance for a strict 1x Multicast (i.e. no Multicast) reduced from 75/60/40% to 70/45/30%.
^947-^* Chance for a strict 2x Multicast increased from 25/20/30% to 30/35/35%.
^947-^* Chance for a strict 3x Multicast increased from 0/20/15% to 0/20/20%.
^947-^* Chance for a strict 4x Multicast remains unchanged at 0/0/15%.
   ^274*^* Overall long-term average Multicast multiplier increased from 2.05x to 2.2x.
   ^274*^* For Level 3 Chaotic Flames, average damage distribution for 1x/2x/3x/4x changed from approximately 20/29/22/29% to 14/32/27/27%, respectively.

^947+^* These changes to Blacksmith should considerably bolster his early game performance, giving him more early game reliability, roaming power and laning power (and thus, increasing his competitive viability).
^947+^* Additionally, these changes even open up other laning options and more dynamic roles for Blacksmith depending on how the game is going.
^947+^* The changes to Chaotic Flames resulted in a more normalized damage distribution while keeping the chance mechanic/flavour on Blacksmith, and also reduced the likelyhood of getting only a 1x Multicast when Chaotic Flames is at max level.
^947+^* Credits to MacroHard for assistance with the mathematics!

_______________________________________________

^059Bramble^*

^256Spore Breath^*
^947-^* Now heals Bramble for 0.5x the Magic Damage dealt to an enemy hero.
   ^274*^* Overhealing will grant Bramble a Shield state for 5 seconds. Max Shield Health of 400. Shield states are shared between abilities.

^256Chomp!^*
^947-^* Cooldown reduced from 12/10/8/6 seconds to 11/9/7/5 seconds.
^947-^* Now heals Bramble for 0.5x the bonus Magic Damage dealt by this ability.
   ^274*^* Overhealing will grant Bramble a shield state for 5 seconds. Max Shield Health of 400. Shield states are shared between abilities.

^947+^* These changes should allow Bramble to remain more durable in the front lines and allow Ensnaring Shrubbery to be used on allies (instead of herself) more frequently.

^059Drunken Master^*

^256Drink^*

   ^274*^* Max cooldown reduction for Lunge and Stagger increased from 2 seconds to 4 seconds (if the skill was channeled for the full duration).
^947-^* Mana Cost reduced from 50 to 20.
^947-^* Healing from the natural expiration of a Drunk Charge increased from 5 Health to 6 Health.
^947-^* Extra Movement Speed Slow granted to Lunge increased from 20% to 30%.
^947-^* Extra Movement Speed granted to Stagger increased from 30 to 50.
^947-^* Now gains 10 Drunk Charges upon obtaining a Kill or an Assist on a real enemy hero.

^947+^* Rather than addressing Drunken Master's other weaknesses, Drunken Master's Drink usage was made considerably more powerful so that he has access to Lunge & Stagger even earlier than before if he chooses to channel Drink mid-combat.

_______________________________________________

^059Engineer^*
^947-^* Base Strength reduced from 22 to 20.

^256The Keg^*
^947-^* Magic Damage changed from 110/160/205/240 to 90/150/210/270.

^256Steam Turret^*
^947-^* Movement Speed Slow per charge changed from 3.5% to 2.5/3/3.5/4%.
   ^274*^* Max Movement Speed Slow changed from 35% to 25/30/35/40%.
^947-^* Push distance per bullet hit increased from 10/15/20/25 to 15/25/35/45.
^947-^* Magic Damage per bullet changed from 10/18/26/34 to 14/18/22/26.
   ^274*^* Max Potential Damage changed from 100/180/260/340 to 140/180/220/260 (though the realistic damage from this ability is pitiful due to how the mechanics work).

^947+^* In practical situations, heroes only get hit by maybe 6-12 bullets, not 50. Steam Turret was never really used for its damage anyway and was more used for its zoning properties.
^947+^* Furthermore, the damage has been shifted more towards The Keg as it is a skillshot that should yield high rewards, while Steam Turret should remain more focused as a utility spell.
^947+^* In fact, Steam Turret's push effect is now much more formidable compared to before & has gained a big overall net buff in terms of utility.

^256Spider Mines^*
^947-^* Spider Mines now automatically detonate upon their initial placement if there is at least 1 enemy unit within its blast radius, dealing 0.75x Magic Damage when detonated in this manner.
^947-^* Spider Mines can now only be detonated manually once they have successfully been planted.
^947-^* Cooldown reduced from 8 seconds to 6.5 seconds.
^947-^* Max number of Spider Mines placed on the map at once reduced from 6 to 3/3/4/5.
^947-^* No longer has a Spider Mine stacking limit mechanic.

^947+^* The Magic Damage on Spider Mines has been reduced when used as an on-demand nuke so that the ability is less of a stellar reliable ranged nuke compared to other support heroes.
^947+^* Engineer had way more damage than other support heroes prior to these changes (he is secretly a hero with 3 nukes), so these changes put him on a more even power level relative to other support heroes, while frontloading more of the Magic Damage on The Keg (a skillshot) instead.
^947+^* The overall changes here should let Engineer have a smoother distribution of Magic Damage between his abilities & over time, rather than having him get all the meaningful Magic Damage in a reliable manner only through Spider Mines.
^947+^* With the changes to the max number of Spider Mines placed at once, there is no need for a stack limit mechanic any longer. By the time Engineer maxes out Spider Mines, there is a small time window where 5 Spider Mines can 1-shot an enemy hero, but this still needs considerable setup time & takes away from potential scouting uses for said time period. Once this short time period passes by, Engineer should use Spider Mines as a scouting tool and an on-demand nuke when it comes down to practical usage.
^947+^* The changes to the detonate mechanic removes a great discrepancy between lower & mid skill level brackets vs. high skill brackets in terms of hero performance statistics, so it should be much easier to appropriately balance Engineer for all skill brackets from this time onward.
^947+^* Overall, Engineer is now more in line with the damage progression of a ganker/nuker, while having support-like utility once he gets access to Energy Field and higher levels of Steam Turret.

_______________________________________________

^059Flint Beastwood^*

^256Hollowpoint Shells^*
^947-^* Bonus Physical Damage increased from 15/30/45/60 to 20/40/60/80.

^256Dead Eye^*
^947-^* Now grants allies within 900 radius a 25/50/75/100% chance to gain True Strike.
   ^274*^* Level 4 of the ability is considered to grant True Strike.

^947+^* The True Strike aura mechanic was taken from Arachna's Precision to give Flint Beastwood a little bit more of a reason to be picked in games.
^947+^* Flint Beastwood also got a small power boost on Hollowpoint Shells to keep up with the power level of today's meta.

^059Rampage^*

^075Ability 4: The Chains that Bind (Reworked)^*
   ^274*^* Range: 200
   ^274*^* Mana Cost: 150/200/250
   ^274*^* Cooldown: 100/85/70 seconds

^947-^* Binds the target to Rampage for 2.5/3/3.5 seconds and drags the target behind Rampage as he moves.
^947-^* The dragged target will take 25/35/45% of the distance they are dragged in Physical Damage.
^947-^* If Rampage and the dragged target gets separated by more than 600 units, the chains are broken.

^947+^* Rampage's Greater Rhino lacked uniqueness and function when compared to other heroes.
^947+^* By giving Rampage one of his iconic abilities back, he can once again controllably reposition a foe against their will.

^059Rhapsody^*
^947-^* Attack Range increased from 550 to 600.

^256Disco Inferno^*
^947-^* Enemies take 0.75x damage while they are moving on the Dance Floor (instead of taking no damage).
^947-^* Allies heal for 1.25x more while they are moving on the Dance Floor (instead of receiving no healing).
^947-^* Heal per second changed from 15/25/35/45 to 13/22/31/40.
^947-^* Rhapsody now has the option of attaching Disco Inferno to herself by pressing the ability hotkey for Arcane Hymn (hotkey "E" by default).

^256Arcane Hymn^*
^947-^* Now has 5 levels, with a base level of 1 (due to the Disco Inferno changes). At the base level, the ability will not do anything, but the active ability will be available if Disco Inferno is leveled.
^947-^* Radius increased from 500 to 900.
^947-^* Now also grants 15/20/25/30% increased Healing Output while affected by Arcane Hymn.
   ^274*^* Elaboration of mechanics: for Rhapsody, since Arcane Hymn applies to Rhapsody all the time, Disco Inferno will heal for an increased amount from Arcane Hymn.
   ^274*^* Another example: Jeraziah is affected by Arcane Hymn, and he casts Inner Light to heal an ally that is not affected by Arcane Hymn. Inner Light still gets its healing amplified, since the source of healing (Jeraziah) is affected by Arcane Hymn.
   ^274*^* An example where the healing output increase does not apply: Jeraziah is not affected by Arcane Hymn, and casts Inner Light on another hero that is affected by Arcane Hymn. Inner Light will not have its healing amplified, since the source of healing (Jeraziah) is not affected by Arcane Hymn.

^947+^* Rhapsody has been falling behind in the meta as other supports proved to be more versatile and reliable than her, so her abilities have been adjusted to compensate for that.
^947+^* Disco Inferno has a new option that properly rewards movement with Rhapsody & heroes in general, should the player choose to use that option.
^947+^* Note that Arcane Hymn's aura radius will no longer match Protective Melody's radius. You will have to have more spatial awareness to know when Protective Melody will affect an ally!

^059Shadowblade^*

^256Gargantuan's Blast^*
^947-^* Bonus Armor increased from 0.75/1.5/2.25/3 to 3/6/9/12.
^947-^* Bonus Magic Armor increased from 0.75/1.5/2.25/3 to 1/2/3/4.
   ^274*^* The bonus Armor and Magic Armor now only lasts for 3.5 seconds after entering Gargantuan Form (instead of lasting for the full duration of Gargantuan Form).
   ^274*^* The bonus Strength still persists for the full duration of Gargantuan Form.

^256Feint's Siphon^*
^947-^* Now only steals Attack Damage when used against enemy player-controlled units.

^947+^* Shadowblade had many frustration factors, and most of them stem from the long defensive bonuses from Gargantuan Form throughout the entire duration.
^947+^* Buffing the Armor & Magic Armor bonus substantially while reducing the duration means that the player only gains the mass Armor/Magic Armor bonuses for a brief duration when using Gargantuan's Blast to damage & slow enemies.
^947+^* Feint's Siphon no longer steals Attack Damage to avoid him speedfarming neutrals when he obtains a Runed Cleaver.
^947+^* Ideally, Shadowblade should be a more true jack-of-all-trades hero, so his base stats have been substantially increased to bolster his early game performance, at the cost of his scaling (and thus, his carry capability) in the mid/late game.
^947+^* Shadowblade should now perform much better as a semi-carry jack-of-all-trades hero, even in the laning phase (where his starting Attack Damage has been considerably increased, particularly in Soul's Sight form). Because of this change, he can be played as a support hero too!

_______________________________________________

^059Shellshock^*

^256Rolling Thunder^*
^947-^* Now allows you to cancel the roll by activating the ability again (without the need to have Staff of the Master).
^947-^* Allies can no longer enter Rolling Thunder if they are Stunned or Rooted.
   ^274*^* They can still enter Rolling Thunder if they are Restrained though!

_______________________________________________

^059Skrap^*

^256Summon Vorax^*
^947-^* Now gains 2 Attack Damage, 0.3 Armor and 20 Max Health per game minute elapsed when spawned.

^947+^* These changes should help Vorax maintain relevance throughout the game as time passes by.

_______________________________________________

^059Wildsoul^*

^256Bear Form^*
^947-^* Now has a cooldown of 1 second once Wildsoul finishes morphing into Bear Form (reverting forms does not trigger the cooldown).
^947-^* Now has a Mana Cost of 20.

^947+^* These changes are not intended to be nerfs, but rather to implement a mechanism to deter players from endlessly spamming the visual/sound effects.

_______________________________________________

^059Witch Slayer^*

^256Graveyard^*
^947-^* Magic Damage changed from 80/140/200/260 to 100/160/210/260.

^256Silver Bullet^*
^947-^* Magic Damage increased from 500/650/850 to 650/750/850.
^947-^* Cooldown changed from 105/80/55 seconds to 120/85/50 seconds.

^947+^* Witch Slayer is now more differentiated from Pyromancer with a much higher early game damage on Silver Bullet (at the cost of an increased cooldown).
^947+^* These changes also increase Witch Slayer's power when played as a support hero to be more comparable to other supports, while still not taking away his niche option to solo the middle lane (depending on the matchup) for a quick Portal Key to control the game's tempo.

_______________________________________________

^059Xemplar^*

^256Fractal Field^*
^947-^* Now only triggers on illusions within 900 radius of Xemplar.

^256Wall of Mirrors^*
^947-^* Magic Damage increased from 30/60/90/120 to 60/90/120/150.
^947-^* Each subsequent dash from the same cast instance after the third dash deals 0.7x Damage of the previous dash.

^256Schism^*
^947-^* For the first 5 seconds, the Schism illusions now only receive 66% of the damage they would normally receive (i.e. the same damage they would receive as if they were a regular hero).

^947+^* Arcane Bomb is intended to counter illusion heroes, but Xemplar relies on his illusions quite a bit.
^947+^* By making his Schism illusions less vulnerable to being killed right away, Xemplar should be able to output his damage more reliably before his Schism illusions die.

=^075 Items ^*=



^077New Item: Pegasus Boots^*
^947-^* Buildup: Post Haste (2500) + Recipe (2000) = 4500 Gold Total
^947-^* Has the same properties as Post Haste, with the following changes:
   ^274*^* Allows the user to also teleport to real ally heroes.
   ^274*^* Teleport target will not be disabled while the channel effect is happening.
   ^274*^* The teleport effect still cancels if the target hero dies before the channel ends.
   ^274*^* Base Movement Speed increased from 105 to 125.
   ^274*^* Max out-of-combat movement speed reduced from 45 to 35.

^947+^* This item is meant to be a luxury upgrade from Post Haste, allowing a hero to teleport to another ally hero for global ganks and pushes without relying on the presence of a creep wave or ally creep.



^077Armor of the Mad Mage (Reworked)^*
^947-^* New buildup: Luminous Prism (1500) + Helm of the Victim (900) + Recipe (800) = 3200 Gold Total
^947-^* Passive bonuses: 8 Armor, 5 Health Regeneration per second, 18 Intelligence
^947-^* Passively applies -3 Magic Armor to enemies within 900 radius.
^947-^* Active: activate to grant yourself 20 Armor that tapers off over 6 seconds.
   ^274*^* Mana Cost: 75
   ^274*^* Cooldown: 45 seconds

^947+^* Armor of the Mad Mage got its price point reduced so that it is a more viable midgame item that amplifies Magic Damage for the team while being near the middle of the teamfight.
^947+^* As a result, its benefits were also slightly nerfed from before (and also had its self-purge effect removed).
^947+^* However, its new buildup makes it easier to obtain & also grants some viable early Armor & Health Regeneration sustain. It is also no longer considered an item that uses an Activation Modifier.

_______________________________________________

Support-based Items

^077Nome's Wisdom^*
^947-^* Magic Armor granted by the aura reduced from 3 to 2.
^947-^* Armor granted by the aura reduced from 3 to 2.
^947-^* Max Shield Health reduced from 500 to 300.
^947-^* Shield duration reduced from 10 seconds to 7 seconds.
^947-^* Adrenaline no longer receives a penalty for this item's shield/heal mechanic (as the hero has been reworked such that it is unable to abuse this mechanic like before).

^947+^* Nome's Wisdom was granting a little too much value for its price point and enabled deathball compositions a little too much. The changes should make it less of an all-around good item, while ensuring that it still gives a substantial increase in Magic Armor for the team.

^077Ophelia's Pact^*
^947-^* Cost reduced from 100 Gold to 80 Gold.
^947-^* Can now be purchased at the Observatory.
^947-^* Can now be placed in a Consumable Slot.

^947-^* Now requires a 50 Gold recipe.
   ^274*^* Total Cost increased from 500 Gold to 550 Gold.
   ^274*^* Can no longer be disassembled.
   ^274*^* As a result, the Total Cost of Lex Talionis, Abyssal Skull and Nome's Wisdom have also increased by 50 Gold.

   ^274*^* Mana Regeneration bonus reduced from 150% to 125%.
^947-^* Now has Wind Whistle in its buildup instead.
^947-^* Now has a 100 Gold Recipe.
   ^274*^* Total Cost reduced from 2675 Gold to 2650 Gold.
   ^274*^* Can no longer be disassembled.

^947+^* Just a minor component change, since Stormspirit already grants increased Movement Speed.
^947+^* Wind Whistle is now a viable early game item for support heroes to take if they plan on building Stormspirit!

Lightning Item Changes

^077Thunderclaw^*
^947-^* Area Magic Damage on proc reduced from 90 to 85.

^077Hypercrown^*
^947-^* Attack Speed increased from 80 to 90.
   ^274*^* The Attack Speed for Hypercrown previously did not add up to the Attack Speed bonus granted by its parts (at the minimum). This has been corrected.
^947-^* Recipe Cost increased from 800 Gold to 1000 Gold.
   ^274*^* Total Cost increased from 5600 Gold to 5800 Gold.
^947-^* Active ability can now only target ally heroes.

^947+^* Farming items that actually have an upgrade should be expensive, as farming items are normally obtained for the purposes of farming & not to substantially increase your actual hero combat ability.
^947+^* Furthermore, the active ability was used to accelerate farm in a split pushing manner, and was just doing too much. As a result, Thunderclaw and Hypercrown have received these minor (but important) changes so that their power and intention more closely match their Gold costs.

^077Elder Parasite^*

^947+^* Elder Parasite has been changed to be slightly more appealing to pick up as a midgame item that gives a burst of Attack Speed when required.

^947+^* Whispering Helm is still way too good at enabling carries to stack easily.
^947+^* As the +10 Attack Damage came out of nowhere & Whispering Helm does not need to be any better at bolstering the hero's combat ability, it has been removed from the item.

^947+^* This change is propagated from Whispering Helm.

^077Icebrand Item Line Changes & Frostfield Plate Changes^*

^077Icebrand^*

^947-^* Movement Speed Slow increased from 25% to 30% for Melee wielders.

^077Dawnbringer^*
^947-^* Movement Speed Slow increased from 35% to 40% for Melee wielders.

^077Frostwolf's Skull^*
^947-^* No longer passively applies an Attack Speed Slow to affected enemy units.
^947-^* Active mechanics reworked: activating it once will spawn 2 Scouting Frost Wolves that seek out the nearest enemy heroes within radius, Sighting and Revealing them and applying a 20% Movement Speed Slow for 3 seconds.
   ^274*^* Radius: 5000
   ^274*^* Mana Cost: 25
   ^274*^* Cooldown: 50 seconds

^947-^* After activating it this way, can activate again within the next 50 seconds to send Dire Frost Wolves to impact nearby enemy player-controlled units, dealing 100 Magic Damage and applying a 50% Movement Speed Slow to enemies in radius for 4 seconds.
   ^274*^* Radius: 800
   ^274*^* Mana Cost: 0
   ^274*^* Cooldown: 30 seconds if the net cooldown is less than 30 seconds once the Scouting Frost Wolves have been sent out.

^947+^* Frostwolf's Skull is now more differentiated from Frostfield Plate with a different active effect, as well as having a heavier focus on Movement Speed Slow.
^947+^* Its scouting utility is a unique effect in the item pool, while still granting the same in-combat usage without directly tying it to the scouting part of the item.
^947+^* Shoutout & thanks to Whyzozerious for the amazing visual effects on the Frost Wolves!

^077Frostfield Plate^*
^947-^* Strength bonus reduced from 6 to 3.
^947-^* Agility bonus reduced from 6 to 3.
^947-^* Armor reduced from 15 to 14.
^947-^* Attack Speed Slow Aura magnitude reduced from 20% to 10%.
^947-^* Active state's Movement Speed Slow reduced from 50% to 20%.
^947-^* Active state now also applies a 30% Attack Speed Slow (stacking additively with the aura's Attack Speed Slow).

^947+^* Frostfield Plate is now much more focused on a heavy Attack Speed Slow on its active ability, at the cost of having less effective passive bonuses and a weaker Movement Speed Slow on the active state.
^947+^* As a result, Frostfield Plate's usage and purpose is now even more differentiated from Frostwolf's Skull.

^077Lightbrand Item Line Changes^*

^077Lightbrand, Frozen Light, Searing Light, Dawnbringer, Grimoire of Power^*
^947-^* Searing effect is now also applied by non-Returned, non-DoT Damage.

^077Lightbrand^*
^947-^* Now also grants 6% non-DoT Magic Damage Lifesteal (2% against non-hero units).

^077Frozen Light and Searing Light^*
^947-^* Now also grants 10% non-DoT Magic Damage Lifesteal (3% against non-hero units).

^077Dawnbringer^*
^947-^* Now also grants 15% non-DoT Magic Damage Lifesteal (5% against non-hero units).
^947-^* Searing effect's Magic Damage per second per charge increased from 16 to 18.

^077Grimoire of Power^*
^947-^* Recipe Cost reduced from 800 Gold to 700 Gold.
   ^274*^* Total Cost reduced from 5100 Gold to 5000 Gold.
^947-^* Magic Damage Lifesteal no longer applies on Magic DoT effects.
^947-^* Non-DoT Magic Damage Lifesteal increased from 15% to 20% (7% against non-hero units).
^947-^* Ravenor no longer receives a penalty for this item's non-DoT Magic Damage Lifesteal.
^947-^* Active effect completely reworked:
   ^274*^* Activate to instantly dispel Debuffs and Stuns on self, and grant 100% Debuff and Stun Duration reduction for the next 1 second. Note that this will not protect you from continuous area effects (such as Chronos' Chronofield) or blue-bordered states like Succubus' Hold.
   ^274*^* Grimoire of Power can be activated even while Stunned or Perplexed.
   ^274*^* Cooldown: 70 seconds
   ^274*^* Mana Cost: 100


^947+^* Grimore of Power's previous active effect (increased Magic Lifesteal) was too specific to heroes & it was hard to take advantage of that mechanic if you enter a battle at close to full HP & you need to use your important spells as soon as possible.
^947+^* The item's active effect has been completely reworked to be more suitable for heroes that want to ensure their important spells/item usage go off even if they are caught by surprise (so long as they are not bursted down before having a chance to react).
^947+^* This new effect is powerful enough such that it is generally usable on all heroes, but will benefit heroes with key abilities & items more often. The duration is short enough such that there is counterplay.
^947+^* Finally, the Lightbrand item line now only works on non-DoT Magic Damage sources. This is due to some effects (like Mock of Brilliance) synergizing too well with a cheap item like Lightbrand.

==^960 Design ^*==



=^075 Hero Guides (NAEU/International Client Only) ^*=

Every single default hero guide in-game has been updated with `WhatYouGot's hero guides! Thank you for providing a much-needed update for HoN's hero guides.

^947+^* `WhatYouGot is a competitive-level HoN player, Twitch Streamer & Caster/Commentator for the recent HoN Tournaments & was also a competitive HoN player for Complexity Gaming back in 2015.

^947+^* These updated hero guides are meant to act as a reliable base reference for new players and average players. As players get more experienced, they should adapt their skill builds & item choices to their current game as needed.

^947+^* All of the old default hero guides written by Zlapped still exist, just not as the current default guides anymore. Zlapped's guides were solid in the past, but most have been outdated & been replaced with more up-to-date guides for today's HoN meta.



=^075 Hero Rework - Qi ^*=



^059Qi (Reworked)^*

^947+^* The premise of Qi's Enlightenment dealing damage based on the target enemy's Attack Damage is fundamentally flawed on its own. Heroes naturally acquire Attack Damage throughout the game even if they don't have a single item (due to their primary attribute stat gains as they level up). Most heroes also tend to buy items that augment their primary attribute. The combination of these factors means that Qi's bonus damage output based on the target's Attack Damage will always be too weak or too strong against unfarmed or farmed heroes, or unable to be fairly manifested against the intended spectrum of heroes (farmed heroes with high autoattack damage output over time).

^947+^* A more accurate & fair representation of an enemy's autoattack damage over time has been implemented for Qi's Enlightenment ability. This can simply be expressed as "Extra Autoattack DPS" (Damage per Second), and is the difference between the target's total autoattack DPS and their base autaottack DPS.

^947+^* Mathematically: Extra_Autoattack_DPS = Total_Autoattack_DPS - Base_Autoattack_DPS

^947+^* As a result, Qi now properly has a niche role in obliterating farmed autoattack enemy carries, while dealing below-average damage to underfarmed support heroes and not totally obliterating heroes in between that spectrum.

^947+^* Qi's scaling is now much more dependent on his enemy's scaling as a result, so Qi's overall kit and numbers have been tailored towards being a Support Hero.

_______________________________________________

^256Crippling Puncture^*
^947-^* Cooldown increased from 12 seconds to 24/20/16/12 seconds.
^947-^* Movement Speed Slow and Disarm duration increased from 1/2/3/4 seconds to 1.6/2.4/3.2/4 seconds.
^947-^* SuperiorMagic Damage changed from 100/120/140/160 to 90/160/180/200.

^256A Thousand Cuts^*
^947-^* No longer applies an instance of non-Damage-over-Time (DoT) Ability Damage while the Spirit Shredder is travelling.
^947-^* The Spirit Shredder now has its DoT effect active starting from its initial launch (rather than only after reaching its initial destination).
^947-^* Initial Mana Cost changed from 90/100/110/120 to 100.
^947-^* Upkeep Mana Cost per second changed from 3/6/9/12 to (5/6/7/8 + 0.6/0.8/1/1.2% of your Max Mana).
^947-^* Magic Damage per second increased from 30/50/70/90 to 45/70/95/120.

^947+^* A Thousand Cuts no longer deals an instance of non-DoT Ability Damage, as it was proven to provide too much burst damage with Enlightenment. It now only deals DoT damage to act as a zoning tool.

^256Enlightenment (Reworked)^*
^947-^* This ability has 5 levels & starts at level 1 at the start of the game.
^947-^* Passively deals 20/35/50/65/80% of an enemy hero's Extra Autoattack Damage per Second (DPS) as SuperiorMagic Damage for each instance of Ability Damage dealt to them. Extra Autoattack DPS is the difference between the target's Total Autoattack DPS and their Base Autoattack DPS.
   ^274*^* Damage over Time effects apply the correct amount of bonus damage per game frame (0.05 seconds).
^947-^* Staff of the Master: Qi's autoattacks bleed the enemy's Chi, dealing 1 SuperiorMagic Damage per second for 2 seconds. This counts as Ability Damage.

^947-^* Active: Target a position to reposition the Spirit Shredder from A Thousand Cuts (if it is out).
   ^274*^* Range: 700
   ^274*^* Cooldown: 4 seconds
   ^274*^* Mana Cost: 50

^256Ancestral Assault^*
^947-^* Mana Cost changed from 150/200/250 to 100/175/250.
^947-^* Number of instances reduced from 3/4/5 to 1/2/3.
^947-^* Impact interval between each instance increased from 0.1 seconds to 0.3 seconds.
^947-^* SuperiorMagic Damage changed from (20 + 60/67.5/75% of the target's Attack Damage) to 125.
^947-^* Now also applies a 1 second Disarm and Perplex to affected enemies upon impact.
   ^274*^* Note that Enlightenment is procced from Ancestral Assault's damage instances now.
^947-^* Damage no longer tapers based on the target's distance from your original cast location.

_______________________________________________



=^075 Hero Rework - Drunken Master ^*=



^947+^* Drunken Master's ability usage in teamfights was too linear and 1-dimensional.

^947+^* This rework aims to fix those issues by introducing factors of decision-making back to Drunken Master, as well as improving his ebb and flow in actual combat.

^947+^* Healthy elements of the legacy Drink ability has been added back to Drunken Master to make this happen!

_______________________________________________

^059Drunken Master (Reworked)^*

^256Lunge^*
^947-^* Movement Speed Slow reduced from 20/30/40/50% to 15/20/25/30%.
^947-^* Cooldown changed from 14/12/10/8 seconds to 13/12/11/10 seconds.

^256Stagger^*
^947-^* Cooldown is now reduced to 0.8 seconds when Lunge is finished (instead of being refreshed when Lunge is first cast).

^075Ability 3: Drink (Reworked)^*
   ^274*^* Mana Cost: 50
   ^274*^* Cooldown: 20/16/12/8 seconds
   ^274*^* Channel Time: 2 seconds
^947-^* Channel to Drink, gaining 2/3/4/5 Drunk Charges and reducing the cooldown of Lunge and Stagger by 0.25 seconds per 0.25 seconds of channeling.
   ^274*^* Max of 16/24/32/40 Drunk Charges.
^947-^* Each Drunk Charge grants 1% Evasion and 1% Stun/Debuff Duration Reduction.
^947-^* Each Drunk Charge expires naturally every 2 seconds, healing you for 5 Health.
^947-^* When Lunge is used, consumes 6 Drunk Charges to add 20% Movement Speed Slow.
^947-^* When Stagger is used, consumes 4 Drunk Charges to add +30 Movement Speed.



^947+^* Using Drink in combat sacrifices immediate attack-time-on-target and mobility for reduced cooldowns on Lunge and Stagger (i.e. increased mobility and damage after a short time). It also grants Drunken Master defensive and mobility bonuses, as Lunge and Stagger now consume Drunk charges.

^947+^* To make Drink more interesting, Drink now has a low cooldown when the ability is maxed, allowing Drunken Master to consistently upkeep the Drunk charges in and out of combat.

^947+^* Much like the first iteration of Drink, Drunken Master has more resilience during the laning phase with the healing over time from Drink.

^947+^* Additionally, there is a slight delay before Stagger comes off cooldown after the Stagger-Lunge combo, allowing enemies to have a slightly larger time window for counterplay to 3 Point Strike.

Penalties will not include the action of muting players (due to multiple complaints about lack of necessary communication in the picking phase).
   ^274*^* Note that everything else in the RAP System for the NAEU/International Client remains the same.



=^075 General ^*=

^947-^* AFK Timer duration increased from 2.5 minutes to 3 minutes.



=^075 Forests of Caldavar ("New") ^*=

^947-^* The warding high cliff tower near the bottom rune on the Legion jungle side has been shifted very slightly to the southeast (makes ward vision of the Legion camps more consistent).
^947-^* The bottom rune has been shifted slightly towards the southeast in accordance with the previous change.
^947-^* The top-left corner of the map has been filled in with some untraversable lava (fixes some pathing issues around that area).



=^075 Mid Wars ^*=

^947-^* Merrick's Rune now spawns close to the first set of ramps southeast of the middle area of the river.
   ^274*^* This should allow Merrick's Rune to be more commonly contested compared to when it spawned at the boss lair.

^947-^* Backdoor Protection damage mitigation against heroes increased from 50% to 60%.
^947-^* Token of Sight now regenerates 15% Max Health and 15% Max Mana over 5 seconds for allies within 900 radius upon being obtained.
   ^274*^* This regeneration effect can stack up to 2 times.
   ^274*^* Dispels upon taking damage.

^947-^* Respawn time added per hero level increased from 0.5 seconds to 0.6 seconds.
^947-^* Respawn time added per missing ally non-base towers reduced from 3.75 seconds to 3.5 seconds.
   ^274*^* Overall, respawn time at level 25 increased from 12.5/16.25/20 seconds to 15/18.5/22 seconds.

^947+^* This adjustment should make early game deaths feel somewhat impactful again, while still increasing respawn times slightly later on.



=^075 Neutral Creeps ^*=

^947-^* Medium Camp: total Experience per camp reduced from 185 to 170.
^947-^* Hard Camp: total Experience per camp reduced from 215 to 205.

^947+^* The jungle changes allowed junglers to level up slightly too fast from the previous patch, so this should slow down their progression slightly.

The Experience changes to each individual neutral creep are as follows:
   ^274*^* Vagabond Assassin: 25 → 20 (this also reduces the Easy camp's experience containing the Vagabond Assassin to 140, which makes camp's Experience equal to every other Easy camp)

   ^274*^* Ebula: 55 → 50
   ^274*^* Pustula: 75 → 70
   ^274*^* Fire Ogre: 60 → 55
   ^274*^* Ice Ogre 65 → 60
   ^274*^* Alchemist Ogre: 60 → 55
   ^274*^* Wild Hunter: 93 → 85
   ^274*^* Wolf: 55 → 50
   ^274*^* Wolf Commander: 75 → 70
   ^274*^* Catman Soldier: 100 → 95
   ^274*^* Catman Champion: 115 → 110
   ^274*^* Goatar: 55 → 50
   ^274*^* Vulture Lord: 135 → 125
   ^274*^* Skeleton King: 135 → 125
   ^274*^* Vagabond Leader: 130 → 125

==^960 Forests of Caldavar ("New") ^*==

The main goal of the changes to the "new" Forests of Caldavar map is to make the viability of each laning setup choice to be more on par with one another, while retaining their own unique benefits and disadvantages.

^947-^* Legion bottom lane and Hellbourne top lane trees were rearranged significantly.
^947-^* Legion's southeastern most pull camp: a tree to the left of the camp (and to the right of the Observatory) has been removed to allow heroes to move through the nearby path.

^947+^* There were far too many spots near the lane that made it nearly impossible to find a hero unless you were right next to the hero (and even then, sometimes it was nearly impossible to do so)., which led to unhealthy play patterns by solo unsafe lane heroes.
^947+^* These changes address that issue, while still allowing for some niche hiding spots to be discovered. While this may appear to be a nerf to solo unsafe lane heroes, the jungle changes will more than compensate for that.



=^075 Jungle Changes ^*=

Hellbourne bottom lane - Medium pull camp (unsafe lane)
^947-^* Camp is now more accessible to the unsafe lane to be pulled into the lane
^947-^* Trees in this area now allow for better vision from the unsafe lane, while the vision of that camp ends up being more obscured for the safe lane
^947-^* Also reworked tree patch in this area to more closely mirror the Hellbourne side of the map.

Legion Ancients camp
^947-^* Now spawns a Hard neutral camp instead of an Ancients neutral camp.
^947-^* The other central Hard neutral camp has been replaced with a Medium neutral camp.
^947-^* A few trees have been removed to allow for more visibility & to allow for a slightly easier double stack.

Legion left-most Medium & Hard neutral camps
^947-^* Camps were shifted slightly towards the bottom-right.
^947-^* Leash range of these camps have been reverted to normal.
^947-^* Path between both camps are now more cleared of obstructions.
^947-^* Distance between both camps are now more similar to the Hellbourne equivalent (top-right-most) camps.
   ^274*^* These changes prevent the camps from being pulled towards the Legion middle lane, while simultaneously solving the difficulties of being difficult to double-stack these camps (due to a prior temporary fix).

Legion top lane - Medium pull camp (unsafe lane)
^947-^* Camp is now more accessible to the unsafe lane to be pulled into the lane.
^947-^* Trees in this area now allow for better vision from the unsafe lane, while the vision of that camp ends up being more obscured for the safe lane.

Hellbourne Ancients camp
^947-^* Now spawns a Hard neutral camp instead of an Ancients neutral camp.
^947-^* The other central Hard neutral camp has been replaced with a Medium neutral camp.
   ^274*^* This camp has also been rotated to face the new Hard camp more directly.

Hellbourne top-right-most Hard camp
^947-^* This neutral camp was shifted slightly downward.
^947-^* Leash range of the camp has been reverted to normal.
^947-^* This prevents the camps from being pulled towards the Hellbourne top lane.



^947+^* Most of these jungle changes were done to balance the jungles between the Legion side and Hellbourne side, as well as make the top and bottom lanes slightly more equal in terms of the safe and unsafe lanes.

^947+^* Overall, these changes (coupled with other changes in previous patches like the Merrick's Rune change) should make laning setup choices more balanced with regards to one another (any permutation/combination of 2-1-2 vs. 1-1-2-jungle and other dynamic variants involving partial/full roamers).

_______________________________________________



=^075 Neutral Creep Changes ^*=

^059Dragon^*
^947-^* Gold Bounty: 28-36 -> 35-45
^947-^* Physical Armor: 2-> 3
^947-^* Attack Damage: 55-> 60

^059Dragon Master^*
^947-^* Gold Bounty: 100-120 -> 130-145
^947-^* Magic Armor: 12 -> 10

^059Dreadbeetle^*
^947-^* Gold Bounty: 44-52 -> 55-65
^947-^* Physical Armor: 3 -> 6
^947-^* Base Attack Time: 1.4 -> 1.3

^059Dreadbeetle Queen^*
^947-^* Gold Bounty:  74-82 -> 90-105
^947-^* Physical Armor: 6 -> 10
^947-^* Base Attack Time: 1.4 -> 1.3

^059Predasaur^*
^947-^* Gold Bounty: 48-56 -> 60-70
^947-^* Physical Armor: 3.5 -> 6
^947-^* Max Health: 900 -> 1000

^059Predasaur Crusher^*
^947-^* Gold Bounty: 58-66 -> 70-85



^947+^* The previous Gold Bounty nerfs have been reverted because there will now only be 1 Ancient Camp in the "new" Forests of Caldavar map.
^947+^* Some stat adjustments were also made to make the camps more comparable to one another in terms of power, while still retaining their unique strengths and weaknesses.

_______________________________________________



=^075 Mid Wars Changes ^*=

The main goal of these changes to Mid Wars is to resolve some common frustrations encountered by players in this mode, while slowly leaning towards rewarding properly applied early/mid game aggressive plays & pushes.



^947-^* Zorgath and Transmutanstein return to Mid Wars!
   ^274*^* Say goodbye to Snotter Boss until next time!

^947-^* Matchmaking constraints for Mid Wars are slightly less relaxed, which should result in increasing the chances of getting a more enjoyable match.

^947-^* Earliest concede time availability reduced from 10 minutes to 5 minutes.
   ^274*^* This vote still requires 5 votes from a single team to actually pass (at least until 15 minutes where the usual reduction in required votes occurs).



^947-^* Backdoor Protection is now applied to the Melee and Ranged Barracks as well, and grants the Barracks 50% Damage Reduction against non-hero units while active (Main Base structure has 50% Damage Reduction against non-hero units by default already).
   ^274*^* Additionally, the Backdoor Protection state on the Main Base Structure and Barracks now also grants 50% Damage Reduction against heroes.

^947+^* This change should help slow down uncoordinated & uncommitted attempts to end the game by backdooring to the point where the defending team can react and take action before the Main Base Structure falls.
^947+^* The intention is not to remove backdooring (as it has its place to end the game when there are long stalemates for long periods of time), but to make it more of a committal and coordinated strategy to execute & introduce more opportunities to counterplay it. We understand that games ended abruptly without much means for counterplay makes for an anti-climatic ending, but having backdooring as an alternate game-ending option for long games is still necessary if the circumstances make it viable.



^947-^* Main Base Structure's Health Regeneration per second reduced from 50 to 20 (when not damaged by an enemy within the last 10 seconds).

^947+^* This change partially addresses the frustration of getting the base to a low Health value, only to see it regenerate to full Health too quickly.


^947-^* Neutral camp composition has been changed (no longer spawns the traditional Hard neutral camps).
^947-^* Instead, now spawns 1 of the following camps:
   ^274*^* Dragon Master, Skeleton King, Vagabond Leader, Ice Ogre
   ^274*^* Predasaur Crusher, Vulture Lord, Minotaur, Wolf Commander
   ^274*^* Dreadbeetle Queen, Catman Champion, Vulture Lord, Wild Hunter

^947+^* This change provides minimal disruption to the current map, while making the Gold & Experience value of the neutral camp more suitable for the mid & late game (rather than something that can be tackled in the early game by 1-2 heroes) and simultaneously discourages raw jungling early on (to keep the spirit of the map's name - Mid Wars).
^947+^* Furthermore, this change allows Ophelia and Parasite to make use of Ancient Creeps if they have Staff of the Master.
^947+^* This also allows these heroes more variety of creeps they can take control of in the early game, though the Ancient creep has to be cleared (or the camp needs to be stacked) for them to spawn more neutral creeps they can take control of in the early game.



^947-^* Respawn time equation has been changed.
   ^274*^* Old equation: Respawn Time (s) = 0.6 * Hero_Level
   ^274*^* New equation: Respawn Time (s) = (0.5 * Hero_Level) + 3.75 * (2 - Ally_Nonbase_Towers_Remaining)

^947+^* The new respawn times allow for slightly faster early game respawns, while extending the respawn times by 3.75 seconds for each of your ally non-base towers that have been destroyed.
^947+^* This gives pushing some tangible benefit in Mid Wars and disfavours turtling, without making early/mid game strategies too snowbally in nature.
^947+^* Overall, this increases the respawn time at level 25 from 17 seconds (in all situations) to 12.5/16.25/20 seconds when 0/1/2 of your non-base towers are down. Further adjustments may be made in the future if this does not prove to be enough of a change.

_______________________________________________



==^960 Design ^*==

^947-^* Max Disconnection Time reduced from 5 minutes to 4 minutes.
^947-^* AFK Timer duration reduced from 4 minutes to 2.5 minutes.
   ^274*^* AFK Warning time reduced from 3 minutes to 1.5 minutes.

^947+^* While we understand that real-life situations & technical difficulties can happen once in a blue moon, they should not be happening frequently in any capacity.

^947+^* Players should be active in the game for as much time as possible without disrupting the gameplay for others, and these changes help encourage players to make sure that they exhibit due diligence on their part to do so.



Fortification of Sol / Demonic Fortification
^947-^* Now goes on a 90 second cooldown when the game starts.
   ^274*^* Prevents players from accidentally triggering it at the start of the game while typing & transitioning from the hero pick lobby to the in-game map.

^059Doctor Repulsor^*
^947-^* Starting Attack Damage changed from 47-57 to 50-54.
^947-^* Attack action time reduced from 500ms to 350ms.

^256Magnetic Contraption^*
^947-^* Cooldown increased from 4 seconds to 10/8/6/4 seconds.
^947-^* Cast backswing reduced from 400ms to 300ms.

^256Opposite Charges^*
^947-^* Mana Cost reduced from 100 to 70/80/90/100.
^947-^* Duration reduced from 1/1.5/2/2.5 seconds to 1/1.4/1.8/2.2 seconds.
^947-^* No longer applies a Movement Speed Slow to self upon use.

^256Electric Frenzy^*
^947-^* Now also resets your autoattack cooldown upon using an ability.
^947-^* Magic Damage increased from 30/50/70/90 to 40/60/80/100.
^947-^* Now grants +50 Attack Speed for the boosted attack (helps make this one attack launch faster).

^256Ludicrous Speed^*
^947-^* No longer resets your autoattack cooldown upon use (since Electric Frenzy does that now).
^947-^* No longer Stunned while travelling.
^947-^* Instead, Doctor Repulsor is Perplexed (unable to use items) while travelling.
   ^274*^* This means that Doctor Repulsor can autoattack & cast spells while Ludicrous Speed is active!
   ^274*^* The Perplexed state remains on Doctor Repulsor for 0.8 seconds after exiting Ludicrous Speed.
^947-^* Magic Damage dealt per 100 units travelled reduced from 15/20/25 to 6/12/18.
^947-^* Max Magic Damage dealt per instance reduced from 300/400/500 to 200/300/400.
^947-^* Cooldown increased from 0.3 seconds to 0.4 seconds.



^947+^* Doctor Repulsor has an odd power curve due to the nature of Ludicrous Speed. His laning presence has been virtually non-existent because of his long range ganking potential at level 6, so some raw damage was taken away from Ludicrous Speed, while giving him much better tools for laning & the early game in general.

^947+^* These changes do weaken the raw damage output through his ultimate alone, but the autoattack reset mechanic on Electric Frenzy opens up new combo routes & makes micromanaging his mana pool for Ludicrous Speed even more important. Player mechanical skill in terms of mouse speed & accuracy, cancelling the cast backswing of his abilities, as well as taking advantage of some returning mechanics on Ludicrous Speed will optimize the effectiveness of Doctor Repulsor, while simultaneously retaining options for counterplay (e.g. the Perplex state on Doctor Repulsor means item usage is not immediately available to him after exiting Ludicrous Speed).

^947+^* Overall, these changes favour Doctor Repulsor more in terms of getting that long range ganking power spike at level 6 (which is his unique strength compared to every other hero), without frontloading the power on Ludicrous Speed itself. The changes make him less effective when played as a raw carry if relying on the damage from Ludicrous Speed, and more of a core hero that focuses on capitalizing on the uniqueness of his ganker-carry role and playstyle.

^947-^* Can no longer be dropped or shared to other units (to prevent it from taking up space in an ally hero's inventory without them being able to get rid of it).
   ^274*^* You can still remove it from your inventory without using the item by selling it for 0 Gold, if you wish to do so.

^947-^* Silver Coins for playing 10/20 games of Forests of Caldavar & Mid Wars: disabled since it did not end up in the live client.
^947-^* Silver Coin discount in the store: disabled since it did not end up working in the live client.

^947+^* We deeply apologize for the inconvenience.

==^960 Design ^*==



=^075 Hero Rework - Nitro ^*=



^059Nitro (Reworked)^*

^947+^* Nitro's fire-while-moving mechanic as a primary mechanic is conceptually broken/imbalanced and frustrating to deal with in this game. This concept simply does not work in HoN in a fair manner.
^947+^* This results in making her high-highs too distinct from the other levels of performance, and also drastically punishes the average player who plays her while disproportionately rewarding veteran Nitro players.
^947+^* Additionally, she was statistically impossible to balance, both in terms of power, performance and frustration levels.
^947+^* The rework maintains her hit-and-run mechanic and makes her kit much more fun overall, but allows both the Nitro player and other players in the game to interact with her more fairly.
^947+^* She even gets to keep a fun & balanced version of her fire-while-moving mechanic on her ultimate as a nice bonus!



^947-^* Now has default auto-attack behaviour.

_______________________

Stats

Strength: 18 + 1.5 per level
Agility: 22 + 3.5 per level (Primary)
Intelligence: 18 + 2.2 per level

Attack Range: 600
Movement Speed: 290
Starting Armor: 2.08
Starting Attack Damage: 40-47

_______________________

^075Ability 1: Ballistic (Reworked)^*
   ^274*^* Range: 600
   ^274*^* Mana Cost: 10
   ^274*^* Cooldown: 3/2/1/0 seconds
^947-^* Causes attacks to spawn 3 shrapnel behind the target for up to 8 seconds. Shrapnel explodes when touched by an enemy unit, dealing 10/16/22/28 Magic Damage.
   ^274*^* Right-click to enable auto-casting of this skill.
   ^274*^* Max of 21 shrapnel can be out at once.

 

^075Ability 2: Blast Off (Reworked)^*
   ^274*^* Mana Cost: 110/120/130/140
   ^274*^* Cooldown: 15 seconds

^947-^* Target a cone area to blast all enemy units backwards with an explosive shell over 1 second, Stunning & displacing them 600 units away from Nitro, dealing 70/140/210/280 Magic Damage and applying a 25% Movement Speed Slow for 1/1.5/2/2.5 seconds after.



^075Ability 3: Three’s a Crowd (Reworked)^*
   ^274*^* Mana Cost: 50
   ^274*^* Cooldown: 16 seconds

^947-^* Passive: Nitro gains 5/10/15/20 Movement Speed.
^947-^* Active: for 4 seconds, the Movement Speed bonus is increased to 20/40/60/80 (0.5x if there are enemies within 300 radius of Nitro).

 

^075Ability 4: Overdrive (Reworked)^*
   ^274*^* Mana Cost: 100/125/150
   ^274*^* Cooldown: 100/80/60 seconds

^947-^* Target an enemy to focus on them for up to 5 seconds.
^947-^* While focused, you will continuously attack the target with 400 bonus Attack Speed, even while moving, but deal 65/55/45% less Damage to it.
   ^274*^* Activate the ability again during the effect to stop shooting the target.

=^075 User Interface ^*=

^947-^* Account Icon is now approximately 50% larger in a user's statistics page and no longer has an unnecessary border around it (new User Interface).



=^075 Music ^*=

^947-^* Lobby soundtrack has been updated to the Arcadian theme.
^947-^* Main (out-of-game) soundtrack has been updated to the Apollo theme.



=^075 General ^*=

Ranked Pick
^947-^* Now has a 5 second delay when transitioning from the hero banning phase to the first hero pick of the game.
^947-^* Max number of hero bans that can take place at the end of the banning phase increased from 4 to 6.



=^075 Neutrals ^*=

^059Fire Ogre^*
^947-^* Max Mana increased from 0 to 200.
^947-^* Now has an active ability: Immolating Flames
   ^274*^* Mana Cost: 100
   ^274*^* Cooldown: 30 seconds
   ^274*^* Range: 800
   ^274*^* Target an ally unit to apply Immolating Flames to it for 30 seconds. Immolating Flames deals 20 Magic Damage per second in a 200 radius around the affected target.
   ^274*^* Fire Ogre will not use this ability if it is not under a player's control.



=^075 Runes ^*=

^077New Rune: Merrick's Rune^*
^947-^* Replaces Refreshment Rune in the game.
^947-^* Restores 135 Health and 80 Mana over 3 seconds and is dispelled on damage (same as a single Bottle use).
^947-^* Grants 35 Gold, plus 2 Gold per minute of elapsed game time (excluding the pregame time).
^947-^* When bottled, it refills your Bottle to full (3/3), behaving the same as a regular rune.
   ^274*^* Previously filled to 2/3 when it was Refreshment Rune.
^947-^* If targeted via Ioyn Stone, the rune will be consumed and gives Ioyn Stone 2 charges (the same as before with Refreshment Rune).
   ^274*^* Gives the user of Ioyn Stone the gold benefits of the rune immediately when this occurs. The gold benefits do not transfer to the Ioyn Stone charges.

^947+^* Merrick's Rune is intended to replace Refreshment Rune. It solves the feeling of utter disappointment, as the player who gets Merrick's Rune will have much more of a compensation if they do not end up obtaining the primary rune that spawns.



=^075 Mid Wars ^*=

^947-^* Snotter Boss has returned to Newerth to replace Transmutanstein & Zorgath (for now)!
^947-^* Merrick's Rune now spawns at the top of the river and floats down towards the boss lair.
   ^274*^*  This occurs every 30 seconds after creeps spawn, starting from 0:15.
^947-^* MMR for Mid Wars: only your own MMR is now visible in the hero pick lobby.

^947+^* Mid Wars is not a game mode where we want players to feel like they are under pressure and/or judge others based on their MMR. We simply want them to have fun & enjoy the game!
^947+^* For the players who use their Mid Wars MMR as a measure of personal progression, your own MMR is still visible only to yourself.

^059Ravenor^*

^256Storm Blades^*

^256Static Shock^*
^947-^* Number of autoattacks required to proc reduced from 3 to 2.
^947-^* Max number of side targets affected by Static Shock reduced from 3 to 1.
   ^274*^* Total max number of targets affected by Static Shock reduced from 4 to 2.

^947+^* Making Static Shock proc more frequently should be an overall improvement to Power Overwhelming's charge buildup rate without it getting too out of hand.

_______________________________________________

^059Scout^*

^256Disarm^*
^947-^* State duration reduced from 2.5 seconds to 2 seconds.

_______________________________________________

^059Shadowblade^*

^256Gargantuan's Blast^*
^947-^* Mana Cost changed from 90 to 75/85/95/105.
^947-^* Magic Damage reduced from 60/120/180/240 to 55/110/165/220.
^947-^* Now suffers a 20% Damage Penalty and -15% Movement Speed while this form is active.

^256Feint's Siphon^*
^947-^* Mana Cost changed from 90 to 75/85/95/105.
^947-^* Magic Damage reduced from 60/120/180/240 to 55/110/165/220.
^947-^* Now receives an additional 8% Damage while this form is active.

^947+^* Shadowblade's kit has a little too much early game power and survivability in high level play, making his snowball a little too difficult to deal with.

^947+^* Additionally, the Gargantuan Form is still overly strong compared to the other forms, as maintaining the benefits of the base form while also having such a good early game survivability tool that scales well into the game was a bit much. The intention of the Gargantuan Form is durability first & foremost, and not to grant Shadowblade high damage upkeep while doing so.

^947+^* Feint Form was also a little bit too good against enemy carries. In general, Gargantuan Form and Feint Form now have small downsides to them so the player thinks a little bit more about their form shifting usage.

_______________________________________________



=^075 Items ^*=

 

^077New Item: Spyglass^*
^947-^* Given to all players at the start of the game.
^947-^* Activate to bring up the targeting cursor.
   ^274*^* Using Spyglass will transform it into harmless confetti at the target location.
   ^274*^* Sells for 0 Gold (can be sold though).
   ^274*^* Cannot be dropped.
   ^274*^* Expires after 90 seconds.

^947+^* This item solely exists for the select few players who are experiencing the AoE indicator lag, as there has not been a common factor among the tiny fraction of players that experience this issue & nobody has been able to pinpoint what causes this issue.
^947+^* Previously known as one of the most useless items in HoN's history, Spyglass makes its way back into Newerth and finally becomes useful to a small subset of players!

_______________________________________________

^077Jade Spire^*
^947-^* Bonus Max Mana reduced from 400 to 300.
^947-^* Bonus Mana Regeneration reduced from 150% to 115%.
^947-^* Mid Wars only change: bonus Range granted to abilities increased from 100 to 150.

^947+^* Jade Spire was giving way too much additional benefits upon completion for only 600 Gold. Toning down these benefits makes the item more in line with the benefits other items receive upon their completion.

_______________________________________________

^077Orb of Zamos^*
^947-^* New recipe: Minor Totem (50) + Recipe (250) = 300 Gold Total (Total Cost unchanged from before)
^947-^* Bonus Strength, Agility and Intelligence reduced from 2 to 1.
^947-^* Gold bonus for creep kills is now halved if the distance between the linked heroes is greater than 1500.

^077Grave Locket^*
^947-^* Now requires a 25 Gold Recipe.
   ^274*^* Total Cost increased from 775 Gold to 800 Gold.
^947-^* Bonus Agility reduced from 5 to 2.
^947-^* Gold bonus for creep kills is now halved if the distance between the linked heroes is greater than 1500.
^947-^* Bonus Experience granted to the linked hero per enemy creep kill reduced from 15 to 10.
^947-^* Now only grants Bonus Experience to the linked hero for an enemy creep kill if the linked hero is not within the experience range of the creep kill.

^947+^* These changes should help reduce the performance gap between solo laners in the unsafe lane ("suicide lane heroes") and the support hero of a dual lane.

^947+^* Furthermore, both of these items were a little too efficient. They should now be considered less of a "must-buy" item as a laning support.

^947+^* In particular, Grave Locket was literally generating free experience if both laning heroes are in lane and killing creeps. This issue has been remedied with this change.

_______________________________________________

^077Refreshing Ornament^*
^947-^* New recipe: Trinket of Restoration (350) + Minor Totem (50) + Recipe (150) = 550 Gold Total
^947-^* Bonus Agility reduced from 5 to 2.

^077Astrolabe^*
^947-^* Bonus Agility reduced from 8 to 5.
^947-^* Recipe cost increased from 400 to 510.
   ^274*^* Total cost increased from 1440 to 1550.

^947+^* Agility carry heroes who picked up Refreshing Ornament for an efficient laning item that also granted them Agility, while upgrading the item into Astrolabe as an extremely cost-efficient survivability item that also granted them Agility was way too powerful.

^947+^* Changing the components closer to how they were in the past should remedy this issue by quite a bit. Astrolabe was also too cost-efficient for what it did, so a slight cost nerf is also warranted.

_______________________________________________

^077Barrier Idol^*
^947-^* State duration increased from 8 seconds to 10 seconds.
^947-^* State duration is now doubled for non-hero units.

^077Nome's Wisdom^*
^947-^* Bonus Magic Armor from the aura increased from 2 to 3.
^947-^* Component priority changed from "Mystic Vestments -> Ring of the Teacher -> Manatube -> Recipe" to "Ring of the Teacher -> Manatube -> Mystic Vestments -> Recipe".

^947+^* Magic Armor items for pushing and teamfights were a little lacking. These changes give them a little bit more utility and flexibility for their intended function.

_______________________________________________

^077Spellshards (Reworked)^*
^947-^* Component Buildup: Perpetual Cogwheel (1325) + Recipe (825) = 2150 Gold Total.
^947-^* Can be upgraded up to 2 times (max of 3 total levels).
   ^274*^* Total Gold Cost is therefore 2150/2975/3800 Gold.
^947-^* Passively grants 150/175/200% Mana Regeneration and 5/10/15 Intelligence.
^947-^* Passively grants +10/15/20% Spell Damage against non-hero units.
^947-^* Passively grants 30/45/60% Magic Armor Penetration.

^947+^* This rework to Spellshards makes it less universally amazing for all heroes, while also properly gating its power behind a reasonable price point.
_______________________________________________

^077Ringmail^*
^947-^* Armor reduced from 5 to 4.
^947-^* Cost reduced from 550 to 450.

^077Lex Talionis^*
^947-^* Recipe Cost increased from 100 Gold to 200 Gold.
   ^274*^* Total Cost remains unchanged from before (1150 Gold).
^947-^* Bonus Armor reduced from 6 to 5.
^947-^* Armor addition/reduction when applied to an ally/enemy reduced from 6 to 5, respectively.
   ^274*^* Armor reduction on self when this occurs is also reduced from 6 to 5.

^077Sol's Bulwark^*
^947-^* Recipe Cost increased from 1000 Gold to 1050 Gold.
   ^274*^* Total Cost reduced from 1550 Gold to 1500 Gold 
   ^274*^* This is due to the reduction of cost in Ringmail.

^077Daemonic Breastplate^*
^947-^* Total Cost reduced from 5250 Gold to 5200 Gold.
   ^274*^* This is due to the reduction of cost from both Ringmail and Sol's Bulwark.

^077Ultor's Heavy Helm^*
^947-^* Recipe cost increased from 1000 Gold to 1025 Gold.
^947-^* Total cost reduced from 3725 Gold to 3650 Gold.
   ^274*^* This is due to the reduction of cost from Ringmail.

^077Shield of the Five^*
^947-^* Recipe Cost increased from 200 Gold to 300 Gold.
   ^274*^* Total Cost remains unchanged from before (800 Gold).
^947-^* Passive effect changes: Armor reduced from 5 to 4.
^947-^* Active effect changes:
   ^274*^* Cooldown increased from 30 seconds to 60 seconds.
   ^274*^* Duration reduced from 60 seconds to 30 seconds.
   ^274*^* Effects are no longer doubled for the first 10 seconds of the state duration.
   ^274*^* Armor granted to ally units and structures reduced from 2 to 1.

^077Plated Greaves^*
^947-^* Passive effect changes: Armor reduced from 7 to 6.
^947-^* Active effect changes:
   ^274*^* Cooldown increased from 30 seconds to 60 seconds.
   ^274*^* Duration reduced from 60 seconds to 30 seconds.
   ^274*^* Effects are no longer doubled for the first 10 seconds of the state duration.
   ^274*^* Armor granted to allied heroes and structures reduced from 2 to 1.5.
   ^274*^* Ally non-hero units now also receive the Armor bonus.

^947+^* These Armor items were a little too efficient early on, and Plated Greaves and Shield of the Five did a little too much as well. These adjustments should put them more in line with the game's general power level.

_______________________________________________

^077Hypercrown^*
^947-^* Recipe Cost increased from 700 Gold to 800 Gold.
   ^274*^* Total Cost increased from 5500 Gold to 5600 Gold.

^077Icon of the Goddess^*
^947-^* Mid Wars only changes:
   ^274*^* Gift of the Goddess state: Healing per second reduced from 15 to 7.5.
   ^274*^* Gift of the Goddess state: Mana Regeneration per second reduced from 20 to 10.
   ^274*^* Max charges reduced from 25 to 15.

^077Twin Blades^*
^947-^* Bonus Mana Regeneration reduced from 75% to 50%.
^947-^* Damage on the secondary attack reduced from 100% to 80%.

_______________________________________________

^077Ward of Revelation^*
^947-^* Initial placement that gives vision of enemy Wards in fog: radius of this mechanic reduced from 800 to 400.
   ^274*^* The default reveal radius of 800 remains untouched.

^947+^* Counterwarding is a pretty one-dimensional choice when placing them: simply cover the most area possible.

^947+^* By reducing the radius of the intiial placement that gives vision of existing enemy Wards that are in fog, players now have to choose more carefully between optimal area coverage of possible enemy ward locations, or getting suboptimal coverage while revealing ward on high ground.

^947+^* Players can still technically gain the both these benefits if they have a hero that can grant temporary clearvision of wards, or if they purchase the Flying Courier from the item shop.

==^960 Design ^*==



=^075 Staff of the Master & Master's Legacy Changes ^*=



^059Gemini^*

^256Fire and Ice^*
^947-^* Staff of the Master effect addition (new):
^947-^* Cooldown reduced from 45 seconds to 20 seconds.
^947-^* Now also splits Gemini into a third elemental form, Light.
   ^274*^* Light's life essence is still tied to the other elementals, i.e. Gemini still dies if either Fire, Ice or Light dies.
   ^274*^* For Fire & Ice, their Rebirth ability now also requires Light to be nearby for them to all recombine.
   ^274*^* For Fire & Ice, their Rebirth ability now sets Gemini's Health and Mana percentage to the highest of all 3 elemental forms.

^947+^* Fire gains a subability:
^947-^* Ability 3b - Together in Bright Spirit: After a 3 second cast time, teleports Fire to Light's location.
   ^274*^* Shares cooldown with Together in Spirit.
   ^274*^* Default activation hotkey: D

^947+^* Ice gains a subability:
^947-^* Ability 3b - Together in Bright Spirit: After a 3 second cast time, teleports Ice to Light's location.
   ^274*^* Shares cooldown with Together in Spirit.
   ^274*^* Default activation hotkey: D



^947+^* Light's abilities are as follows:

^947-^* Ability 1 - Light Breath: Unleashes a cone of light that deals 100/175/250 Magic Damage and applies a 40/60/80% Miss Chance to enemies for 3 seconds.
   ^274*^* Has the same general parameters (Cast Range, Mana Cost, Cooldown) as its analog ability on Fire and Ice.

^947-^* Ability 2 - Light Fang: Shoots Light at target enemy, making him invulnerable during the process. Deals 60/120/180 Magic Damage and applies a 0.5 second Stun.
   ^274*^* Has the same general parameters (Cast Range, Mana Cost, Cooldown) as its analog ability on Fire and Ice.

^947-^* Ability 3a - Together in Fiery Spirit: After a 3 second cast time, teleports Light to Fire's location.
   ^274*^* Cooldown: 30 seconds
   ^274*^* Shares cooldown with Together in Icy Spirit.

^947-^* Ability 3b - Together in Icy Spirit: After a 3 second cast time, teleports Light to Ice's location.
   ^274*^* Cooldown: 30 seconds
   ^274*^* Shares cooldown with Together in Fiery Spirit.
   ^274*^* Default activation hotkey: D

^947-^* Ability 4 - Rebirth / Lightning Form:
   ^274*^* Passive: While 1200 units away from enemy heroes, Light becomes an an agile elemental also known as Volt, gaining 10/50/10000% Movement Speed. Max Movement Speed is set to 750. This effect is disabled for 12 seconds when Light uses an ability, attacks, or takes damage.
   ^274*^*  Active: Activate to begin casting for 1.25 seconds. Upon finishing the cast, recreates Gemini at the current location with Health and Mana Percentages equal to the highest of Fire, Ice, and Light. Disabled if Fire, Ice and Light are more than 300 units apart.

^947+^* Light also serves as an agile scouting elemental. This rewards skilled Gemini players that can micromanage all 3 elementals optimally, all while taking advantage of Light's out-of-combat speed to scout and set up a constant global roaming presence in the late game.

_______________________________________________

^059Geomancer^*

^256Crystal Field^*
^947-^* Staff of the Master effect addition (to pre-existing):
   ^274*^* Now also applies a 35% Movement Speed Slow to enemies within 600 radius before the initial explosion occurs.

_______________________________________________

^059Magebane^*

^256Flash^*
^947-^* Staff of the Master addition (new):
   ^274*^* Enemy units within 600 radius of Magebane's blink location have Mana Feedback applied to them for 6 seconds. Active abilities cast by these affected targets will deal 110% of that ability's Mana Cost to the caster.

^256Mana Rift^*
^947-^* Staff of the Master effect change (complete replacement of the previous one):
   ^274*^* Mana Rift's Damage dealt to the main target now propagates to 1 other enemy hero within 800 radius with 60% power.
   ^274*^* Enemies affected by Mana Rift within the inner 225 radius will not be affected by this new effect, and will still take full damage.

_______________________________________________

^059Martyr^*

^256Sol's Conviction^*
^947-^* Staff of the Master effect addition (new):
   ^274*^* Allows Martyr to cast the ability on willing ally heroes within 600 Cast Range.

_______________________________________________

^059Ophelia^*

^256Command^*
^947-^* Staff of the Master effect change (to pre-existing):
   ^274*^* Ability now only grants 0.25x the Movement Speed bonus to her Ancient creeps.

_______________________________________________

^059Soul Reaper^*

^256Demonic Execution^*
^947-^* Staff of the Master effect addition (to pre-existing):
   ^274*^* Demonic Execution's Damage dealt to the main target now also chains to enemy heroes within 500 radius with 60% power each time, up to 5 times.
   ^274*^* The chaining effect cannot apply to a hero that is already hit by the same cast instance of Demonic Execution.

_______________________________________________

 

=^075 General ^*=

^947-^* On the hero pick screen while selecting an avatar, clicking on an unowned avatar's "Need Coins" button will now redirect you to the HoN Store.
   ^274*^* If players are lacking Gold Coins to purchase an avatar, they can now navigate to the HoN Store and click on the "Buy Coins" button there. 

^075Forests of Caldavar ("New") - Neutral Creeps^*
^947-^* Slightly reduced the max chase time & chase distance for the 2 bottom-left-most Legion neutral camps and the top-right-most Hellbourne neutral camp.
   ^274*^* Greatly mitigates the unintended behaviour of pulling the nearest lane creeps with these neutral camps.
   ^274*^* Note: this change also makes the double stack timings a bit more strict.
   ^274*^* This change is considered to be temporary until proper map changes can be made.

^075Towers^*
^947-^* Aggro check duration against enemy-player-controlled units that attack nearby ally heroes reduced from 2 seconds to 1 second.
   ^274*^* This does not affect the actual aggro duration of the tower against the targeted unit (i.e. after the tower has locked onto the target). It only affects the time in which it checks whether it needs to switch aggro or not.
   ^274*^* Mitigates cases where you damage an enemy hero when you are outside the tower's attack range & then proceed to go within tower range to last hit a creep and having the tower lock onto you.
   ^274*^* A more thorough remedy may be investigated in the future when time permits.

_______________________________________________

 



=^075 Heroes ^*=



^059Adrenaline^*

^256Rush^*
^947-^* Max number of Dread Stacks reduced from 12/20/28/36 to 15/20/25/30.

^256Death's Halo^*
^947-^* Number of Dread Stacks applied changed from 12/24/36 to 14/22/30.

^947+^* When playing against Adrenaline, it is simply too frustrating to be almost literally any hero with any amount of farm and be solo killed by Adrenaline with relatively less effective items as a solo ganker with maybe just a touch of ally support.
   ^274*^* No, hanging back and waiting to react with Stormspirit is not the ideal counterplay against Adrenaline, as it is not always feasible nor reliable.

^947+^* With these changes, Adrenaline's solo kill potential requires a little bit more ally assistance or proper itemization.

^059Ellonia^*

^256Flash Freeze^*
^947-^* Total Magic Damage is now capped at 550.

^947+^* When coupled with Staff of the Master, Flash Freeze's effect on her Absolute Zero ability dealt excessive area damage if Frosted Stacks were accumulated on enemies ahead of time (particularly when coupled with ally crowd control).
^947+^* Implementing a cap on the total Magic Damage in these circumstances & for long wars of attrition will prevent Ellonia from 1-shotting a hero with a large number of Frosted Stacks on them.

_______________________________________________

^059Engineer^*
^947-^* Primary Attribute changed from Agility to Intelligence.
^947-^* Agility growth per level reduced from 2.3 to 2.1.
^947-^* Intelligence growth per level increased from 2.1 to 2.5.

^947+^* Engineer is underperforming and most of his item build consists of Intelligence items. This small change should help his performance significantly by increasing his damage by mid/late game compared to other support heroes.

^947-^* No longer requires the damage to exceed 15% of Ichor's Current Health for the Damage Reduction to take place.
^947-^* Max total Damage Reduction changed from 40/45/50/60% to 25/35/45/55%.

_______________________________________________

^059Moira^*

^256Shards of Harkon^*
^947-^* Cast Action Time reduced from 300ms to 200ms.
^947-^* Cast Time reduced from 600ms to 200ms.

^256Ephemeral Forge^*
^947-^* Cooldown increased from 60/55/50/45 seconds to 95/90/85/80 seconds.
^947-^* Max duration increased from 10/14/18/22 seconds to 12/18/24/30 seconds.

^947+^* The cooldown of Ephemeral Forge caused Moira to be able to assist allies in ganks and teamfights without any risk far too frequently.
^947+^* Increasing the cooldown of Ephemeral Forge will force Moira to use this ability more wisely. Otherwise, she will have to put herself at risk more often if she uses this ability too haphazardly.

_______________________________________________

^059Myrmidon^*
^947-^* Attack Range increased from 450 to 500.

^256Weed Field^*
^947-^* State duration increased from 1/1.33/1.6/2 seconds to 1/1.4/1.8/2.2 seconds.

^256Magic Carp^*
^947-^* Mana Cost reduced from 95/100/105/110 to 80/90/100/110.
^947-^* Now applies a 30/40/50/60% Movement Speed Slow for 0.6 seconds if the target is not moving at the time of impact.

^947+^* The change to Magic Carp only ever so slightly makes it easier to ensure that Weed Field hits your intended target. The change looks stronger on paper than it is in practice.

^947-^* Mana Cost changed from 50 to 60.

^059Parasite^*

^947-^* Consume subability can no longer be used while the Infested creep is Silenced or Stunned.

^947+^* Parasite should not be able to break out of crowd control early from his Infested creep. This was a big oversight for most of the HoN's lifetime.
^947+^* As compensation for this adjustment, the previous nerf has been toned back.

_______________________________________________

^059Qi^*

^256Ancestral Assault^*

   ^274*^* 300 Range and below: starts at 100% Damage
   ^274*^*  Max Range: scales down to 40% Damage

_______________________________________________

^059Sapphire^*

^256Unstable Shards^*
^947-^* Area Magic Damage upon hitting the Unstable Shard increased from 110/115/120/125% to 115/120/125/130% of the Attacker's Attack Damage.

_______________________________________________

^059Scout^*

^256Electric Eye^*
^947-^* Effect type of the Silence application changed from SuperiorMagic to Magic.
^947-^* Silence duration reduced from 3/4/5/6 seconds to 2/3/4/5 seconds.
^947-^* Placement restriction for the minimum distance from a tree increased from 75 units to 100 units.
^947-^* Cast Range increased from 100 to 150.
^947-^* Vision radius and reveal radius changed from 800 to 700/800/900/1000.
^947-^* Proximity detection range (for enemies to see the Electric Eye) increased from 200 to 300.

^947+^* Scout's Electric Eyes have been a fundamentally broken tool since his inception. The ability has been made more reasonable (and more importantly, unable to pierce Magic Immunity as a non-ultimate ability) and simultaneously made less aggravating to play against if the Scout player uses Electric Eyes mostly to block neutral camps from spawning, while focusing more on the scouting/vision aspect of the Electric Eyes.

^947+^* If Scout needs buffs in the future, he will receive them in the appropriate parts of his kit. Until then, these adjustments should make playing against Scout a little more bearable without affecting his actual power much.

_______________________________________________

^256Unleash Vorax^*

   ^274*^* The distance value has also increased from 1000 units to 1500 units in the above change.

   ^274*^* State will still be unable to be transferred by abilities like Parasite's Facehug for technical reasons.

^256Bound by Fate^*

   ^274*^* State will still be unable to be transferred by abilities like Parasite's Facehug for technical reasons.
   ^274*^* Purging 1 of the debuff states on a target will purge the tether state on the other target.

^256Luck of the Draw^*
^947-^* Critical Strike chance increased from 20% to 25%.

 _______________________________________________
