==^960 Bug Fixes & Optimizations ^*==

 

=^075 General ^*=

^947-^* Fixed a MMR exploit where players intentionally made every player's MMR behave as 1500 (and making everyone's MMR change +/- 5 for the game) by switching game modes before entering the matchmaking queue.
   ^274*^* Players who abused this bug should be ashamed of themselves.
   ^274*^* Remember to report big game exploits to the Senior Game Masters (SGMs) when possible! The players will be suspended in these scenarios.

^947-^* MvP Show/Hide button no longer displays at the beginning of a subsequent game.

^947-^* Water tile definitions near the middle lane ramps were adjusted to more accurately match the water's visual location for the purposes of abilities dependent on the presence of a water tile (e.g. Riptide's In My Element ability).



=^075 Heroes ^*=

^059Berzerker^*
^947-^* Chain Spike timer is now properly readjusted when the initial state is applied on an enemy (particularly when used against an opponent that reduces debuff durations).

^059Geomancer^*
^947-^* Slightly increased sight radius while Geo Stalk is active.
   ^274*^* Fixes a corner case scenario where Geomancer cannot see a target within the Geo Stalk detection radius at very specific angles.

^059Grinex^*
^947-^* Fixed a minor tooltip error with Nether Strike when the Staff of the Master effect is on Grinex.

^059Hellbringer^*
^947-^* Malphas can no longer attack buildings if Hellbringer isn't within 925 units.
^947-^* Summon Malphas visual effects at the target location are now displayed properly for Batllebringer and Arimane the Pit Lord alternate avatars.

^059Lodestone^*
^947-^* Lodestone Plates visuals now show the appropriate cosmetic for all alternate avatars.
^947-^* Lodestone Plates visuals are now properly shown when the ability timer reaches 0.

^059Moira^*
^947-^* Mana Sunder no longer causes single target spells cast by the affected targets to apply Mana Sunder to that spell's target for 1 second.

^059Myrmidon^*
^947-^* Forced Evolution now propagates the area effect properly to illusions with 33% power when they attack (when boosted by Staff of the Master)

^059Nitro^*
^947-^* Tesla Cannon Nitro now plays her attack sounds properly when using Ballistic.
^947-^* Tesla Cannon Nitro's textures are now displayed properly on Low and Medium graphic settings.

^059Swiftblade^*
^947-^* Counter Strike (Counter Attack, when boosted by Staff of the Master) active ability state now properly disappears when used to proc a Null Stone or effects similar to it (e.g. Moraxus' Arcane Shield)

^059Xemplar^*
^947-^* Illusions spawned from Schism now have properly have their attack animations synchronized with the actual attack impact time.

 

=^075 Items ^*=

^947-^* The following items can no longer be transferred to other units or be dropped on the ground to fix an exploit where more than 1 completed of the item can be owned by a particular player in their hero's inventory at any given time:
   ^274*^* Alchemist's Bones
   ^274*^* Jade Spire
   ^274*^* Restoration Stone

^947+^* Note that there is still 1 way to attempt to bypass this, but the player is in for a gold loss surprise (and/or just flat out being inefficient) if they try to go that far. So just don't even try to exploit this, it will only hinder you.
   ^274*^* This was an unintended exploit. Please use RAP to report players if you see them repeatedly performing any kind of exploit that bypasses an item's intended limit per player.



^077Flying Courier^*
^947-^* Can no longer perform a gold pooling exploit by dropping a shareable item with charges & picking it up again with the Flying Courier to change the item's ownership.
   ^274*^* This was an unintended exploit. Please use RAP to report players if you see them continuously performing any kind of gold pooling exploit in the future, as they will be suspended if they continue to do so.

^077Hypercrown^*
^947-^* Damage is now sourced properly from the original owner of Hypercrown if the procs from its Charged Up state ends up damaging & killing enemy units.
   ^274*^* This properly gives gold bounty to Hypercrown's owner when the Charged Up state kills an enemy unit.

^980Version 4.8.2^*
-------------
^98011 February 2020^*



==^960 Design ^*==



=^075 Hero Changes - Swiftblade ^*=

^947+^* Swiftblade gets his own section in these patch notes since he has a Staff of the Master change that pertains to a skill rework!

^947+^* Counter Attack was an ability that was a result of an afterthought when filling the last ability of Swiftblade's kit.
^947+^* By changing the ability to be more interactive and have practical use throughout the game, this allows the hero to be more engaging to play and transitions him more to a dueling playstyle!

 

^059Swiftblade^*

^256Counter Attack^*
^947-^* In addition to its current effects, now has a new active effect: Activate to block all incoming autoattacks for 1.5 seconds, while countering with Swiftblade's autoattacks against the next 1/2/3/4 hero attacks during this time.
^947-^* These counterattacks proc Way of the Sword.
   ^274*^* Note that this state is a Buff, so Blade Frenzy & other sources of Purge effects will dispel the buff.
   ^274*^* The passive effects of Counter Attack are disabled while Counter Attack's buff state is active.
   ^274*^* Note that Counter Attack cannot be activated while Blade Frenzy is active (since Blade Frenzy silences Swiftblade).
   ^274*^* Has a Mana Cost of 50 and a Cooldown of 23/20/17/14 seconds.

^947-^* Staff of the Master effect addition (new):
   ^274*^* If a hero attack is blocked, when Counter Attack expires, Swiftblade becomes Invulnerable for 1 second, and Counter Attack changes to Counter Strike for the next 5 seconds (800 Cast Range).
   ^274*^* Target an enemy to use Counter Strike, attacking & teleporting to them and applying a 80% Movement Slow for 2 seconds.
   ^274*^* Counter Attack's buff duration resets when the initial Invulnerability period ends.

_______________________________________________

 

=^075 Hero Changes - Keeper of the Forest ^*=

^947+^* Ever since the readjustment of Keeper of the Forest's Animated Trees to comfortably kill Ancient Creeps early, Keeper of the Forest has been drastically underperforming.

^947+^* Keeper of the Forest received buffs to compensate for this, and also received changes that accentuate his unique structure defense role & being a structural fortifier and healer.
^947+^* Additionally, Keeper of the Forest received another boost from Staff of the Master. This time, it is his legacy Tree Sight ability!

 

^059Keeper of the Forest^*
^947-^* Strength growth per level increased from 3 to 3.3.

^256Nature's Guidance^*
^947-^* Bonus Health Regeneration per second increased from 1/2/3/4 to 2/3/4/5.
^947-^* Structures now gain an extra +3 Armor and Health Regeneration per second from this ability.
^947-^* Immobilize state duration increased from 0.5/0.8/1.1/1.4 seconds to 0.8/1.2/1.6/2 seconds.
^947-^* Units other than Keeper of the Forest attacking out of stealth now apply the Immobilize state for 0.5x the duration.

^256Animate Forest^*
^947-^* Max Health of Animated Trees from this ability increased from 370/430/490/550 to 390/460/530/600.

^256Strength in Numbers^*
^947-^* Staff of the Master effect addition (new):
   ^274*^* Grants an active ability that enchants a tree, granting 800 Clearvision around the area.
   ^274*^* The Clearvision persists as long as the enchanted tree is alive.
   ^274*^* Enemies can see an enchanted tree if they have vision of it.
   ^274*^* Ability has a 150 Cast Range, a Mana Cost of 100, and a cooldown of 30 seconds.

_______________________________________________

 

=^075 Master's Legacy & Staff of the Master effect Changes ^*=

 

^077Master's Legacy^*
^947-^* Cost reduced from 2500 Gold to 2300 Gold.
^947-^* Now grants an additional +5 Strength, Agility and Intelligence when upgraded from Staff of the Master.
   ^274*^* This item now grants a total of +15 Strength, Agility and Intelligence.

^947+^* Players are a little bit too deterred from upgrading their Staff of the Master to Master's Legacy to help their allies.
^947+^* Getting some bonuses for themselves while lowering the cost of the upgrade by just a bit should incentivize players to buy Master's Legacy without making it an obvious item to buy in the majority of their games.

_______________________________________________

^059Andromeda^*

^256Void Rip^*
^947-^* Staff of the Master effect changes (to pre-existing):
   ^274*^* Cooldown is now reduced to 20 seconds (instead of 10 seconds).
   ^274*^* Magic Immunity duration reduced from 3 seconds to 2 seconds.

_______________________________________________

^059Artesia^*

^256Essence Projection^*
^947-^* Staff of the Master effect changes (to pre-existing):
   ^274*^* Cast Range increased from 5000 to Global.

_______________________________________________

^059Tremble^*

^256Hive Mind^*
^947-^* Staff of the Master effect changes (to pre-existing):
   ^274*^* Now also spawns the uncontrollable Shudder (the one that Tremble normally has without Staff of the Master) in addition to the controllable Shudder.
   ^274*^* In this scenario, the uncontrollable Shudder will be more transparent than the controllable Shudder (to avoid unnecessary screen obstruction & confusion).

_______________________________________________



=^075 User Interface: Minimap Changes ^*=

^947-^* When Player Colours are enabled: Hero icons in the minimap now have their icon borders match the colour of the player (for your own team only; the icon borders for enemy heroes will still be red).
^947-^* When Player Colours are enabled: also sets the Courier minimap icons to player colours (note that the Courier minimap icon colour will be white if Player Colours are disabled).
   ^274*^* Note: Player Colours can be toggled via the button above the minimap.

^059Gravekeeper^*

^256Corpse Explosion^*

^947-^* Magic Damage per corpse reduced from 20/40/60/80 to 10/20/30/50.

^947+^* Ever since the consistent area damage change, Corpse Explosion has finally not been useless. In fact, it was overperforming and was a free ticket to winning the lane if Gravekeeper chose to max Corpse Explosion.

^947+^* Normalizing the damage in the early levels and maintaining its above average power at the max level of the ability will keep it powerful, but only at higher levels of the ability.

^059Moira^*

^256Shards of Harkon^*

_______________________________________________

^059Nomad^*
^947-^* Strength growth per level reduced from 2 to 1.8.
^947-^* Agility growth per level reduced from 2.9 to 2.8.
^947-^* Intelligence growth per level reduced from 2.2 to 2.

_______________________________________________

^059Nitro^*

^256Ballistic^*
^947-^* While Nitro and Mauser are together, projectile lifetime reduced from 0.35s to 0.25s.
   ^274*^* Projectile lifetime remains unchanged when Mauser is detached from Nitro.

^947+^* Reducing the projectile lifetime should drastically increase the accuracy of her shots for less mechanically accurate players without affecting veteran Nitro players nearly as much. This should reduce the size of Nitro's performance gap between lower and higher bracket play.

_______________________________________________

^059Parasite^*

^256Infest^*
^947-^* Infested creep now receives +50% Damage from player-controlled enemy units (+25% Damage if Staff of the Master is on Parasite).

^947+^* Parasite's Infest mechanic that allows him to hide inside a mobile creep has been incredibly frustrating to deal with since the hero has been introduced to the game (in the event Alchemist's Bones is not available to be used as a counter when engaging him). Every time Parasite is inside a creep, he basically has a second ~1000 Health bar that soaks up enemy crowd control and overkill damage from nukes with practically no cost to Parasite.

^947+^* This change should allow enemy players to more quickly deal with Parasite's "second Health Bar" without it affecting Parasite's jungling capabilities.

_______________________________________________

^059Qi^*
^947-^* Attack projectile speed increased from 900 to 1100 units per second.

_______________________________________________

^059Silhouette^*

^256Shadow^*
^947-^* Like a Ninja (swap ability) changes:
   ^274*^* Cast time and cast action time reduced from 0.15 seconds to 0 seconds.
   ^274*^* Cast time and cast action time now increases by 0.1 seconds for every 1000 units between Silhouette and her Shadow (0.15 seconds for the first 1000 units).
   ^274*^* Max cast time and cast action time of 0.55 seconds.

^947+^* Silhouette's relative safety for split-pushing and potential backdooring is much higher than almost every other hero's safety for most of HoN's history because her swap subability is a reliable and quick means of escape with an abnormally long teleport range (relative to other escapes).

^947+^* Increasing the risk ever so slightly should increase her window of vulnerability just enough where Silhouette will have to be mindful of her overall Shadow positioning, or be extra mindful of the current situation and swap out earlier than normal if she wishes to be 100% safe.

_______________________________________________

^059Solstice^*

^256Graceful Strikes^*
^947-^* Day Time: Bonus Physical Damage per proc changed from 50/75/100/125 to 60/80/100/120.
^947-^* Night Time: Bonus Physical Damage per proc increased from 70/105/140/175 to 80/115/150/185.

_______________________________________________

^059Soul Reaper^*
^947-^* Attack projectile speed increased from 900 to 1300 units per second.

_______________________________________________

^059Valkyrie^*
^947-^* Attack projectile speed increased from 900 to 1100 units per second.

^256Javelin of Light^*
^947-^* Projectile touch effect type changed from True to Magic.
   ^274*^* This is actually considered a buff, as it means that Magic Immune units can no longer "catch" the Javelin & receive no damage and no Stun, which ends up blocking it for their allies without penalty.
   ^274*^* Instead, the projectile will go through Magic Immune units and no longer consider them as a valid target for impact.

_______________________________________________

^059War Beast^*

^256Metamorphosis^*
^947-^* Cooldown reduced from 115/95/70 seconds to 115/90/65 seconds.

_______________________________________________

^059Warchief^*
^947-^* Intelligence growth per level reduced from 2.9 to 2.7.

_______________________________________________

 

=^075 Items ^*=

 

^077Alchemist's Bones^*
^947-^* Gold bounty of the creep killed by Alchemist's Bones active ability changed from 3x the creep bounty to 165 Gold.
   ^274*^* The XP bounty multiplier still remains the same, so targeting a harder creep with Alchemist's Bones will still yield more experience.

_______________________________________________

^077Hypercrown^*
^947-^* Active ability can now be used on allies even if they are Magic Immune.

_______________________________________________

^077Rejuvenation Potion^*
^947-^* Cost increased from 300 Gold to 350 Gold.

_______________________________________________

==^960 Bug Fixes & Optimizations ^*==

 

=^075 Heroes ^*=

^059Andromeda^*
^947-^* Void Rip (when boosted by Staff of the Master): Magic Immunity effect counts as an Activation Modifier (so the Magic Immunity portion of the buff granted to Andromeda cannot be active at the same time as Shrunken Head or Void Talisman).
   ^274*^* If you use one of these items and then activate Andromeda's Void Rip, the Magic Immunity from Void Rip will not apply to Andromeda at all. Note that this only has gameplay implications in the most niche of scenarios - most scenarios will still be the same.

^059Corrupted Disciple^*
^947-^* Electric Tide's inner radius offset was increased from 50 units to 80 units to mitigate cases of its hitbox being undetected by a unit that is moving towards Corrupted Disciple (in the event that one or both units have high Movement Speed).

^059Moira^*
^947-^* Units affected by Mana Sunder now regain the appropriate amount of mana if they have debuff duration reduction sources.

^059Monkey King^*
^947-^* Illusive Dash's initial cast is now properly detected as a spellcast by certain sources in the game (e.g. Power Supply).

^059Nymphora^*
^947-^* In the Mid Wars map, Teleport restriction boundaries have been adjusted to be the boundary that is roughly defined by the Tier 2 towers.

^059Predator^*
^947-^* Stone Hide's Magic Immunity effect now counts as an Activation Modifier (so the Magic Immunity effect cannot be active at the same time as Shrunken Head or Void Talisman).
   ^274*^* If you use one of these items and then activate Predator's Stone Hide, if the item's immunity duration runs out & Stone Hide's duration is still active, you will NOT receive the immunity effect until other Activation Modifier effects wear off.

^059Swiftblade^*
^947-^* Blade Frenzy's Magic Immunity effect now counts as an Activation Modifier (so the Magic Immunity effect cannot be active at the same time as Shrunken Head or Void Talisman).
   ^274*^* If you use one of these items and then activate Swiftblade's Blade Frenzy, if the item's immunity duration runs out & Blade Frenzy's duration is still active, you will NOT receive the immunity effect for the rest of the state duration. Note that this only has gameplay implications in the most niche of scenarios - most scenarios will still be the same.

 

=^075 Items ^*=

^077Whispering Helm^*
^947-^* Cooldown is now properly halved if the dominated creep becomes controlled by another source (e.g. Parasite's Infest).

^256Summon Malphas^*

^947-^* Malphas Attack Damage reduced from 80/130/180 to 80/125/170.
^947-^* Malphas' Destruction passive ability: bonus damage dealt to structures reduced from 50/75/100% to 30/40/50%.

_______________________________________________

^059Keeper of the Forest^*

^256Nature's Guidance^*
^947-^* Can now target ally structures to apply the Armor and Health Regeneration state (but not the stealth state).
   ^274*^* Any damage a structure takes while the Backdoor Protection System is in effect & the appropriate conditions are fulfilled will not trigger the system if the structure's current health is greater than the "checkpoint" health value saved by the Backdoor Protection System.
   ^274*^* Example: After a regular push (under non-backdoor conditions), a tower's Health drops from 1900 (max Health) to 1500 (this is the checkpoint health value). The If Nature's Guidance heals the Tower's Health above 1500 and an enemy attempts to backdoor & fulfills the system's conditions, then the Backdoor Protection System will not grant 20 Health Regeneration per second to the tower until it is below the checkpoint health value (1500). This bonus regeneration will only occur until it reaches the checkpoint value again - Nature's Guidance's regeneration will continue to take place throughout these events as intended.

_______________________________________________

^059Nitro^*

^256Ballistic^*
^947-^* Range increased from 700 to 850.
^947-^* Projectile's fixed lifetime reduced from 500ms to 350ms (400ms while detached from Mauser).
   ^274*^* Properly results in a faster projectile now.
^947-^* Touch radius increased from 65 to 70.

_______________________________________________

^059Slither^*

^256Poison Spray^*

==^960 Staff of the Master & Master's Legacy Changes ^*==



^947+^* This patch will be the first in a series of multiple patches that will focus on rebalancing Staff of the Master effects across different heroes, as well as focus on making this item's effects "worth it" for heroes that don't normally pick up the item (e.g. carry heroes), but can obtain the effect from an ally's Master's Legacy.

^947+^* Additionally, some of the staler Staff of the Master effects will be revamped, and some new & exciting Staff of the Master effects will be added to the game!

^947+^* To ensure that the power of all of these effects are properly gated, the price of Master's Legacy has to increase by a substantial amount.



^077Master's Legacy^*
^947-^* Recipe Cost increased from 2000 Gold to 2500 Gold.

_______________________________________________

^256Emerald Lightning^*
^947-^* Staff of the Master additions (new):

_______________________________________________

^059Nitro^*

^256Ballistic^*
^947-^* Range reduced from 800/900/1000/1100/1200 to 700.
^947-^* Touch radius reduced from 90 to 65.

^256Divide & Conquer^*
^947-^* Base Attack Time for Mauser increased from 1.2 to 1.7.
^947-^* Attack Action Time for Mauser reduced from 0.4 seconds to 0.2 seconds.
^947-^* Bonus Attack Damage multiplier for Mauser reduced from 1.2x to 1.1x.
^947-^* Bonus Movement and Attack Speed granted when Ballistic hits an enemy hero reduced from 10/20/30/40 to 10/15/20/25.

^947+^* Nitro's excessive range and kiting capabilities were too frustrating to deal with in many scenarios for too much of HoN's lifetime, allowing Nitro to avoid interaction far too often. Ballistic's hitbox was also far bigger than its visual indicator.
^947+^* These changes will force Nitro to commit more when attacking foes and force her to be more careful with spacing, while simultaneously providing her opponents more opportunities to properly interact with her.
^947+^* Having less range means that it's actually easier to hit her shots too, so landing Nitro's autoattacks will be much easier for the players who don't normally play her.

_______________________________________________

^256Nature's Wrath^*
^947-^* Mana Cost increased from 70/80/90/100 to 90/100/110/120.
^947-^* Cooldown increased from 13/12/11/10 seconds to 14/13/12/11 seconds.

==^960 Bug Fixes & Optimizations ^*==



=^075 General ^*=

^947-^* The previous changes for skill flow improvement in Patch 4.8.0 (Doctor Repulsor's Ludicrous Speed, Lodestone's Headsmash, and Prisoner's Shackled) no longer have their subability change to an activatable state if a non-visible enemy hero (stealthed or in fog) is within the ability's cast radius.
^947-^* The game no longer displays the following message when the following event occurs: "The creeps have grown stronger...).
   ^274*^* Prevents unnecessary screen clutter in the chat log.

 

=^075 User Interface ^*=

^947-^* Pinging items in an enemy's or ally's Consumable Slot in the bottom-right corner window will now display the appropriate information in your team's chat log.
^947-^* Stat columns on the post-game statistics page is now player-sortable for the new UI.
^947-^* A new stat column that represents "Wards Placed" has been added to the post-game match stats screen.
^947-^* The CoN Season Info button in the top-right corner of the "Ranked" mode option in the mode pick screen has been removed.
^947-^* Minor optimizations were performed for a few sections in the User Interface.
^947-^* Added Ranked MMR to the profile page in the new UI (NAEU Client only).
^947-^* Friend list now updates properly in the new UI.

 

=^075 Heroes ^*=

^059Blacksmith^*
^947-^* Risky Cast (boosted by Staff of the Master) now plays the correct visual effect if a 4x Multicast is procced.

^059Chronos^*
^947-^* Christmas Chronos no longer plays Father Time Chronos' knocked-down sound when she plays her knocked-down animation (e.g. from self-taunting).

^059Nitro^*
^947-^* Abilities that force Nitro to attack a target (Legionnaire's Taunt, Puppet Master's Puppet Show, and Kane's Face Off) now function properly on Nitro.

^059Predator^*
^947-^* Stone Hide visuals are now displayed more clearly on certain Predator alternate avatars.
   ^274*^* Visuals were also made more consistent with the majority of Predator's alternate avatars.

 

=^075 Items ^*=

^077Hypercrown^*
^947-^* No longer plays lightning sounds globally (i.e. will no longer alert enemies of your activity when fighting creeps in fog).

^980Version 4.8.0^*
-------------
^9808 October 2019^*

 

==^960 Champions of Newerth (CoN) ^*==

^947-^* The new Champions of Newerth Season will last indefinitely until further notice.
   ^274*^* This can change at any point Garena & Frostburn Studios deems appropriate.

^947-^* All players will be reset to a base MMR of 1500 at the start of this Season (instead of starting off at a base MMR of 1250).
   ^274*^* This MMR reset combats all of the smurfing and stat manipulation that took place in the past, and the MMR system will be considered much more stable since it now uses the Legacy MMR system after the placement match period.
   ^274*^* Placement Matches will still have additional weight for determining your initial rank, but the weight from these Placement Matches is much lighter than prior Seasons.

 

=^075 User Interface ^*=

^947-^* Shadowpicking will no longer function during the Hero Ban Voting Phase of Ranked Pick (to prevent any confusion or unintended behaviour after the Voting Phase ends).

^947+^* The following changes were implemented from the MVP Mod (credits to EngineerT):
^947-^* Added a close button to the MVP window (so it does not obstruct your view if you wish to control your hero for a short time after the game ends).
^947-^* Added a disconnect button to the MVP Window (so you can disconnect from the game without having to navigate to the menu).

^947-^* As a result of the changes for this Champions of Newerth Season, the Ladder button has been re-enabled (since MMR will now really be "permanent" and matters a lot more than before).
   ^274*^* Disclaimer: the Ladder's displayed information may not be accurate & may need additional time to synchronize properly, or may need additional time & resources to fix the issue. There is no estimated time for a fix if this feature does not display the correct information.

^947-^* HoN Live has also been re-enabled to allow players to watch other HoN games once again!

 

=^075 General ^*=

^075Well/Tarpit^*
^947-^* Well's/Tarpit's attacks now deal 1.35x damage to enemy heroes.
^947-^* Well's/Tarpit's attacks now have 25% Armor Penetration against enemy heroes.

 

=^075 Neutral Creeps ^*=

^947-^* Aggro range increased from 225 to 275.
   ^274*^* Applies to all neutral creeps except for those that are able to spawn in the Easy neutral camps.

^059Dragon Master^*
^947-^* Max Health reduced from 1800 to 1600.

^059Dragon^*
^947-^* Max Health increased from 900 to 1000.

 

=^075 Forests of Caldavar ("New") Map ^*=

^947-^* The line of trees closest to the left & right sides of the map near each side lane's Tier 1 (outermost) Tower have been drastically shortened.

==^960 Items ^*==



^077Arcane Bomb^*
^947-^* Bonus Magic Damage dealt to enemy player-controlled-non-hero units reduced from 40% of the target's Max Health to 30% of the target's Max Health.
   ^274*^* Still deals the base 150 Magic Damage to enemy player-controlled-non-hero units.

   ^274*^* Initial stock of 1.
   ^274*^* Item is only purchaseable after the 6-minute mark.
   ^274*^* Refreshes at a rate of 1 stock every 15 minutes.
   ^274*^* Maximum of 1 stock available in the store at any given time.

^947-^* True Damage mechanics dealt to enemy illusions changed.

^947-^* Previously:
   ^274*^* Target enemy illusion receives 900 True Damage or 75% of their Max Health, whichever is higher.

^947-^* After this patch:
   ^274*^*  Target enemy illusion receives 500 True Damage or 50% of their Max Health, whichever is higher.
   ^274*^*  Each enemy illusion affected spawns an invisible arcane shockwave of 600 radius that only affects other enemy illusions in the area, making them receive 200 True Damage or 15% of their Max Health, whichever is higher.
   ^274*^*  i.e. a Behemoth Shockwave echo effect only for enemy illusions.

^980Version 4.7.9^*
-------------
^98013 August 2019^*



==^960 Design ^*==



=^075 General ^*=

The following abilities can now be double-activated to cast the spell at the caster's location:
^947-^* Artillery's Mortar Strike
^947-^* Engineer's The Keg

^980Version 4.7.8^*
-------------
^98016 July 2019^*



==^960 Design ^*==

 

=^075 Forests of Caldavar ("New") ^*=

^947-^* Legion base towers (Tier 4 towers) have been repositioned more towards the side lanes (rather than towards the center lane).
   ^274*^* Allows Hellbourne to place a Ward of Sight that remains undetected at the top of the staircase in front of the World Tree.



=^075 Kongor & Hell Kongor Bosses ^*=

^947-^* Now drops a Token of Life if killed by a team who has killed another Kongor Boss within the last 180 seconds (i.e. if the "Kongor Slayer" state is active on them).
^947-^* Token of Life cannot be dropped by Kongor if the team who killed it has heroes with the "Kongor Decimator" state (the state that contains the bonus Structure Damage for 180 seconds after killing the second Kongor boss within the "Kongor Slayer" state duration), nor if they have the "No Kongor Decimator" state (the state that prevents a team from obtaining the "Kongor Decimator" state 120 seconds after either the "Kongor Decimator" or "Kongor Slayer" states expire).
^947-^* Bonus damage against Structures granted by the "Kongor Decimator" state reduced from +80% to +60% (+40% to +30% for Mid Wars).
   ^274*^* NB: These changes do not apply to the "old" Forests of Caldavar map.

^947-^* Hero renamed from "Chi".

   ^274*^* Note that both are still referring to the same thing and are equally valid, and pronounced the same way. We currently do not have a hero name that starts with the letter "Q" though, and Qi looks a lot cooler!

^059Jeraziah^*

^256Righteous Strike^*
^947-^* Cooldown increased from 7 seconds to 8 seconds.
^947-^* Bonus True Damage reduced from 60/80/100/120% to 40/60/80/100%.
^947-^* Movement Speed Slow reduced from 20% to 15%.
^947-^* Movement Speed Slow duration reduced from 2.5 seconds to 1.5 seconds.
^947-^* Cleave damage percentage of total damage dealt reduced from 40% to 30%.
^947-^* In-game role designation changed from "Support, Melee" to "Solo, Carry, Support, Melee".

^947+^* Jeraziah's carry potential was a little bit too strong. The cleave effects were supposed to be add-ons that make him feel more like his HoN beta iteration and weren't supposed to be particularly impactful.

^947+^* Some players still play Jeraziah primarily as a support. This is suboptimal, as Jeraziah should be played as one of the core heroes on your team since he needs to be on the front lines to actually make full use of all his abilities. This means that he requires farm, and should typically be played as a semi-carry at a minimum to have a reasonable impact on the game, which implies he should play as a solo hero or with a ranged hero as a lane partner. Jeraziah is meant to be a core hero first & foremost, with pretty strong support abilities as a supplement. He is not meant to be played as a traditional hard support hero, and some support heroes like Jeraziah are never meant to be played as a traditional hard support hero with very little items.

^947+^* Here is an educational remark for players regarding Core Heroes. A Core Hero (i.e. a "Core") does not necessarily equate to a traditional Hard Carry (high autoattack DPS that scales with items and levels). This is an important distinction that many players do not grasp. Using the standard number priority system from 1 to 5 to represent general resource allocation for a particular hero on a given team, where "1" is the highest resource allocation & "5" is the lowest resource allocation, Cores are typically between priorities 1 to 3 throughout the entire game. Most hard carry heroes are priority 1, and most non-hard carry heroes fluctuate between priorities 2 & 3 depending on the game, hero lineup & context of the game at any given moment during the game. By the way, did you know number priorities for each hero may change over the course of the game? For example, a Behemoth/Magmus may be considered anywhere from priority 1-4 depending on how important it is for them to secure their Portal Key as fast as possible in that particular game. After that hero obtains their Portal Key, their priority typically shifts to a 4 or 5 (not always though, if they have to be played as a Core in that particular game).

^947+^* For those who took the time to read all of this to become better players, now that we know Hard Carry Heroes are only a type of Cores (and being a Core does not mean you are a hard Carry Hero), it is a fact that most non-Hard-Carry Cores need a reasonably high gold & level allocation. While they won't hard-carry through hard DPS like traditional Hard Carries do with enough of a gold/level advantage, they end up doing a little bit of everything while having high enough survivability and high enough damage (plus their unique tools) to make them a threat. But they can only achieve this if they get a particular amount of gold & levels relative to your team & to the enemy team. Their positions will typically vary from 2 to 3 throughout the entire game. Core heroes are typically mid/late semi-carries, and examples include: Torturer, Defiler and Soul Reaper, since these heroes thrive the longer teamfights go for if they can stay alive.

^947+^* All of this context is necessary to explain that Jeraziah will always be suboptimal as a traditional hard support hero, especially if he itemizes as one. He has very limited effectiveness in the back lines and needs to have enough durability and damage on the front lines to pose a threat, and this is why melee support heroes must be played as core heroes/semi-carries at the least to be effective. 

^947+^* Basically: don't play Jeraziah as a traditional hard support hero, but also don't play him like a traditional hard carry hero. Play him as a Core Hero. If for some reason Jeraziah has to be shafted to priority 5, then at least get your survivability & damage up with cost/slot efficient items so that you can at least be in the front lines long enough to do something meaningful.

^947+^* Finally, remember HoN is a dynamic & highly adaptable game, and while there sometimes isn't a definitive "best" resource allocation & "best" way to play certain heroes depending on the unique scenario you are playing in, there are certainly a number "highly viable" ways to play them, and a large amount of suboptimal ways to play them in given scenarios.

_______________________________________________

^059Magebane^*
^947-^* Base Attack Time reduced from 1.7 to 1.45.
^947-^* Starting Agility reduced from 35 to 25.
^947-^* Agility growth per level reduced from 3.5 to 3.1.
^947-^* Starting Armor reduced from 1.52 to 1.5.
^947-^* Base Damage increased from 13-17 to 23-27.
   ^274*^* As a result, starting Damage increased from 46-50 to 48-52.

^256Mana Rift^*
^947-^* Now also affects enemies within an area of 225 radius around the target without the need of Staff of the Master.
   ^274*^* Staff of the Master now doubles the radius.
^947-^* Now also applies a 0.1/0.2/0.3 second Stun to affected enemies.

^947+^* Magebane's high Agility was problematic because of the high Armor value associated with high Agility in the late game. At the same time, Magebane has been statistically underperforming.

^947+^* Rather than continuing to adjust his Agility in small increments, Magebane reacquired his low Base Attack Time (BAT, as one of the only exceptions in the game, due to how much it actually impacts Magebane relative to other heroes who had a low BAT. This allows him to scale much better with Agility and Attack Speed indirectly than other heroes without the side effect of drastically increasing his Armor late game. The attribute adjustments to Magebane are considered both a nerf and a buff.

^947+^* The second adjustment that Magebane received to compensate for the multitude of nerfs to Magebane in Patch 4.7.3 (direct nerfs, and the Runed Cleaver nerfs and Ancients gold bounty nerfs) was a small buff to his ultimate to increase Magebane's contributions to early and mid game teamfights. The mini-stun was returned to prevent Homecoming Stones from acting as a free escape, and was overall a quality-of-life change for him. With all of these changes, Magebane should be in a more mechanically balanced spot than before.

^256Retribution^*

^059Plague Rider^*

^256Extinguish^*
^947-^* Ability can now only target non-player-controlled (NPC) creeps.
   ^274*^* Levels 1, 2 and 3 of the ability can still only target ally creeps like before, with the added restriction of it only being able to affect NPC creeps.
   ^274*^* Level 4 of the ability also still allows targeting of enemy and neutral creeps, with the added restriction that they must be NPC creeps.

^947+^* This fixes an exploit where Plague Rider was able to deny high value targets from allies like Booboo and Shudder. However, on the grander scheme of things, Plague Rider has no need to deny an ally player-controlled creep since it is griefing almost all the time if done.

^947+^* Although Plague Rider loses a little bit of utility against enemy player-controlled creeps later on (at level 4 of the ability), Plague Rider will still be a strong hero.

==^960 Bug Fixes & Optimizations ^*==

 

=^075 General ^*=

^947-^* The HoN Live button is now disabled for the International (NAEU/non-SEA) Client to combat committed griefers by reducing the amount of game disrupting exploits.
   ^274*^* Report any player that is suspected of continuing to perform these exploits. They will be permanently suspended if found guilty.

^947-^* Fixed the Prophets map/mode by skipping the hero selection phase so that the gameplay functions as intended.

^947-^* Predasaur Crusher's attack animation now accurately matches the attack's actual impact time.

 

=^075 Heroes ^*=

^059Arachna^*
^947-^* Spider Sting's projectile models are now correct for each of Arachna's alternate avatars.

^059Engineer^*
^947-^* No longer displays a Staff of the Master visual if he obtains that state in any way (since he does not have a Staff of the Master effect on any of his abilities).

^059Kinesis^*
^947-^* Thoughtsteal's visuals are now displayed properly after the first time this ability is used in a game.

^059Nymphora^*
^947-^* The Well Invulnerability state (when a hero respawns) for heroes around Nymphora will now be continuously dispelled while Teleport is being channeled.
   ^274*^* This allows Teleport to correctly select the number of ally heroes to teleport when Nymphora uses Teleport at her ally Well, without breaking any other invulnerability interactions.

^059Prisoner 945^*
^947-^* Jailbreak (Prison Break, boosted by Staff of the Master) now properly applies its effects when targeting an enemy unit.

^059Rampage^*
^947-^* Horned Strike now properly deals 33% of its damage when procced by Rampage's illusions.

^059Revenant^*
^947-^* Essence Shroud now properly plays its cast sounds if the ability is cast while Revenant is moving.

 

=^075 Items ^*=

^077Arcane Bomb^*
^947-^* Now properly has a shared cooldown with other Arcane Bomb items in a unit's inventory (if any).

^077Dreamcatcher^*
^947-^* Affected enemies can now be properly denied (with the current hero deny rules).

^077Hypercrown^*
^947-^* Moved from the Combative section to the Morph Attack section in the Item Shop.

^077Soultrap^*
^947-^* Affected enemies can now be properly denied (with the current hero deny rules).

^980Version 4.7.3^*
-------------
^98026 February 2019^*



==^960 Design ^*==

^947-^* Tributes to certain staff members have been put into some item flavour tooltips to thank them for all their hard work throughout the game!
   ^274*^* Go look for them! =)



=^075 General ^*=

Mystic Illusion Rune
^947-^* No longer stacks with Grimoire of Power's Cooldown Reduction.

Ancient Creeps
^947-^* Gold Bounty reduced by 20%.
   ^274*^* Dragon: 35-45 -> 28-36
   ^274*^* Dragon Master: 130-145 -> 100-120
   ^274*^* Dreadbeetle: 55-65 -> 44-52
   ^274*^* Dreadbeetle Queen: 90-105 -> 74-82
   ^274*^* Predasaur: 60-70 -> 48-56
   ^274*^* Predasaur Crusher: 70-85 -> 58-66

 

=^075 Forests of Caldavar ^*=

^947-^* Added 3 more extra buildings to each base (for a total of 7 extra base buildings).
   ^274*^* Slightly helps slow down being pushed by upgraded creeps.

^947-^* The entire upper-left area of the map has been filled with trees.
   ^274*^* Prevents the Legion team from having numerous approach options vs. Hellbourne top lane when ganking.
^947-^* Top lane and bottom lane's side path tree line has been adjusted such that lane wards have more visibility and flexibility.
^947-^* The tree space above Hellbourne's top lane Tier 1 tower has been adjusted so that its layout is more similar to the tree space below Legion's bottom lane Tier 1 tower.
   ^274*^* Each of the above points have been adjusted to make lane performance more symmetrical between both teams.

^947-^* Fixed a small pathing issue in a small tree patch to the bottom-right of Hellbourne's middle lane Tier 1 tower.
^947-^* The side paths near Legion's top lane Tier 2 tower and Hellbourne's bottom lane Tier 2 tower have been completely removed.
   ^274*^* There still exists a small side path near their respective Tier 1 towers to reward their opponents for destroying those Tier 1 towers and obtain a small edge in ganking near the respective Tier 2 towers. The level of ease to gank Tier 2 towers has been drastically reduced with the above change, however.

^947+^* Overall, these changes lead to less areas with mass juking.

 

=^075 Concede Mechanics ^*=

^947-^* Under regular game conditions, time threshold for requiring 4 votes to pass a Concede Vote has been reduced from 30 minutes to 20 minutes.
   ^274*^* End Result: 15 minutes for 5 votes required to pass a Concede Vote, 20 minutes for 4 votes required to pass a Concede Vote.

=^075 Hero Reworks ^*=

 

^059Bramble (Partially Reworked)^*

^075Ability 1: Spore Breath (Reworked)^*
   ^274*^* Mana Cost : 100
   ^274*^* Cooldown: 14 seconds

^947-^* Activate to spew out spores in front of you for 2.5 seconds, dealing up to 100/175/250/325 Magic Damage over 3 seconds. Damage effect lingers for 0.5 seconds.
^947-^* After 2.5 seconds, anyone within the cone will be Stunned for 1.75 seconds.

_______________________________________________

^075Ability 2: Ensnaring Shrubbery (Reworked)^*
   ^274*^* Mana Cost: 60
   ^274*^* Radius: 275
   ^274*^* Trail Radius: 200
   ^274*^* Cooldown: 15 seconds

^947-^* Target an Ally (or self) to enshroud them with shrubbery for 3/4/5/6 seconds. Enemies around the target receive a 30% Movement Speed Slow.
^947-^* While affected by Ensnaring Shrubbery, taking hero damage Heals the target and all allies in a 400 unit radius for 40% of the damage taken.
^947-^* Also spawns a trail that grants yourself and allies 40/50/60/70 Movement Speed and Unitwalking while they are on the trail. Each part of the trail remains on the ground for 6 seconds after spawning.
   ^274*^* The ability can be double-activated to cast on self.

^947+^* Bramble's previous iteration did not have a clear role. With the return of Ensnaring Shrubbery (while also having the ally speed boost effect of Enhanced Shrubbery) and Spore Breath, Bramble can now gank more frequently without his ultimate while serving as a durable frontline hero with the niche of mitigating Area of Effect damage through Ensnaring Shrubbery, all while maintaining reasonable damage with his current Chomp passive.
   ^274*^* Note that some numbers on Ensnaring Shrubbery were reduced due to how powerful this skill is after the rework.

^059Engineer (Reworked)^*

^947-^* Base Strength increased from 20 to 22.
^947-^* Base Agility increased from 18 to 20.
^947-^* Agility growth per level reduced from 3.0 to 2.3.
^947-^* Base Intelligence increased from 20 to 24.
^947-^* Base Armor increased from 1.5 to 4.2.

^075Ability 1: The Keg^*
^947-^* Magic Damage changed from 70/140/210/280 to 80/140/200/260.
^947-^* Stun Duration reduced from 2 seconds to 1.4/1.6/1.8/2 seconds.
   ^274*^* The Mana Cost and Stun Duration are still a net buff compared to the pre-4.5.0 iteration of this ability.

_______________________________________________

^075Ability 2: Steam Turret (Reworked)^*
   ^274*^* Range: 800
   ^274*^* Mana Cost: 75
   ^274*^* Cooldown: 15 seconds

^947-^* Deploys a Steam Turret at target position facing the vector direction. The Turret fires 10 shots per second in a 800 distance, 400 radius cone while the Turret is active. 
   ^274*^* The Turret lasts 5 seconds.
^947-^* Each bullet deals 10/20/30/40 Magic Damage, pushes 10/15/20/25 units away from the Turret, and applies a charge of Suppressed for 3 seconds. 
   ^274*^* Suppressed is capped at 10 charges and each charge has its own duration. 
^947-^* Each additional bullet (i.e. charge of Suppressed) causes the damage and push to be reduced by 10%.
^947-^* The Turret provides 1000 day and night vision and is destroyed in 3 hits.

_______________________________________________

^075Ability 3: Spider Mines (Reworked)^*
   ^274*^* Range: 450
   ^274*^* Detection & Explosion Radius: 250
   ^274*^* Stack Limitation Radius: 700
   ^274*^* Mana Cost: 90/100/110/120
   ^274*^* Cooldown: 5 seconds

^947-^* Target a position to place a Spider Mine there. 
   ^274*^* Cannot place a mine if 2/2/2/3 mines already exist within target (Stack Limitation) radius. 
^947-^* Up to 6 may be placed at once, each dealing 100/175/250/325 Magic Damage.
^947-^* Consume one charge to place a mine. Up to 1/2/3/4 charges may be stored, refreshing every 15 seconds.
^947-^* Spider mines are stealthed and appear when an enemy comes near them, chasing them until contact.
   ^274*^* Each mine grants vision around itself.
   ^274*^* Does not block neutrals from respawning.

_______________________________________________

^075Ability 4: Energy Field (Reworked)^*
   ^274*^* Radius: 475
   ^274*^* Mana Cost: 200
   ^274*^* Cooldown: 105/95/85 seconds

^947-^* Places a gadget at the Engineer's location that lasts 6/8/10 seconds.
^947-^* The gadget takes 4 hits to destroy, and creates an Energy Field in a 475 radius around itself.
^947-^* Enemies who enter or exit the field has their buffs dispelled, takes 100 True Damage, and receive a 2 second Silence & Perplex with a tapering 100% Movement Speed Slow.
^947-^* Enemies inside the Energy Field take 50/75/100 Magic Damage per second.

^947+^* Engineer's pre-4.5.0 kit was more versatile, enabled him to be one of the most viable support heroes, and gave him a good reason to be picked in a game. By reverting to this iteration, adding in some number adjustments, and adding a mechanic to Spider Mines that discourages stacking all of them into one general location (while still allowing for Spider Mine kills on enemy heroes that are injured enough), this iteration of Engineer aims to be the best one possible.

^947+^* The reduced power of Spider Mines at level 1 should discourage Engineer players from stacking them up early on as well, and should encourage them to be used as a scouting and harassing tool early game instead of a kill tool.

^947+^* As an additional note, the post-4.5.0's Steam Turret iteration was incredibly difficult to balance for a variety of situations. The pre-4.5.0 Engineer was much more stable for balance.

=============================================

^075Ability 4: Crystal Field (Reworked)^*
   ^274*^* Range: 1000
   ^274*^* Radius: 350
   ^274*^* Mana Cost: 200/250/300
   ^274*^* Cooldown: 100/90/80 seconds

^947-^* Creates a Crystal Field at target area, which is visible to all players. After 2.5 seconds, the field explodes and deals 300/450/600 Magic Damage to enemies in radius and Stuns for 2/2.5/3 seconds.
^947-^* Staff of the Master effect: Deals an additional 100/120/140 Magic Damage and applies a 50% Movement Speed Slow for 4 seconds in a 600 radius.

^947+^* Geomancer's post-4.2.0-patch Crystal Field was too inflexible and was too easily counterplayed.

^947+^* By reverting Crystal Field to its legacy iteration, Geomancer can take advantage of newly available tools (e.g. Sand Scepter, Stormspirit).
   ^274*^* This can be combined with his Geo Stalk ability to grant Geomancer much more potential and overall flexibility in his kit and performance.

   ^274*^* Bounce Radius: 650
   ^274*^* Mana Cost: 100
   ^274*^* Cooldown: 15 seconds

^947-^* Target an enemy unit to fire a bolt that deals 75% of your Attack Damage + 30/60/90/120 Physical Damage and applies a 60% tapering Movement Speed Slow for 4 seconds to all units around the initial target within radius.
^947-^* The Bolt will bounce up to 2/4/6/8 times to nearby enemies within the bounce radius, dealing 10/20/30/40 + (6 x Bounce #) in Physical Damage.
^947-^* If Luck of the Draw is leveled, the initial impact and bounces can apply Critical Strike from Luck of the Draw.

_______________________________________________

^075Ability 2: Far Scry (Reworked)^*
   ^274*^* Range: 1000
   ^274*^* Mana Cost: 30/35/40/45
   ^274*^* Cooldown: 25/23/21/19 seconds

^947-^* Target an enemy hero to Mark them for 8/10/12/14 seconds.
^947-^* When you attack, your Attack will bounce to the Marked Target if they are within a 800 unit radius of your main target.
   ^274*^* If attacking the Marked target, it will bounce to the closest nearby enemy hero within radius.
^947-^* A Marked target cannot dodge attacks, has -10/20/30/40% Armor, and becomes Sighted and Revealed.

_______________________________________________

^075Ability 3: Bound by Fate^*
^947-^* No longer Reveals the primary target.
^947-^* Killing one of the 2 Bound units will once again snap the Bind to apply the pull, Stun and Magic Damage to the other Bound unit.
^947-^* Killing the Bound unit to snap the Bind will only apply 50% of the Magic Damage and Stun Duration to the other Bound target if the killed unit is a non-hero unit.
^947-^* Stun duration reduced from 1.6/1.9/2.2/2.5 seconds to 1.25/1.5/1.75/2 seconds.

_______________________________________________

^075Ability 4: Luck of the Draw^*
^947-^* Critical Damage Multiplier reduced from 2/3.25/4.5x to 2/2.75/3.5x.

^947+^* Tarot gains a lot of power and utility by doing this partial revert to her old iteration.
^947+^* Far Scry is now back to its vanilla iteration with some tweaks that actually doesn't let it lose out on an extra attack on another target if Tarots attack her main target (instead of forcing her to attack a different target to maximize its use).
   ^274*^* This makes the ability actually intuitive compared to its vanilla iteration.

^947+^* Tarot also gains some of her utility in Bound by Fate for laning power, except the effectiveness of the Stun is drastically reduced if she kills a creep to forcibly snap the Bind. This is a fair reward for the skill requirement to pull off - it was too ridiculous at 100% Stun Duration in the past.
^947+^* Some of Tarot's power elsewhere in her kit has been reduced in power in exchange for better overall reliability, utility and damage scaling.

^059Drunken Master^*

^256Stagger^*
^947-^* Area Physical Damage increased from 40/60/80/100 to 45/70/95/120.
^947-^* Area Physical Damage dealt is now 1.75x against non-hero units.
^947-^* Now also grants 5/10/15/20 Movement Speed for 4 seconds.
   ^274*^* This bonus does not stack with Magic Brew's Movement Speed bonus. Whichever one grants more Movement Speed will take priority.
^947-^* Attack Damage bonus changed from 40/60/80/100 to 35/50/65/80.
^947-^* Cooldown reduced from 8 seconds to 7 seconds.

^947+^* Drunken Master's older skill iterations cannot be reverted because they caused a huge amount of frustration for the opponents. Drunken Master is already a hero that is versatile in laning and performance throughout the entire game and has a lot of utility in his kit, so his kit cannot be overloaded any further without a compromise.

^947+^* That being said, Drunken Master's Stagger now brings back some of his out-of-hero-combat mobility and improved damage against creeps. These general adjustments facilitate his creep farming rotations, but not to the extent of breaking his kit.

_______________________________________________

^059Electrician^*

^256Electric Shield^*
^947-^* Shield Health value 0.75/0.95/1.15/1.35x to 0.75/0.9/1.05/1.2x of the Mana Spent to use this ability.

_______________________________________________

^059Emerald Warden^*

^256Hunter's Command^*
^947-^* Number of Wolves spawned increased from 1 to 2.
^947-^* Movement Speed Slow increased from 10% to 20%.
^947-^* Movement Speed Slow, Sighted and Reveal duration reduced from 5 seconds to 4 seconds.
^947-^* Cooldown increased from 12 seconds to 15 seconds.
^947-^* Mana Cost increased from 80 to 80/90/100/110.
^947-^* Movement Speed for Wolves increased from 550 to 600.

^256Overgrowth^*
^947-^* The delay to activate the self-destruct ability (default hotkey "D") on the overgrowth after it spawns has been reduced from 1.5 seconds to 0.4 seconds.
^947-^* Cast Range reduced from 900 to 600.
^947-^* Magic Damage per second reduced from 125 to 90.
   ^274*^* Now deals 1.5x Magic Damage per second to non-hero units.
^947-^* Cooldown increased from 5 seconds to 9 seconds.
^947-^* No longer blocks neutral camps from spawning.

^947+^* Emerald Warden now has his old 2 wolves on Hunter's Command back, as well as having the manual Overgrowth proc mechanic return to a realistically usable on-demand immobilize ability. There is still a small delay when activating Overgrowth manually to allow for a small amount of counterplay.

^947+^* Note: Emerald Warden is still in a net buff state when compared to Patch 4.4.0 (where the wolf count on Hunter's Command was reduced from 2 to 1) and Patch 4.4.3 (before the overgrowth kill subability had a large delay before it can be activated after spawning an overgrowth).

=^075 New Items ^*=

^077New Initiation Item: Sacrificial Stone^*
^947-^* Component Buildup: Power Supply (300) + Pretender's Crown (165) + Recipe (435) = 900 Gold Total
^947-^* Grants: 6 Strength, 6 Agility, 6 Intelligence, +10% Healing Done.
^947-^* A charge is gained every time an enemy within 1200 radius casts a spell. 
^947-^* Active: When activated, all charges are removed.
   ^274*^* Restores 10 Health and 15 Mana per charge consumed.
   ^274*^* Max of 15 charges.
   ^274*^* Cooldown: 17 seconds

^947-^* On death, heals allies within 1200 radius for 50 Health.
^947-^* Sacrificial Stone glows after getting a hero kill or assist.
^947-^* While glowing, Sacrificial Stone grants +2 Strength, +2 Agility and +2 Intelligence, and reduces death timer by 8 seconds upon death.
   ^274*^* The glow dissipates after death.
   ^274*^* Death timer reduction does not occur in Mid Wars.

^947+^* Sacrificial Stone returns in the form of the legacy Grave Locket with a few small extra touches, such as the increasing amount of Healing Done (from the second iteration of Jade Spire), as well as an area heal on death (tribute to the legacy Sacrificial Stone).

^947+^* Its general purpose is to be a cost-and-slot-efficient early game stat item. It is slightly more expensive than legacy Grave Locket due to the improved baseline stats on it, having Power Supply's active, as well as having a few bonus perks on top of that.

_______________________________________________

^077New Weapons Item: Toxin Claws^*
^947-^* Cost: 300 Gold
^947-^* Passively applies Poison to enemies you attack for 3 seconds, applying a 12% Movement Speed Slow and dealing 6 Magic Damage per second.
^947-^* Poison only applies a 4% Movement Speed Slow and deals 3 Magic Damage per second when wielded by a ranged hero.
^947-^* This effect propagates to illusions for 33% of the duration.
^947-^* This item has a Freeze modifier, preventing its Movement Speed Slow state from stacking with the Icebrand line of items.

^947+^* Toxin Claws is a cheap early game item to be picked up by melee heroes who could use the extra Movement Speed Slow and Magic DoT effects to either harass or help kill their foes.

   ^274*^* Total Cost increased from 4700 Gold to 5000 Gold.

_______________________________________________

^077Icon of the Goddess^*
^947-^* Base Health Regeneration per second increased from 2 to 3.
^947-^* Base Mana Regeneration bonus increased from 20% to 30%.

_______________________________________________

^077Ioyn Stone^*
^947-^* Can no longer be obtained in Mid Wars.

_______________________________________________

^077Orb of Zamos^*
^947-^* Bonus Attack Damage while synchronized reduced from 5 to 4.
^947-^* Bonus Movement Speed while synchronized reduced from 10 to 5.
^947-^* Bonus Gold obtained from killing an enemy unit and granted to your synchronized partner reduced from 10 to 8.

^947+^* Orb of Zamos has been improving the performance of supports, although it has been doing so a little too much such that supports are generally the highest statistical performers.

^947+^* As a result, Orb of Zamos is receiving some minor adjustments for the laning phase. This also allows for a better reason for heroes to upgrade to Grave Locket earlier if they wish to maximize the rewards of the synchronization mechanic throughout the game.

_______________________________________________

^077Plated Greaves^*
^947-^* On the active state: Base Damage bonus granted to ally creeps reduced from 20% to 10%.
^947-^* On the active state: Deflection granted to ally creeps reduced from 10 to 8.

_______________________________________________

^077Runed Cleaver^*

^947-^* Recipe Cost increased from 600 Gold to 900 Gold.
   ^274*^* Total Cost increased from 4100 Gold to 4400 Gold.
^947-^* The bonus damage values against creeps have been changed from +60%/20% of your Attack Damage to +60/20 damage if the wielder is melee/ranged, respectively.
   ^274*^* Addresses a numerical inconsistency when upgrading Logger's Hatchet to Runed Cleaver.
   ^274*^* Note that this bonus damage value still does not apply to the Cleave damage. The only number that gets changed is the damage dealt to the primary target.

_______________________________________________

^077Spiked Bola^*
^947-^* Cooldown increased from 16 seconds to 22 seconds.
^947-^* Cannot be used on a target that has been previously affected by Spiked Bola within the last 10 seconds.

==^960 Matchmaking Maps & Modes ^*==

 

^075Champions of Newerth (CoN)^*

==^960 Bug Fixes & Optimizations ^*==



=^075 Forests of Caldavar ^*=

 - Units can no longer traverse through the cliff area to the east of the Top Lane Legion Tier 1 tower without cliffwalking or cliffwalking-like properties.

 

=^075 General ^*=

^947-^* The Ladder button has been disabled due to players abusing an exploit.
   ^274*^* Results on the ladder are meaningless since a few years ago anyway, so this change does not negatively affect players.

^947-^* SEA Client only: MMR values visibility has been removed.
   ^274*^* The NAEU/International Client will still have MMR value visibility.

^947-^* Players should be able to 4-man queue again with the Old UI.
   ^274*^* Disclaimer: this is one of the only bugfixes that will ever be made regarding the Old UI.

^947-^* Fixed a bug with charging-related abilities from Deadlift, Legionnaire, Nomad and Solstice where using items that can target another entity and insert their action at the front of your action queue (instead of the back of your action queue) will change the target of the charge ability to the entity targeted by the item usage, when done after the charging-related ability has activated but before the hero has reached their initial target.
   ^274*^* As a result, these heroes are Perplexed while these skills are active.
   ^274*^* To players exploiting these bugs: please stop exploiting these bugs, as doing so has the potential to gimp certain heroes/items/mechanics every time these are fixed. Some of them could have unintended consequences like nerfing your favourite heroes.

^947-^* Power Rune now properly applies the Mystic state to the hero that picked it up.

 

=^075 Heroes ^*=

The following Heroes are now properly enabled in Tournament Rules:
^947-^* Artillery
^947-^* Deadlift
^947-^* Engineer
^947-^* Fayde
^947-^* Goldenveil
^947-^* Prophet

 

^059Arachna^*
^947-^* Broodlings (Spider Sting, when boosted by Staff of the Master) now have the models properly corresponding to its alternate avatar for all of the additional Spiderlings spawned.

^059Kinesis^*
^947-^* Hellbringer's Demon Strike now functions correctly when the ability is copied via Thoughtsteal.
^947-^* If War Beast's Summon Hellhounds ability is copied using Thoughtsteal, the Hellhounds now properly die if Kinesis dies or chooses to forget the Summon Hellhounds ability.

^059Master of Arms^*
^947-^* Overcharged Shot now properly displays the overhead stun swirl visuals on the primary target.
^947-^* Illusions now spawn with Master of Arms' current mode of Weapon Enhancement.

^059Monkey King^*
^947-^* Vault now functions properly when cast on a building at max range when Jade Spire is in your inventory.

^059Revenant^*
^947-^* Mortification visuals now properly correspond to Revenant's alt avatar when affecting enemy units.

^059Rhapsody^*
^947-^* Death Metal Rhapsody no longer plays a sound when knocked down.
   ^274*^* This primarily addresses self-Taunt spamming.

^059Valkyrie^*
^947-^* Azrael Valkyrie's Javelin of Light now properly plays an impact sound when it hits an enemy at far distances.

 

=^075 Items ^*=

The following Items are now properly enabled in Tournament Rules:
^947-^* Flying Courier
^947-^* Orb of Zamos

^077Hypercrown^*
^947-^* Illusions now properly proc the Chain Lightning effect.

^077Logger's Hatchet^*
^947-^* Damage bonus no longer applies on bounces (e.g. Moon Queen's Multi-Strike).

^077Restoration Stone^*
^947-^* No longer resets its own cooldown to 0 seconds under certain conditions while Mystic Illusion Rune's Mystic state was active.
   ^274*^* Also fixed with all variants/combinations with Grimoire of Power's cooldown reduction as well.

^077Runed Cleaver^*
^947-^* Damage bonus no longer applies on bounces (e.g. Moon Queen's Multi-Strike).

^077Spellshards^*
^947-^* Now properly applies its bonus Spell Damage to creeps.



_______________________________________________



^980Version 4.7.2^*
-------------
^98029 January 2019^*

 

 

==^960 Design ^*==

 

=^075 (Old) User Interface ^*=

^947-^* Players now have the option to switch back to the old User Interface by going to Options -> Interface -> Miscellaneous -> New Main Interface and unticking the checkbox (requires client restart to take effect).
^947-^* Players now have the option to switch back to the old Shop Interface by going to Options -> Interface -> Miscellaneous -> New Shop Interface and unticking the checkbox (requires client restart to take effect).

   ^274*^* Since these are options for the old interfaces, bugs and the removal of certain features may occur as a result of using these options.
   ^274*^* Always do a client restart if you change these settings, as it could make your user interface unusable if done in a real game.
   ^274*^* Users who use these options use them at their own risk. Bug reports regarding these features will most likely be rejected, as these features are not officially supported.

 

=^075 Matchmaking Rating (MMR) ^*=

^947-^* MMR will now once again be displayed for each player on the hero pick screen (for both the Old and New User Interface).
   ^274*^* Rank icons and titles will still be displayed beside the MMR value.
   ^274*^* Note that the MMR system never actually went away, and only its mechanics were adjusted throughout the last few years after Champions of Newerth (CoN) started.
   ^274*^* Note that the MMR system is practically back to its legacy mechanics, and has been this way for 2 CoN Seasons.

 

=^075 Concede Vote Mechanics ^*=

^947-^* Now requires 5 players to concede after the 15 minute mark (instead of 4 players).
^947-^* Now requires 4 players to concede after the 30 minute mark (instead of 3 players).
   ^274*^* The above points assume an ideal 5v5 scenario in the Forests of Caldavar map where no other player has been terminated. The proper behaviour will take place if at least 1 player is terminated on the team that calls the concede vote.

 

=^075 Forests of Caldavar ^*=

^947-^* Two trees blocking a continuous side path from the bottom lane Tier 1 Legion Tower to the bottom lane Hellbourne Tier 2 Tower has been removed.
   ^274*^* Maintains consistency when comparing the side path from the top lane Tier 1 Hellbourne Tower to the top lane Tier 2 Legion Tower.

^947-^* The cliff ward spot closest to top lane Legion Tier 1 tower has been removed & replaced with trees.
   ^274*^* This addresses some of the ward vision inconsistencies between Legion top lane offlaners and Hellbourne bottom lane offlaners.



=^075 Runes ^*=

^077New Rune: Mystic Illusion Rune^*
^947-^* Replaces Illusion Rune.
^947-^* Illusion Rune has been renamed to Mystic Illusion Rune.
^947-^* In addition to the Illusion rune's current effects, now also applies Mystic for 45 seconds.
   ^274*^* Mystic grants 30% Reduced Cooldown and 30% Reduced Mana Costs.

 

=^075 Mid Wars ^*=

^947-^* Zorgath & Transmutanstein's respawn times reduced from 6 minutes to 4 minutes.
^947-^* Hero respawn time reduced from (0.75 * Hero Level) to (0.7 * Hero Level).

 

=^075 Hero Reworks ^*=

^059Prophet^*
^947-^* Reverted to its pre-4.5.0 version:

   ^274*^* Strength: 20 + 1.8 per level
   ^274*^* Agility: 20 + 2 per level
   ^274*^* Intelligence: 27 + 2.5 per level (Primary)

   ^274*^* Movement Speed: 300
   ^274*^* Attack Range: 600
   ^274*^* Starting Attack Damage: 49-57
   ^274*^* Starting Armor: 3.92

   ^274*^* Mana Cost: 90/100/110/120
   ^274*^* Cooldown: 12 seconds

   ^274*^* Cooldown: 120/105/90 seconds

^947-^* Activate to apply Shared Fate to all units within radius around you for 4/6/8 Seconds. Enemies affected by Shared Fate receive a 40% Movement Speed Slow that lasts for 1/1.5/2 seconds and then tapers off slowly.
^947-^* Any of the Prophet's spells that land on a target also apply to all other valid nearby hero targets with Shared Fate. Prophet's attacks also propagate and deal 50% damage to all nearby enemy heroes with Shared Fate on them.

^947-^* +60 Max Health

^947-^* When in your inventory, right-click a Rune (or use this item targeting a Rune) to power the Stone with 3 charges based on the Rune. Does not consume the Rune
^947-^* The charges last up to 4 minutes
^947-^* When powered, target yourself or an ally to consume a charge and give them a short duration buff based on the Rune that is stored in the Stone.
^947-^* Does not stack with other Ioyn Stones or Bottles.

_______________________________________________

^077Plated Greaves (Reworked)^*
^947-^* Reverted to its version before Patch 4.5.4, and added a Guardian Ring to its recipe:

^947-^* Recipe: Marchers (500) + Shield of the Five (800) + Guardian Ring (175) = 1475 Gold Total
^947-^* Grants: 3 Strength, 3 Agility, 3 Intelligence, 65 Movement Speed, 7 Armor.

Active:
^947-^* Activate to grant +3 Armor to allied heroes/structures and applies Plated to allied non-hero units in radius for 60 seconds. Armor bonus on heroes/structures and damage bonus on non-heroes are doubled for the first 10 seconds.
^947-^* Plated grants +20% Base Damage and 10 Deflection to creeps.
   ^274*^* Does not stack with Shield of the Five.
   ^274*^* Radius: 600
   ^274*^* Cooldown: 30 seconds

^980Version 4.7.0^*
-------------
^98018 December 2018^*

 

==^960 Design ^*==

 

=^075 Support Heroes ^*=

^947+^* Hard Support heroes have had their power recalibrated to be more dominant between levels 1-4, but be slightly weaker (in terms of nuke damage) level 5 and onward (to prevent them from being outright better than gankers with their early advantage). This change further separates Support heroes from the rest of the hero pool and allows Support heroes to truly dominate the laning phase.

^947+^* Some support heroes remained untouched due to their hybrid role nature (e.g. Witch Slayer being much more oriented as a support-ganker that is quite dependent on levels & scaling), while other heroes received changes that are outside these guidelines due to other factors (e.g. buildup of damage over time being outright buffed because it simply is not as reliable as burst damage, or being a special case that scales somewhat unorthodoxically like Martyr).

 

^059Empath^*

^256Essence Link^*
^947-^* Now also deals 10 True Damage to the target and heals Empath for 10 Health upon the initial cast.

_______________________________________________

^059Glacius^*

^256Tundra Blast^*
^947-^* Magic Damage changed from 80/130/180/230 to 110/150/180/210.
^947-^* Movement Speed Slow increased from 30% to 30/35/40/45%.

^256Chilling Presence^*
^947-^* Now also grants 1.5 Mana Regeneration to self.

_______________________________________________

^059Ichor^*

^256Life Leech^*
^947-^* Magic Damage changed from 80/120/160/200 to 100/140/170/200.

^256Transfusion^*
^947-^* Magic Damage changed from 70/140/210/280 to 100/160/200/240.

_______________________________________________

^059Martyr^*

^256Retribution^*
^947-^* Base Magic Damage increased from 75/100/125/150 to 100/125/150/175.
^947-^* Max Magic Damage increased from 175/300/425/550 to 210/330/440/550.
^947-^* Percentage threshold to reach Max Magic Damage & Movement Speed Slow application is now also increased by 0.3% for every 1 point of Intelligence Martyr has.
   ^274*^* e.g. if Martyr has 100 Intelligence, then the Max Health threshold to reach Max Magic Damage & Movement Speed Slow application is 30% of Martyr's Max Health.

^256Sol's Conviction^*
^947-^* Cooldown reduced from 45 seconds to 45/35/25/15 seconds.

_______________________________________________

^256Mana Sunder^*
^947-^* Magic Damage multiplier changed from 1/2/3/4x to 2/2.5/3/3.5x.

^256Arcane Vortex^*
^947-^* Magic Damage increased from 125/250/375 to 200/300/400.
   ^274*^* The same changes are applied for the Heal values when Staff of the Master's effects are active on Moira.

_______________________________________________

^256Crippling Pollen^*
^947-^* Total Magic Damage changed from 90/140/200/270 to 110/160/195/230.

^947-^* Healing per second changed from 15/35/55/75 to 20/35/45/55.

   ^274*^* Max potential Magic Damage & Healing increased from 150/210/270/330 to 170/230/290/350.

^256Nymphora's Zeal^*
^947-^* Magic Damage per impact changed from 50/90/130/170 to 55/90/115/140.
^947-^* Stun duration increased from 1 second to 1.2/1.3/1.4/1.5 seconds.

^256Grace of the Nymph^*
^947-^* Total potential Mana restored changed from 75/150/225/300 to 100/170/235/300.

_______________________________________________

^059Riftwalker^*

^256Cascade Event^*
^947-^* Magic Damage changed from 80/140/200/260 to 110/160/200/240.
^947-^* Cooldown reduced from 24/21/18/15 seconds to 20/18/16/14 seconds.

^256Shared Existence^*
^947-^* Initial Movement Speed Slow increased from 20/25/30/35% to 30/35/40/45%.
^947-^* Movement Speed Slow per charge changed from 4/5/6/7% to 4/6/8/10%.
   ^274*^* Max Movement Speed Slow from this skill increased from 40/50/60/70% to 50/65/80/95%.

^256Rift Burn^*
^947-^* Initial impact of Magic Damage changed from 50/100/150/200 to 80/140/170/200.
^947-^* Magic Damage over time from pulses changed from 100/200/300/400 to 150/200/250/300 Magic Damage.

_______________________________________________



 

=^075 Heroes ^*=

 

^059Chipper^*
^947-^* Reverted to his iteration before 4.6.0.
^947-^* The following changes are now applied to Chipper:

^256Rocket Barrage^*
^947-^* Rocket speed increased from 1000 to 1200.
^947-^* Rocket touch radius increased from 70 to 75.
^947-^* Rockets can now hit neutral creeps.
^947-^* Rockets now deal an additional 7% of the target's Max Health in Magic Damage if they travel 2000 units or further.

^947+^* Although Chipper's initial rework was incredibly successful, it caused a large amount of frustration due to his long range poke being too potent. Reverting the Rocket Barrage damage numbers back to how they were before and bringing back the (forced) synergy between Rocket Barrage and Tar Toss seems to be what players want so that Chipper can deal reliable damage with Portal Key once again.

^947+^* To allow Chipper to have an additional option of having a long-range poke while still not allowing the long-range poke to be too strong nor too weak (and certainly not too frustrating), the Rockets deal bonus damage if they travel a long distance.

 _______________________________________________

^059Demented Shaman^*

^256Entangle^*
^947-^* Physical Damage reduced from 80/120/160/200 to 65/100/135/170 over 8 seconds.

^256Unbreakable^*
^947-^* Cast Range reduced from 550/700/850/1000 to 650.
^947-^* Cooldown changed from 60/45/30/15 seconds to 50/40/30/20 seconds.
^947-^* Cooldown if the state is dispelled while in Passive mode changed from 15 seconds to 20 seconds.
^947-^* State is now transferrable via Parasite's Facehug once again.
^947-^* When receiving fatal damage, now sets the target's Health to 120/180/240/300 + 6/9/12/15% of the target's Max Health (instead of preventing their Health to be reduced below 1 for 5 seconds).

^947+^* Entangle was doing a little too much damage with the addition of the Shaman Minions, so Entangle's damage over time was reduced slightly to account for this.

^947+^* Demented Shaman's Unbreakable in 4.6.3 overlapped with Martyr's Guardian Angel, so the skill was simply reverted to its previous iteration while still retaining its effects as a non-ultimate skill.

^947+^* Unbreakable also slightly scales now to help compensate its original problem of being useless late-game if leveled early.

 _______________________________________________

^059Master of Arms^*

^256Weapon Enhancement^*
^947-^* Attack Damage penalty against heroes with Cheetah reduced from 24/21/18/15% to 22/18/14/10%.

^256Master's Call^*
^947-^* Attack Speed bonus increased from 100/160/220 to 100/200/300.

^947+^* Master of Arms' power late-game wasn't enough to justify picking him. With these changes, he becomes noticeably more powerful than pre-4.6.2, but without the perks of obtaining massive amounts of Attack Speed by level 7.

_______________________________________________

 



=^075 New Items ^*=

 

^077New Item: Tome of Elements (200 Gold)^*
   ^274*^* Purchasable after the 10 minute mark.
   ^274*^* Initial Stock: 0
   ^274*^* Stock Refresh Time: 10 minutes.
^947-^* Activate to grant you 300 Experience, plus 60% of the difference between your Experience and your team's average Experience if your Experience is the lower value.

^947+^* Tome of Elements is intended to be picked up by underleveled heroes on your team (typically Support heroes) to quickly catch up to the game's tempo, rather than becoming underleveled throughout the entire game.

^947+^* Tome of Elements has little to no benefits for carry or ganker class heroes if they are close to/above their team's average Experience, as the item adds bonus Experience based on the difference between your Experience and your team's average Experience if your Experience is the lower value.

^947+^* This item (and its blessings) has been brought to you by ElementUser himself! <3

_______________________________________________

 

=^075 Items ^*=

 

^077Bottle^*
^947-^* Cost increased from 600 Gold to 650 Gold.

^947+^* This change prevents players from buying it directly at the start of the game.
   ^274*^* Also mitigates the frequency of pooling a Bottle to another ally hero from the start of the game.

 

^077Null Stone^*
^947-^* New Active: Transfers your spell negation ability to another ally hero for up to 15 seconds.

^947+^* Heroes who pick up this item now have the option to transfer the Spell Negation effect to another ally hero!

 _______________________________________________

^077Homecoming Stone^*
^947-^* Cooldown increased from 65 seconds to 80 seconds.

^947+^* Homecoming Stone was being used as a cheap mobility tool to quickly teleport to other lanes for farming rotations, and Post Haste's niche fell out of favour after the introduction of the 2x consumables slot. Adjusting the cooldown of Homecoming Stone resolves both of these respective issues.

=^075 Christmas ^*=

 

^077New Christmas Forsaken Archer Avatar: Santa's Little Archer^*
^947-^* It's Christmas in Newerth, and good old Saint Nick needs all the help he can get spreading holiday cheer across this war-torn land. Santa's Little Archer is overjoyed to bring her bow and snowman friends to the task, efficiently sending volleys of decorations and presents into villages and homes to make sure all of Newerth's citizens can enjoy the holiday spirit. The children know that when Santa's Little Archer dances into town, it's the one time of the year when catching an arrow is a good thing!

 

=^075 General ^*=

 

^077New Flint Beastwood Avatar: Pill Popper Flint^*
^947-^* Pill Popper Flint is the ultimate pharmaceutical rep, traveling among medical tents and Merrick's shops selling the latest health and mana potions to support the research and development of new healthcare products -- as well as the fuel bill for the shareholders' yachts -- and she knows exactly what you need to ease the pain: a nice shot of cutting-edge, explosively beneficial medication that not only quells any current flare-ups, it will also seep into your bloodstream and continue to wreak havoc upon all that ails you, until you feel nothing at all.

 

^077New Pebbles Avatar: Diamante^*
^947-^* Diamante is an ancient, elemental being forged by the same heat and pressure that created the pure evil of Magmus. While Magmus was unfortunately drawn to the destructive power of corruption and the Hellbourne, Diamante's pure, precious gemstones and earth-drawn powers naturally gravitated toward the side of light and creation. After witnessing just how much damage his brother in magma is capable of on the battlefield, Diamante knows the first step in securing the future of Newerth is to return Magmus to a molten state -- permanently.
